enemies.name: G-Soldier
enemies.notes: Fixed soldier waves during the town advance. Low-level draws are Fire, Thunder, and Blizzard; higher spell tiers only appear if the party is heavily overleveled.

enemies.name: Elite Soldier
enemies.notes: Random communication-road enemy. Low-level draws are Fire, Thunder, Blizzard, and Scan.

enemies.name: Geezard
enemies.notes: Random communication-road enemy. Low-level draws are Thunder and Cure; Healing Water can drop, but Mug is unavailable during the exam.

enemies.name: Anacondaur
enemies.notes: Fixed ambush after the bridge. Low-level draws are Fire and Cure; Bio is only available from Lv 30+ Anacondaur, so do not expect it during a normal exam route. Weak to Ice; avoid letting Seifer take the final blow for a perfect record.

chapters.title: After the Exam
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:point|After-exam freedom}} After the Dollet return scenes, Quistis dismisses the party in Balamb Town and the exam is over. Conversation penalties no longer apply, so it is safe to talk to people again. The exact scene flow depends on whether X-ATM092 was destroyed and whether Squall hid in the pub during the escape.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Timber Maniacs - Balamb}} Collect one Balamb **Timber Maniacs** issue now. One copy is in the Hotel upstairs room after staying the night; another is at the train station. Only one can be collected, and the choice affects a later scene but gives no mechanical advantage, so pick whichever route is convenient.

{{CALLOUT:card|Zell Card}} Visit the Dincht house near Balamb Town's central intersection and challenge Ma Dincht to cards for the **Zell Card**. Zell must be in the active party for his mother to use the card. If she will not play immediately, leave the room and re-enter. Once won, return to Balamb Garden by following the road to avoid random encounters.

{{CALLOUT:hint|Do not grind the beach yet}} It is tempting to farm AP on the beach for Siren, but a better opportunity is coming very soon. Return to Garden and keep the story moving.

In Balamb Garden's main hall, approach Cid, Quistis, and Xu for the post-exam scene. Go to the right hallway and speak to Seifer, then return to the main hall directory to receive the 2F Hallway instruction. On the 2F Hallway, talk to Raijin, Fujin, Zell, and the unnamed boy on the right side; the other unnamed students can be ignored. Try to leave, let the scene play, then speak to Cid again for the **Battle Meter**. Take the elevator down and head to the classroom to receive the SeeD score and salary explanation.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|SeeD score result}} The score screen breaks the exam into Conduct, Judgment, Attack, Spirit, Deduction, and Bonus categories. Detailed scoring tables and salary ranks live in the **SeeD Rank & Salary** reference page; this walkthrough only calls out the actions that matter while playing.

Back in the dormitory, change into the SeeD uniform, then speak to Selphie outside to begin the graduation party. When Selphie asks, answer **Yeah, I guess so.** After the balcony scenes, Squall returns to the dormitory. Change back into normal clothes and head to the Training Center with Quistis.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Training Center junction setup}} If Siren was drawn from Elvoret, Quistis gives the Status Junction tutorial here. Put Quezacotl and Ifrit on Squall, and Shiva and Siren on Quistis. Use Switch/Junction Exchange to move Zell's magic onto Quistis quickly. Junction Ice magic to Elem-Atk before entering the Training Center.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

Grat is the immediate source for status magic because ST Mag-RF is not available yet. Draw Sleep, Silence, Berserk, and Confuse stocks from Grat, then junction Sleep to ST-Atk. If T-Rexaur appears early, run unless you are prepared; Quistis can one-shot it with Degenerator if her Limit Break is available, but normal attacks are risky and physical hits wake a sleeping T-Rexaur.

At the first Training Center fork, either direction is fine. Follow the loop until Quistis says you are there, enter the secret area for the scene, then leave by either route. A distress scene triggers the boss battle near the exit.

**Boss: Granaldo** (Lv 1-100) - HP: 1,314-9,700 | AP: 5 | EXP: 40+10x
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Sleep, Blind, Shell | Lv 20-29: Sleep, Blind, Shell | Lv 30+: Sleep, Confuse, Shell, Pain
- Mug: 8x Wizard Stone (50%) - command unavailable before Diablos teaches Mug
- Drop: 4x Wizard Stone (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- LV Up/Down: Yes
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Wind
- Resist: Flying
- Status weak: Doom (40%), Drain, Eject, KO (10%), Poison (40%), Sleep (40%), Slow (90%), Stop (10%), The End

**Boss: Raldo** (Lv 1-100) - HP: 111-6,700 | AP: 3 | EXP: 40+10x
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Fire, Thunder, Protect | Lv 20-29: Fira, Thundara, Protect | Lv 30+: Firaga, Thundaga, Protect
- Mug: 8x Wizard Stone (50%) - command unavailable before Diablos teaches Mug
- Drop: 4x Wizard Stone (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- LV Up/Down: Yes
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: None
- Resist: None
- Status weak: Darkness (40%), Doom (40%), Drain, Eject, KO (20%), Poison (40%), Sleep (40%), Slow (90%), Stop (10%), The End

Strategy: Three Raldos accompany Granaldo. Draw **Protect** from Raldo and **Shell** from Granaldo if you need defensive stock, then defeat the Raldos first to remove Granaldo's strongest throw attacks. Granaldo is vulnerable to Sleep, Slow, Poison, and Gravity; Sleep plus magic or Limit Breaks keeps the fight controlled. Do not plan around Mug here, because the command is not available until after Diablos learns it.

After the battle, return to the dormitory. The next morning, Squall has a private room; collect the **Weapons Monthly April Issue** from the desk before leaving.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Cid before Timber}} Head toward the Front Gate, but before leaving Garden, go to the 3F Headmaster's Office and speak to **Cid**. Collect the **Magical Lamp** and, if missed earlier, the **Battle Meter**. After the Timber Mission begins, both are permanently gone. Challenge Cid now for the **Seifer Card**, one of the best early rare cards and an important Queen of Cards route piece.

{{CALLOUT:warning|Magical Lamp failure condition}} Cid is only available on the 3F at this exact window. If Squall walks further inside Garden instead of visiting Cid first, Cid disappears and the Magical Lamp/Battle Meter opportunity is lost. Save before using the Lamp, rest at the dormitory if needed, and junction carefully.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Magical Lamp setup}} If fighting Diablos immediately, use a balanced setup: Ifrit on Squall, Quezacotl and Shiva on Zell, and Siren on Selphie. Junction Blind to Selphie's ST-Atk if you stocked it from the Dollet tower or Granaldo. Rest, save, then use the Magical Lamp from the item menu.

**Boss: Diablos** (Lv 1-100) - HP: 1,600-80,800 | AP: 20 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Cure, Demi | Lv 20-29: Cura, Demi | Lv 30+: Curaga, Demi, Holy, Flare
- Mug: Nothing (0%)
- Drop: Lv 1-29: 8x G-Returner | Lv 30+: 4x Hero
- Card drop: Diablos (100%)
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Wind x1.5
- Resist: None
- Status weak: Darkness (50%), Drain, Slow (90%), The End

Strategy: Open by inflicting Blind; it nearly removes Diablos' physical threat. Demi and Gravija are percentage-based and cannot kill by themselves, so they set up low-HP Limit Breaks without ending the run. Draw Demi, and at Lv 30+ stock Holy and Flare because they are excellent junction spells. Diablos is weak to Wind and vulnerable to Slow. Finish with Squall, Zell, and Selphie Limit Breaks once HP is low.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Diablos ability order}} After the fight, name the GF and learn Diablos abilities in this order: **ST Mag-RF -> Time Mag-RF -> Enc-Half -> Enc-None -> Mug -> junction abilities -> everything else**. Enc-Half reduces random encounter frequency by exactly 50%; Enc-None is invaluable when drawing, carding, or Mug farming is complete. Mug unlocks steal data shown throughout later enemy cards.

checkpoints.label: Diablos
checkpoints.description: Unlock Guardian Force Diablos. Use the Magical Lamp (received from Cid after graduation) to trigger the Diablos battle. Save beforehand; cast Demi twice to reduce its HP, then defeat it.

checkpoints.label: First Salary
checkpoints.description: Receive the first SeeD salary. Awarded automatically after the Training Center incident and the return to the dormitory.

checkpoints.label: Battle Meter, Magical Lamp
checkpoints.description: Obtain both items from Cid before boarding the train to Timber. (Diablos can be obtained at any point in the game so long as you have the Magical Lamp.)

enemies.name: Grat
enemies.notes: Draw status magic here before ST Mag-RF is available. Sleep is the key ST-Atk junction for safer T-Rexaur handling.

enemies.name: T-Rexaur
enemies.notes: Training Center T-Rexaur is capped low, but it is still dangerous. Sleep works, but physical hits wake it; run or use Quistis Degenerator if available.

enemies.name: Bite Bug
enemies.notes: Appears in the Garden grounds around this story window; low threat and useful mainly as a light AP/source encounter.

enemies.name: Caterchipillar
enemies.notes: Garden-area encounter listed for the post-exam window; watch for status nuisance rather than damage.

enemies.name: Fastitocalon-F
enemies.notes: Beach encounter near Balamb. Better AP grinding opportunities are coming, so do not stall the post-exam route here.

enemies.name: Glacial Eye
enemies.notes: Balamb-area encounter during the return-to-Garden window.

chapters.title: The Timber Mission
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:relationship|Zell Love Quest and novice candidates}} Before boarding the train, return to the Library and talk to the Library Girl again for the **Good-bye Pururun** conversation with Zell. This must be done before the Timber departure. Also visit the Cafeteria and speak to the three Novice SeeD Candidates for their second dialogue window.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Mission preparation checklist}} Before leaving Balamb Garden, confirm you collected **Weapons Monthly April**, received the **Magical Lamp**, obtained Diablos, and challenged Cid for the **Seifer Card**. Suggested GF planning favors long-term stat Bonus growth over perfectly even junction coverage. If you want the long-term route, farm Fastitocalon-F on the beach until Siren has at least **Mag Bonus** and Diablos has his key refinement abilities plus **Enc-Half**. If that grind is too long, stop when the core refinement tools are learned and keep moving.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

{{CALLOUT:card|Queen of Cards setup}} In Balamb Town, save outside town, then lose the **MiniMog Card** to the Queen of Cards near the station. Speak to her afterward: the desired destination is **Dollet**. If she moves to Galbadia/Deling City, reload. This sets up the Kiros Card route and also earns the rare-card-loss achievement if it has not triggered already. Buy the train ticket for **3,000 Gil** when finished.

Board the train. Follow Selphie and Zell into the hallway, keep speaking to Selphie until she says she will meet you in the cabin, then enter the cabin and speak to Zell. Examine the luggage compartment to obtain **Pet Pals Vol. 1**. When asked about Timber, answer **Not too much**. The party then enters the first Laguna dream.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Laguna dream transfer rules}} Squall, Zell, and Selphie become Laguna, Kiros, and Ward. Junctions, stocked magic, items, levels, EXP, and stat growth transfer between the associated characters. Laguna inherits Squall's junctions, Kiros inherits Zell's, and Ward inherits Selphie's. Laguna has a separate 3,000 Gil stash during the dream, normal SeeD salary does not pay out, and weapons remain character-specific. Quistis cannot learn Blue Magic during the dream, but Blue Magic items found there remain usable afterward.

{{ENCOUNTERS:1}}

Follow the linear Timber Forest path. Funguar can provide status magic if you did not refine much from cards. There is a **Cure draw point** on the second forest screen and a **Water draw point** near the truck. Enter the truck to reach Deling City. In Deling City, ignore the sewers unless you deliberately want pointless encounters, go to the Galbadia Hotel, head downstairs, and choose **Let's take a load off**. The other options are optional flavor. Approach the piano, return to the table, then speak to the receptionist for Julia's room.

Back in Timber, approach the man by the stairs where Squall got off the train. Answer **But the Owls are still around** to proceed, then follow him to the Forest Owls train. Save in the next car before doing anything else.

{{CALLOUT:card|Watts and Angelo Card}} Before starting the mission, challenge Watts to Triple Triad. Use the rule-mixing prompt to try to spread **Open** to Galbadia, but do not allow **Same** to be abolished. If Same is abolished or Watts stops asking to mix rules, reset and try again. Win the **Angelo Card** from Watts now; he will not be available again for a long stretch. Angelo Card can be Card Modded into **100x Elixir**, but if you care about card collection, defer the mod until you are ready to recover or replace it later.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Forest Owls train mission}} Save before talking to Watts to begin. The mission has seven phases and a strict **5-minute** timer; running out is Game Over. Phase 1: climb onto the 2nd escort car. Phase 2: sneak across the roof; move only while the red guard scans below, stop when the blue guard scans, and remember that closed blinds mean neither guard is scanning. Phase 3: cross the President car roof. Phase 4: descend by rope and enter three correct 4-code sequences to uncouple the first escort car. Phase 5: watch the dummy/base car movement. Phase 6: descend again and enter five correct 4-code sequences to uncouple the second escort car. Phase 7: escape. The code-entry prompts use the four face-button positions; use the Controls page for your selected platform mapping rather than memorizing PlayStation labels.
| Times Caught | SeeD Rank Change |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | +1 |
| 1-9 | 0 |
| 10+ | -1 |

{{CALLOUT:warning|Train mission retries}} If caught, choosing to try again counts as being caught. Declining the retry gives Game Over, which can be useful if you want to reload and preserve the 0-catch rank bonus. Wrong inputs or timeouts during a code sequence clear that sequence but do not count as being caught.

After the train mission, the game opens the menu. Re-check every junction: Zell and Selphie can lose GF assignments after the dream/mission swap, so re-equip Quezacotl/Shiva/Siren and restock commands. Junction Fire or Earth to Elem-Atk and Slow to ST-Atk if available. If anyone has Mug equipped, remove it before the upcoming undead boss if you want cleaner command access. Grab **Pet Pals Vol. 2** from Rinoa's bed/bench, save, then speak to Rinoa and say you are ready.

**Boss: Fake President** (Lv 1-12) - HP: 58-778 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Cure
- Mug: Nothing (0%)
- Drop: Nothing (0%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: None
- Resist: None
- Status weak: Slow (90%), The End

**Boss: Gerogero** (Lv 1-12) - HP: 350-3,650 | AP: 20 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Esuna, Double, Berserk, Zombie
- Mug: 2x Phoenix Down (50%)
- Drop: 8x Zombie Powder (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Undead: Yes
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Fire x3, Earth x3, Holy x3
- Resist: Poison nullified
- Status weak: Slow (90%)

Strategy: The Fake President is a short first phase. Gerogero is undead, so healing items and restorative magic are lethal: Elixir, Phoenix Down, Curaga, X-Potion, or Recover can end the fight immediately. Before finishing, draw **Esuna**, **Double**, **Berserk**, and **Zombie**. Physical attacks are poor; use healing items/magic or exploit Fire, Earth, and Holy weakness. Gerogero's **Brrawghh!** can inflict Curse, Darkness, and Slow, so cure Curse quickly because it blocks Limit Break access.

After the boss, Rinoa must join the party. Swapping her in for Selphie is the cleanest route, but immediately move junctions and magic so the active party remains functional. Speak to Watts when ready to return to Timber.

{{CALLOUT:shop|Timber Pet Shop}} Visit the Pet Shop near the station stairs first. Buy **Pet Pals Vol. 3** and **Pet Pals Vol. 4**. The shop also sells **Amnesia Greens** and command scrolls for **5,000 Gil each**: Magic Scroll, GF Scroll, Draw Scroll, and Item Scroll. With Tonberry's Familiar later, it also sells **HP-J**, **Str-J**, **Vit-J**, **Mag-J**, and **Spr-J Scrolls** for **10,000 Gil each**. Pet Pals Vol. 5 and Vol. 6 are later Esthar purchases and unlock Angelo Search and Wishing Star.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Angelo Recover and Reverse}} Do **not** teach Rinoa **Angelo Recover** or **Angelo Reverse** until after Angelo Search farming is finished much later. Recover and Reverse trigger automatically and can interrupt idle Angelo Search setups. Pet Pals Vol. 1 teaches Angelo Strike after 800 steps. Vol. 2 teaches Angelo Recover after 200 steps. Vol. 3 teaches Invincible Moon after 920 steps. Vol. 4 teaches Angelo Reverse after 260 steps. Vol. 5 teaches Angelo Search after 400 steps. Vol. 6 teaches Wishing Star after 1,060 steps.

{{CALLOUT:enemy|Optional Timber guard fight}} South of the station, past the Junk Shop and Hotel, two Elite Soldiers threaten Timber guards. Fighting them appears to be character flavor rather than a tangible reward, but it is available before continuing the main route.

Enter the **Timber Maniacs** building. Pick up **Girl Next Door** from the magazine piles; it is needed later on the White SeeD Ship for the Shiva Card route. The left door has a **Blizzaga draw point**. The newsroom contains a **Timber Maniacs** issue and optional NPC stories.

{{CALLOUT:point|Old man house choice}} In the house next door to Timber Maniacs, look through the second-floor window to see the back alley route. The old man's house can either heal the party for free or give **500 Gil** from the cupboards. Talking to the old man first gives the heal; checking the cupboards four times gives 500 Gil and locks out the heal.

Go to the Pub screen. Two G-Soldiers block the way and drop a **Buel Card** after defeat. In the pub, the drunk blocks the back exit. Tell him about the card to receive a **Tonberry Card**, or buy him a drink to receive a **Forbidden Card**. The Forbidden Card is usually more useful for refinement, while Tonberry is still acceptable if you prefer it. Use the back door after he moves.

Use the Save Point and follow the path to the TV station. Scenes play through the broadcast and the party eventually returns outside. Return to the pub, then to the house with the second-floor window for more scenes. Back in control, talk to Quistis twice, then try to leave the house to advance.

{{CALLOUT:spoiler|TV broadcast scene}} The Timber broadcast introduces the Sorceress as Galbadia's public power behind the President. This is a story reveal; the walkthrough keeps the practical route around it but marks the visual as spoiler-adjacent.

{{CALLOUT:item|Zone supply package}} Before leaving Timber, Chief gives **Potion**, **Phoenix Down**, **Soft**, **Antidote**, and **Remedy** for the journey. Accept the free supplies; Remedy is especially useful this early.

{{CALLOUT:sidequest|Timber journalist trigger}} Before permanently departing Timber on Disc 1, return to the town square near the Timber Maniacs building and encourage the aspiring journalist. On Disc 2, return to the same square and speak to him again to receive a **Pet Nametag**. The Disc 1 trigger must be done now.

Speak to the G-Soldier outside, head toward the pub, receive the Galbadia Garden train ticket, then board the train near Zone. On the train, speak to everyone, including Selphie in the hallway until she has no new lines, then speak to Zell and choose **Leave him alone** to proceed.

checkpoints.description: Do not teach Rinoa these skills until you're completely finished with Angelo Searching.

chapters.title: Dollet Exploration
chapters.content:
The next story destination is Galbadia Garden near East Academy Station, but this optional window is the right time to ride the train all the way to **Dollet** instead. If you want to continue the story immediately, get off at East Academy Station; otherwise, use this stop to clean up cards, magazines, and Dollet setup.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

{{CALLOUT:enemy|Useful Dollet-area encounters}} On Dollet beaches, **Adamantoise** can be Mugged for **Whisper**, which teaches Quistis **White Wind** and cannot be obtained through Card Mod. Adamantoise also inflicts Darkness and has very high defenses, so expect long fights. Away from beaches, especially forests and cliff bases, **Anacondaur** is the current source for **Dragon Skin** drops for Zell weapon planning. Dragon Skin is unreliable before Lv 30+, so do not expect quick results at low party levels.

{{CALLOUT:card|Dollet card-rule setup}} Dollet starts with rough Triple Triad rules, especially Random and the lack of Open. Ideally carry Galbadian rules with Open already spread. Challenge the red-dressed girl near the car-rental area, accept the rule-mix prompt, then quit at the rule screen until Open spreads or Random is abolished. If Same spreads, Elemental is abolished too early, or the rule-mix prompt stops with no useful change, reset and try again. After a useful change, return to Timber, follow the road to avoid encounters, challenge the left entrance guard until he stops asking to mix rules, then play a game to re-carry Galbadian rules back to Dollet.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Timber journalist trigger}} While returning through Timber during this setup, go back to the Timber Maniacs building and speak to the man outside. Choose **No way** to encourage him to become a journalist. This Disc 1 trigger is required for the Disc 2 **Pet Nametag** reward.

Stay in the **Dollet Hotel** to collect one **Timber Maniacs** entry. If the Queen of Cards is in Dollet, go upstairs in the pub and manipulate the Trade Rule until she uses **All**. Once All is active, do not play more games with her for now. Another **Timber Maniacs** issue is upstairs in the pub.

{{CALLOUT:card|Dollet Pub owner rewards}} Upstairs in the pub, beat the Pub Owner in Triple Triad to access his private room. Speak to him there to receive **5x Geezard Card**, **4x Red Bat Card**, **3x Buel Card**, **2x Anacondaur Card**, and **1x Cactuar Card**. Challenge him again in the private room to win the **Siren Card**. Siren Card can be Card Modded into **3x Status Atk-J Scroll**, but keep it if you are maintaining a complete card collection.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Occult Fan II}} In the Pub Owner private room, examine the magazine pile nearest the exit. The useful one-time prize is **Occult Fan II**. The stack can award up to three items per room visit; if you receive three items without Occult Fan II, exit the private room, re-enter, and try again. Keep cycling the room until the magazine appears.

{{CALLOUT:sidequest|Dollet Bone Quest timing}} The Bone Quest starts in the artist house south of the pub, where the Queen of Cards son lives. It is available on Disc 1-3 but not Disc 4. The source route recommends waiting until Disc 3 because the rewards upgrade: Disc 1/2 rewards are **Potion**, **Phoenix Down**, and **Soft**; Disc 3 rewards are **X-Potion**, **Mega-Potion**, and **Elixir**. If you want the better tier, leave the quest alone for now.

{{CALLOUT:sidequest|Dollet Bone Quest route}} To complete it, enter the artist house and examine the painting. Talk to the boy, go to the painted location, and talk to the dog by the bone. Repeat this painting -> boy -> dog loop for each location. The three reward pairs are **Potion or X-Potion**, **Phoenix Down or Mega-Potion**, and **Soft or Elixir**, depending on completion disc. Completing the full quest also plays an extra scene.

{{CALLOUT:card|Queen of Cards son}} If MiniMog was lost to the Queen in Balamb, win it back from her son in the artist house. Over the full Queen of Cards quest, this boy can hold cards lost to the Queen, including **MiniMog**, **Sacred**, **Chicobo**, **Alexander**, and **Doomtrain**. Recover each before handing over the next rare card if you want the cleanest inventory.

The Dollet Item Shop and Junk Shop are down the street from the pub in the building marked **Nautilus**. At least open the Item Shop inventory once. If X-ATM092 was not destroyed during the exam, the car here is destroyed and the owner dialogue changes.

{{CALLOUT:card|Dollet card-player tiers}} Dollet has useful low-level card filters. The yellow-dressed woman near the store plays only Level 1 cards. The pub bartender plays Level 2. The blue-sleeved man near the stairs to the beach plays Level 3. The car-rental girl plays only Level 4. No Dollet player here plays Level 5 cards. If using All, quit if the trade rule changes and keep checking until All returns.

You can explore the path toward the Communications Tower by speaking to the guard blocking it twice. There is nothing useful there and the tower itself cannot be re-entered, so treat it as optional scenery only.

When finished, head for **East Academy Station**. Following the nearby railroad tracks avoids random encounters, and the party can safely stop approaching trains instantly.

enemies.name: Adamantoise
enemies.notes: Beach encounter. Mug Whisper for Quistis White Wind; high defenses and Darkness pressure make fights slow.

chapters.title: Journey to Galbadia Garden
chapters.content:
From **East Academy Station**, look for the nearby circular forest. Before entering, move all junctions to **Squall, Quistis, and Selphie**, even if they are not currently the active party. The next dream sequence maps Squall to Laguna, Quistis to Kiros, and Selphie to Ward; Zell and Rinoa do not participate, so anything junctioned to them is unavailable during the sequence.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Excavation site setup}} Lightning on Elem-Atk is useful here because nothing resists it and the strongest enemies, Elastoid and Cyborg Esthar Soldier, are weak to Lightning. This dream works like the first Laguna sequence: magic, items, levels, EXP, and stat growth transfer between paired characters. Actions taken here also change Disc 3 Lunatic Pandora rewards, so follow the route carefully.

{{CALLOUT:enemy|Elastoid priority}} Lv 30+ Elastoids can be Mugged for **Laser Cannon**, which teaches Quistis **Homing Laser** and refines via Ammo-RF into **5x Pulse Ammo**. The source notes you usually should not be Lv 30+ yet, but if you are, this is valuable. Elastoid also has **Dispel** at all levels, **Stop** at mid/high levels, and **Meltdown** to draw; Meltdown is one of the best Vit-J spells in the game.

Choose whichever dialogue option you like, then follow the linear path to the first fork. There is a **Sleep draw point** here. Continue for a back attack against an Esthar Soldier, then defeat three more soldiers in the next battle.

{{CALLOUT:point|Disc 3 reward triggers}} Take the right fork and follow the path down into the blue area. Near the junk on the ground, walk until a dialog opens and choose **Pick it up**; this Old Key unlocks a later **LuvLuv G**. Continue left to the screen with three door-like panels, fiddle with the **middle** lever, then leave; this opens a later **Silence draw point**.

Return to the early fork and take the other path to a three-way fork. The right path has a **Confuse draw point**. Take the center ladder down. Behind the broken barricade on the left in the blue area is another **Old Key**; pick it up to unlock a later **Ultima draw point**. Continue and mess with the large hatch to unlock a later **Phoenix Pinion**.

Return to the right-hand path and go back to the door panels. This time tamper with the **right** panel to unlock a later **Power Generator**, one of the rarest items. Continue north from the first Old Key junction until you reach the detonator. Press the **red switch first**, then the **blue switch**; this opens both sides and unlocks later **Combat King 005** access. Farther up, examine the large rock on the left wall and push it aside to unlock a later **Spd-J Scroll**. Use the Save Point at the three-way intersection, then head upward.

The final section is forced E-Soldier encounters. If every setup step above was done, only the final battle occurs; otherwise, up to five battles can happen. The last battle ends with **Soul Crush** on Kiros and Ward, followed by the wake-up scene.

{{CALLOUT:warning|Wake-up HP check}} Back in the real world, Selphie and Quistis may be at **1 HP** because of Soul Crush. Heal before using them. The forest can now be walked on the world map.

{{CALLOUT:refinement|Dragon Fang route}} If you want Squall's Lion Heart early, farm **4x Dragon Fang** from Grendels in the Yaulny Canyon forest before entering Galbadia Garden. Grendels appear in this forest only while touching the cliff walls. Dragon Fang is a drop from Lv 1-20 Grendels and an uncommon drop from Lv 20-29 Grendels. Do **not** Mug Grendels: their Mug is Dragon Fin, and a successful Mug cancels the post-battle Dragon Fang drop.

{{ENCOUNTERS:1}}

If you are done farming or do not care about Lion Heart now, leave the forest and head west to **Galbadia Garden**. It is very close and should appear shortly after exiting the forest.

chapters.title: Galbadia Garden
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:point|Galbadia Garden arrival}} Run toward the main building. If Quistis was in the party, the game forces a party change. When the announcement calls for the 2F Reception Room, pass through the main hall, take the far hallway, go upstairs, and enter the room at the top.

In the 2F Reception Room, talk to party members in this exact progression to move the story: **Zell -> Quistis -> Rinoa -> Zell -> Selphie**. Everyone else can be spoken to for optional commentary.

{{CALLOUT:character|Irvine joins}} Return to the front gate and talk to Rinoa. After the scene, **Irvine Kinneas** joins with **20x Normal Ammo**. Talk to anyone after receiving orders, choose the party, and watch the Switch tutorial.

{{CALLOUT:card|Trabia rule setup}} The Trabia exchange student in the locker room can be used to fix Trabia Triple Triad rules early. Save in the main hall, run between a Galbadian player and the exchange student, keep Galbadia with Same, and aim to spread Open while removing Random and Plus from Trabia. In Remaster, this is the important early opportunity because reliable RNG-based Trabia rule methods for Discs 1-3 are not known.

{{CALLOUT:drawing|Invisible Haste draw point}} There is an invisible **Haste draw point** in the dead center of Galbadia Garden's main hall. Draw from it before leaving.

When finished, leave through the front gate. Go to the nearby train station, pay **3,000 Gil** for the Deling City ticket, and on the train try to leave for a scene. Enter the corridor, then return to the entrance to continue.

{{CALLOUT:sidequest|Tomb of the Unknown King overview}} The Tomb route is optional beyond the story ID number, but completing it now earns **8x G-Hi-Potion**, **Sacred Card**, **Brothers GF**, and **Minotaur Card**. The tomb map can be opened in-game for orientation. Escaping from the tomb through the map command costs a SeeD rank, so navigate out normally instead.

{{CALLOUT:point|Tomb orientation}} The Tomb uses a quasi-first-person orientation: the direction the camera faces is the direction the party entered from, not necessarily north on the map. Going backward always returns to the previous room, but the camera angle reverses when you do this. The fastest mental rule is simple: turn right whenever possible to visit the outer rooms in order, then head for the center.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

{{CALLOUT:point|Tomb route map}} Use the visual map before entering the outer loop. The sword with the student ID number is marked near the entrance side. (**Note:** the student ID number is randomly generated for each save file — there is no fixed number to look up; memorize it or write it down when it is shown.) Points 1 and 4 are boss fights; check HP and junctions before interacting there.

{{CALLOUT:sidequest|Brothers route}} At point #1, examine the statue to fight **Sacred**. After Sacred flees, keep turning right whenever possible until reaching the sluice. Draw **Float** from the left side, then open the sluice with the chain opposite the draw point. Continue the right-turn route to the water wheel and examine the post on the far wall. Then head for the center: from the first fork turn right, then left at the second fork, then left again at the fourth. Heal, junction Aero or Bio to Elem-Atk, and junction Quake/Earth defense if available before crossing the bridge.

**Boss: Sacred** (Lv 1-75) - HP: 578-27,218 | AP: 20 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Shell, Protect, Berserk, Life
- Mug: Nothing (0%)
- Drop: 8x G-Hi-Potion (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Wind, Poison
- Resist: Earth nullified
- Status weak: Drain, The End

**Boss: Minotaur** (Lv 1-75) - HP: 855-36,375 | AP: 20 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Shell, Protect, Berserk, Double
- Mug: Nothing (0%)
- Drop: 8x G-Returner (100%)
- Card drop: Sacred (100%)
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Wind, Poison
- Absorb: Earth
- Status weak: Drain, The End

Strategy: Cast **Float** on your party to prevent Minotaur from healing via Earth-element counterplay (Minotaur absorbs Earth — avoid Earth-junctioned attacks). Wind and Poison damage hit both Sacred and Minotaur effectively. Sacred uses Shell, Protect, Berserk, and Life; Minotaur uses Shell, Protect, Berserk, and Double. After the fight, name **Brothers** and receive the **Minotaur Card** in the scene. Brothers is Earth-elemental.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Brothers ability order}} Source priority: **Str-J -> Elem-Atk-J -> HP+% abilities -> HP Bonus -> other junctions -> Boost -> everything else**. HP Bonus is powerful but takes a huge AP investment because the HP+ chain must be learned first, so Str-J and Elem-Atk-J are more immediately useful unless you are deliberately delaying levels for bonus growth.

To leave after the Brothers fight, cross the center bridge and keep going straight until outside. If doing this as soon as it becomes available, return to Deling City for the main operation.

enemies.notes: Tomb regular encounter.

enemies.name: Blobra

enemies.name: Buel

enemies.notes: Tomb undead encounter; healing and Life effects are useful.

enemies.name: Sacred
enemies.notes: Boss at tomb point #1 and first half of Brothers fight.

enemies.name: Minotaur

chapters.title: Deling City
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:point|Deling City arrival}} Leave the station and use the bus loop from the central plaza to reach Caraway's Mansion. If the Queen of Cards setup was done earlier, stop at the Shopping Arcade first and challenge the man in black across from the Junk Shop to win the **Kiros Card**.

{{CALLOUT:refinement|Kiros Card refinement}} The **Kiros Card** Card Mods into **3x Accelerator**. Accelerators teach **Auto-Haste**, one of the strongest passive abilities in the game. If you want to use them now, teach one GF in the Strength group and one GF in the Defense group; the Magic group can learn Auto-Haste naturally later. If you prefer stable endgame GF layouts, hold the Accelerators until those groups settle. Rare cards can be reacquired on Disc 4 with the right preparation, but do not mod cards still needed for Queen of Cards quest steps.

Deling's Item Shop has nothing new, but the **Junk Shop** is useful if you already have ultimate-weapon ingredients for Squall or Selphie. At Caraway's Mansion, speak to the soldier outside. He asks for a randomly assigned student ID number from the Tomb of the Unknown King, sells a unique **Location Displayer** for **5,000 Gil**, and offers two hints for **3,000 Gil** each. The hints are unnecessary; the Location Displayer is unique.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Missable - Location Displayer}} Buy the **Location Displayer** before giving the guard the student ID number. It cannot be purchased after the ID is turned in.

After accepting the ID-number objective, let the guard escort you out and choose the car-rental drop-off. Rent a car if desired, or leave on foot. The Tomb of the Unknown King is on the small cape northeast of Deling City; roads do not reach it, so random encounters are possible unless you use Enc-None or a car for part of the trip.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

{{CALLOUT:drawing|Tomb route to ID number}} At the Tomb entrance, draw from the outer **Protect draw point** if desired. Go inside, follow the linear path to the next screen, and examine the sword to see the student ID number. Memorize it or write it down exactly; if you forget it, you must return to the sword. The optional Brothers route belongs to the Tomb section from the previous chapter, but this is the mandatory story step.

{{CALLOUT:card|Queen of Cards - Sacred step}} Before returning to Caraway, you may advance the Queen of Cards quest by returning to Dollet, saving, and losing the **Sacred Card** to the Queen. The desired destination is **Balamb**; reset if she goes elsewhere. Win Sacred back from her son before leaving if you want to keep or mod it later.

{{CALLOUT:refinement|Minotaur Card refinement}} If you completed the Brothers route, the **Minotaur Card** Card Mods into **10x Adamantine**. Adamantine is needed for several ultimate weapons and can also teach **Vit+60%**. Do not mod the **Sacred Card** until its Queen of Cards step is done; it mods into **100x Dino Bone** for easy Quake stock, but the quest timing matters more.

Back in Deling City, use the Shopping Arcade and Hotel prep window. Stay at the Hotel for **100 Gil** to heal, then pick up the **Timber Maniacs** issue near the far bed in the hotel room. A woman across the street from the Hotel uses FH Triple Triad rules; save inside the hotel before using her to manipulate rules for that region.

Give the correct ID number to the mansion guard and enter Caraway's Mansion. Talking to Rinoa in the waiting room advances the story; if Rinoa was in the active party, reform the party but do not spend time moving junctions yet. Follow General Caraway through the city plan, then make final purchases/preparations and return to the mansion to begin the operation.

{{CALLOUT:point|Assassination operation start}} As Quistis, try to leave the room after the scene. As Squall, follow Caraway, talk to him under the arch, then continue toward the Presidential Palace entrance. When control returns to Quistis's party, leave junctions alone for the moment and return to Caraway's Mansion through the park.

Inside the mansion, control switches to Rinoa. Ignore the crates at first and examine the manhole cover to enter the sewers. Junction her if needed, run west until the magazine on the ground appears, collect **Weapons Mon May**, then return to the starting point. Climb the crates to the roof path, follow it until the scene, then walk forward.

Back with Quistis, examine the glasses on the shelf near the door, then use the statue near the window to reveal the hidden passage. Junction the party if the sewer encounters worry you, use the Save Point, and continue until the view returns to Squall.

As Squall, run right between scenes until reaching the gate, then enter. Move GFs and key magic off inactive characters and onto **Squall and Irvine**. Climb the same crate route Rinoa used. Junction **Quake** to Elem-Atk if available, Fire-elemental magic to Elem-Def, and Petrify protection to ST-Def if available. At least one character must have **Draw** equipped before entering Rinoa's room.

{{CALLOUT:achievement|Draw Carbuncle}} The Iguions begin with Reflect that cannot be removed normally. Draw **Carbuncle** from either Iguion immediately; once Carbuncle is drawn, all Iguions lose Reflect.

**Boss: 2x Iguion** (Lv 1-19) - HP: 127-1,747 | AP: 10 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Cure, Esuna, Break, Carbuncle
- Mug: 6x G-Returner (25%)
- Drop: 4x G-Returner (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Earth, Holy x2.5
- Resist: Fire nullified
- Inherent status: Reflect until Carbuncle is drawn
- Status weak: Drain, The End

Strategy: Draw **Carbuncle** first to gain the GF and remove Reflect. Draw **Break** here if you need it, though low Magic can make stocking slow. The Iguions use Magma Breath for single-target Fire damage plus Petrifying, Resonance for magical damage to all, and claw attacks. **Quake** with a decent Magic stat can wipe both out; otherwise physical attacks are enough. Mugging earns **6x G-Returner** instead of the **4x G-Returner** drop, but the item is low priority. Do not finish the fight before Carbuncle is drawn.

{{CALLOUT:story|Edea parade scene}} After naming Carbuncle, use the hatch in the hallway to reach the clock tower. Examine the sniper rifle to switch back to Quistis's party.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Carbuncle ability order}} If you have a **Turtle Shell** from Armadodo or Card Mod, use it on Carbuncle to teach **Vit+20%** and unlock Vit+40% faster. If you obtained an **Orihalcon** from X-ATM092, it can teach **Vit+60%** and unlock Vit Bonus immediately. Ability priority: **Recov Med-RF -> Vit+% abilities -> Vit Bonus -> Mag-J -> other junctions -> Counter -> Auto-Reflect -> remaining abilities**. If Vit+40% was taught early, leave Vit+20% until immediately after Auto-Reflect. Carbuncle's summon applies Reflect to the whole party without dealing damage.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|GF grouping note}} At this point, balance stat Bonus growth and junction coverage across Strength, Defense, and Magic groups. The important local rule is: keep the active party fully junctioned, give everyone who will draw from upcoming bosses access to Mag-J when possible, and move Diablos to the Magic group once Carbuncle learns Mag-J if you want that group to gain HP-J later. Carbuncle's Mag-J is a priority because several upcoming boss spells are not easy to refine yet.

{{ENCOUNTERS:1}}

{{CALLOUT:point|Deling sewer route}} In Quistis's sewer route, water wheels are one-way, some doors open and others do not, and ladders facing the wrong way can be dropped to make bridges. From the regain-control screen, head north, travel left/clockwise around the square of screens, return on the far side of the water, follow the linear path to a water wheel, ride it upward, and continue toward the exit.

Detailed sewer route: in the first screen north, cross and go left. Ignore the ladder side route unless you want the draw point. Continue around until the first water wheel after that draw point, cross the bridge, open the door, and continue. In the next water-wheel screen, cross back if you want the **Zombie draw point**; otherwise ride the two water wheels across. At the next junction, use the water wheel to drop down, go as far south as possible, drop the ladder to create a shortcut back to Caraway's side, then follow the path right until a water wheel. Ride it and follow the walkway to the exit. In the tower, flip the switch.

After the scene, set the active battle party to **Squall, Irvine, and Rinoa**. Move all GFs, commands, and useful magic to them. Bio on Squall's Elem-Atk is useful, Fire protection on Elem-Def helps, and Slow on Squall's ST-Atk is useful. ST-Def is not important for the next two fights. Give Squall Mug if convenient, or leave Mug on the Diablos holder.

**Boss: Seifer** (Lv 1-20) - HP: 176-1,076 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Fire, Cure, Life | Lv 20: Fira, Cura, Life
- Mug: Hero (6.25%)
- Drop: Nothing (0%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Poison x1.5
- Status weak: Drain, Slow (90%), The End

Strategy: Seifer is a solo Squall fight and is mostly a formality, but he is the first mandatory boss here with multiple level groups. Check his draw list to confirm whether you are seeing the Lv 1-19 or Lv 20 version. He relies on Hyperion attacks, rarely pulls the trigger, and can cast Fira. Mug the **Hero** if possible, draw any needed Fire/Cure/Life-family spells, then finish him.

**Boss: Edea** (Lv 1-20) - HP: 1,300-7,000 | AP: 20 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Cura, Dispel, Life, Double
- Mug: Elixir (12.5%)
- Drop: Nothing (0%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Weakness: None
- Resist: None
- Status weak: The End

Strategy: Edea uses -aga elemental spells for heavy damage, can rarely cast Protect and Shell on herself, and will spend turns casting Dispel on party members with Reflect. Immediately summon **Carbuncle** to put Reflect on the whole party, let Edea waste three turns Dispelling it, then resummon as needed. Draw **Cura**, **Dispel**, **Life**, and **Double** if you need stock. Mug the **Elixir** if possible, then win the fight for the AP. Losing does not cause Game Over, but it forfeits the AP.

Watch the scenes after Edea. Disc 2 begins with the party in captivity.

chapters.title: Winhill
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:story|Disc 2 opening}} Disc 2 opens in a Laguna dream at **Winhill**. After the scene, go upstairs and draw from the **Curaga draw point** in the wardrobe before leaving the house. Then go downstairs, leave the house, and head to the **pub** next door south in the square. Speak to **Kiros** for the scene before doing errands elsewhere.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Missable - Eyes On Me}} During the Winhill pub conversation, speak with **Raine** and ask about **Julia** before leaving the conversation. This is the only way to unlock the **Eyes On Me** entry, and the opportunity does not return.

While in the pub, check the **magazine on the table** to collect the **Timber Maniacs** issue available here.

After all conversation topics are done, choose **Let's talk later** so Kiros joins. Kiros inherits **Irvine's junctions**; if you opened the menu before he joined, some GFs may have been removed, so check his junctions before continuing. Return to the town square. Junctioning Fire-elemental magic to Elem-Atk is useful for the patrol enemies.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

Outside, Laguna and Kiros are hunting bugs. The enemies here are not threatening. Draw from the **Dispel draw point** near the river if desired, then follow the path south from the square. At the intersection, either path works, but the lower route has a hidden **Reflect draw point** near a truck. Continue south until Laguna says the patrol is finished.

Before going back to the pub, spend Laguna's separate **3,000 Gil** stash at the shop on the southern edge of the screen. Items bought here carry forward, but unspent dream Gil does not transfer to Squall's party. Buy whatever supplies you want, then return to the square.

Rest in the house if desired, go upstairs in the pub for more scenes, then return to the house and go upstairs to end the dream.

checkpoints.label: Eyes on Me
checkpoints.description: When speaking to Raine in Winhill, ask her about Julia before leaving the conversation. This unlocks the Eyes on Me entry and the opportunity does not return.

enemies.name: Bite Bug
enemies.notes: Patrol enemy; Fire-elemental attacks work well.

enemies.name: Caterchipillar
enemies.notes: Patrol enemy; can provide Thunder/Cure-family magic depending on level.

chapters.title: The Escape
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:point|D-District Prison opening}} As Zell, talk to Rinoa twice, then work through the scenes. For the best scene route, stop the guard when prompted; as Squall, choose **Just let me die**. After returning to Zell, talk to Quistis, leave the cell, and run upstairs to trigger Zell vs. **2x Guard**. The Guards are weak enough to defeat normally.

When the menu opens, fully junction the party. Elem-Atk is not important yet, but status attack works well if it is not Death. Fire and Lightning protection are acceptable on Elem-Def, status defense is not important for this first boss, and everyone should have **Draw**. Give someone **Mug**.

**Boss: Biggs (2nd)** (Lv 1-22) - HP: 1,467-2,235 | AP: 5 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Cure, Haste, Slow, Regen | Lv 20-22: Cura, Haste, Slow, Regen
- Mug: Regen Ring (25%)
- Drop: 3x Elixir (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: None
- Resist: None
- Status weak: Confuse (60%), Darkness (40%), Drain, Poison (80%), Silence (80%), Slow (90%), Zombie (70%), The End

**Boss: Wedge (2nd)** (Lv 1-22) - HP: 1,416-2,139 | AP: 5 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Fire, Shell, Protect, Reflect | Lv 20-22: Fira, Shell, Protect, Reflect
- Mug: Strength Love (25%)
- Drop: 8x Remedy (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: None
- Resist: None
- Status weak: Confuse (60%), Darkness (40%), Drain, Poison (80%), Silence (80%), Slow (90%), Zombie (70%), The End

Strategy: Biggs and Wedge are returning bosses with better draw lists and better steals than drops. Mug **Regen Ring** from Biggs and **Strength Love** from Wedge if possible. Draw **Reflect** from Wedge and **Haste/Slow/Regen** from Biggs as needed. Silence and Blind work well. After the fight, junction any new magic and set Lightning-elemental magic to Elem-Atk.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

{{CALLOUT:point|Prison floor reference}} The prison has 15 floors. Barrier floors force you to go around to continue; no-barrier floors let you pass through. The lower floors contain many missables, so go down for items before heading to the Torture Room objective.
| Floor | Notes | Left Cell | Right Cell |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Exit |  |  |  |
| Control Room | Hidden Save Point |  |  |
| Torture Room | Story objective |  |  |
| 12 | N/A | N/A | N/A |
| 11 | N/A | Card Player (200 Gil) | Hidden Thundaga draw point |
| 10 | N/A | Save Point | Card Player (300 Gil, Character Report) |
| 9 | N/A | N/A | Berserk draw point |
| 8 | No barrier | Man from Garden Shop | N/A |
| 7 | No barrier | Starting location | N/A |
| 6 | Save Point | N/A | N/A |
| 5 | N/A | N/A | Card Player (500 Gil) |
| 4 | N/A | Tent | N/A |
| 3 | N/A | N/A | Pet House |
| 2 | N/A | Pet Nametag | Str Up |
| 1 | No barrier | Hidden Save Point | Combat King 001 |
| Empty hallway |  |  |  |

{{CALLOUT:missable|Missable - Pet Nametag}} Pick up the **Pet Nametag** from Floor 2 left cell before leaving the prison. It is one of only two copies normally available.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Perfect Game - Character Report}} Beat the Floor 10 card player once to receive **Character Report**, the Battle Meter upgrade. If Battle Meter was missed on Disc 1, this upgrade cannot be obtained.

{{CALLOUT:refinement|Rosetta Stone card-player method}} The Floor 11 card player costs **200 Gil** and can award Rosetta Stone items at very low probability. With Enc-None and the Floor 10 save point, the source method is: challenge and refuse the 200 Gil player **54 times**, then play and win. The same player has a one-time **HP Up**; use **64** challenge refusals before playing for that result. This is optional and mainly useful for perfect-game routing.

Restock at the Man from Garden Shop on Floor 8 if needed. The Floor 5 card player costs **500 Gil** and has a one-time **Luck Up** prize at **1/64** odds. Floor 1 right cell has **Combat King 001**. Floor 11 right cell has the hidden **Thundaga draw point**.

Talk to the Moomba nearest the Torture Room door, then to Squall in the Torture Room. Use the Moombas to remove three floor barriers; Floor 9 is the best barrier to remove. Examine the control panel when prompted to press the red button, then examine the door for more scenes.

As Zell, run from the guards. Forced guard groups are manageable with attacks or Limit Breaks. After scenes, choose one more party member to fight alongside Rinoa and Squall; Quistis is a strong choice. Junction the party and head upward until the view switches to Irvine.

{{CALLOUT:story|Irvine bridge scene}} As Irvine, move junctions to the new group and descend to the crane on Floor 3. After the scenes, control returns to Squall. Speak to the Moomba near the stairs for **Cottage** and the farther Moomba for **Rename Card**. Move junctions again, continue upward, use the hidden top-floor Save Point if you have Move-Find, equip Mug, and junction Lightning-elemental magic to Elem-Atk.

**Boss: Elite Soldier** (Lv 1-70) - HP: 45-2,260 | AP: 2 | EXP: 30
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Fire, Thunder, Blizzard, Scan | Lv 20-29: Fira, Thundara, Blizzara, Scan | Lv 30+: Firaga, Thundaga, Blizzaga, Dispel

**Boss: GIM52A** (Lv 1-70) - HP: 1,431-10,850 | AP: 3 | EXP: 30
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Haste, Slow | Lv 20-29: Haste, Slow, Dispel | Lv 30+: Haste, Slow, Esuna, Dispel

Strategy: Defeat the Elite Soldier first. Mug at least one **Missile** from a GIM52A to teach Quistis **Micro Missiles**. Lightning-elemental attacks are effective. After the fight and scenes, hold **Right** during the bridge sequence or the game ends.

{{CALLOUT:point|Desert party split}} In the Galbadia desert, use the **Aero draw point** if desired. Talk to Selphie twice to proceed. Selphie leads the Missile Base team while Squall goes to Balamb Garden. Putting **Zell with Squall** earns a better later reward (**Mega Phoenix** instead of **Remedy**), but the choice is not critical. Squall's team boards the westbound train.

{{CALLOUT:card|Return to Deling City - Rinoa Card}} Before going to Missile Base, Deling City has two optional tasks. First, use available refinements if desired. Second, visit Caraway's Mansion, lose the **Ifrit Card** to General Caraway, then speak to him again; he says he lost Ifrit to Martine and will now play the **Rinoa Card**. Win Rinoa now and hang onto it. Timber and Dollet paths are blocked, so drive to the Missile Base when finished.

chapters.title: Missile Base
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:story|Missile Base team}} Control shifts to **Selphie, Quistis, and Irvine**. Drive to the Missile Base west of Deling City, talk your way past the guards in stolen uniforms, and enter the only building with a door.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|SeeD Rank discipline}} Your choices in the base affect SeeD Rank. When a dialogue option can avoid combat, avoid combat. Fighting, running away, giving bad information, or otherwise blowing cover can trigger avoidable random encounters and large SeeD Exp deductions. The full-bonus route is to keep the disguise intact and only fight the mandatory battles.

At the first card-reader scene, choose **Walk by quietly** for the cleanest response. The other non-combat options are jokes and do not lower rank. Continue to the Save Point room. From there, speak to the maintenance soldiers, go through the north door, tell the soldiers **to go on ahead**, then report back to the maintenance soldiers.

Return to the lone guard near the entrance, speak to him, and enter the side room. Use the **Blizzara draw point** if desired, examine the controls, and choose any harmless options. When confronted, choose **Try to talk my way out** and continue choosing non-fight answers.

Back in the Save Point room, examine the upper-right console area. When questioned, choose **Play it cool**, then **Help out**. Examine the missile launcher and push it until it locks into place. Speak to the supervising soldier, but do not leave the launcher room yet.

{{CALLOUT:point|Invisible Full-Life draw point}} In the launcher room, draw from the invisible **Full-Life draw point** on the left side of the screen, just in front of the missile launchers. It will usually give only a small stock, but Full-Life is too valuable to skip.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Sabotage sequence}} Return to the Save Point room and examine the control panel. Most options are flavor, but **Error Ratio** is mandatory: raise it as high as it goes, then choose **Upload Data**. If Error Ratio is not maxed, the missiles do not miss and the route ends in Game Over.

Before entering the control room, check junctions. Bio on Elem-Atk is recommended for the security fight, as are Ice or Lightning on Elem-Def. Put useful status protection on ST-Def. If possible, junction **Silence** or **Pain** to ST-Atk; Silence is excellent here because the Base Leader is fully vulnerable to it. Move Reflect stock to lower-Mag characters if high-Mag characters need to draw more during the fight, and give someone Mug.

**Boss: Base Leader** (Lv 1-100) - HP: 806-4,400 | AP: 2 | EXP: 30+5x
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Thunder, Confuse, Slow, Reflect | Lv 20-29: Thundara, Confuse, Slow, Reflect | Lv 30-100: Thundaga, Confuse, Slow, Reflect
- Mug: Tent (89.5%); Cottage (10.5%) (50%)
- Drop: Lv 1-19: 2x Potion, Phoenix Down, 8x Shotgun Ammo, Cottage | Lv 20-29: 2x Potion, Phoenix Down, Hi-Potion, Cottage | Lv 30-100: 3x Potion, Hi-Potion, Cottage (75%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Poison x1.5
- Resist: None
- Status weak: Darkness (80%), Drain, Silence, Sleep (80%), Slow (90%), The End

**Boss: Base Soldier** (Lv 1-100) - HP: 217-10,400 | AP: 1 | EXP: 30+5x
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Blizzard, Silence, Confuse | Lv 20-29: Blizzara, Silence, Confuse | Lv 30-100: Blizzaga, Silence, Confuse
- Mug: Hi-Potion (94.1%); 8x Hi-Potion (5.9%) (50%)
- Drop: Potion (89.5%); 8x Normal Ammo (5.9%); Phoenix Down (4.7%) (75%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: None
- Resist: None
- Status weak: Darkness (80%), Drain, Poison (80%), Silence (80%), Sleep (80%), Slow (90%), The End

Strategy: The **Base Leader and Base Soldiers** fight is mandatory and does not penalize SeeD Rank. Silence the Base Leader immediately to stop Confuse and recovery support, then use Sleep or Blind to waste enemy turns. Mug Base Soldiers if you want the rare **8x Hi-Potion** result; otherwise end the fight quickly.

After the security fight, rearrange junctions before the timer begins. Equip Lightning, Water, or Earth to Elem-Atk for the machine boss, remove Mug if you do not want a dead command slot, and examine the left control panel. In the next room, examine the left panel again and set the self-destruct timer. **10 minutes** is enough with normal junctions and gives the best SeeD result; 20, 30, and 40 minutes apply increasing deductions. A 10-minute timer opens the shortcut door back to the entrance room.

**Boss: BGH251F2 (Missile Base)** (Lv 1-22) - HP: 4,200-8,400 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Shell, Protect, Stop
- Mug: Nothing
- Drop: Nothing
- Card drop: Nothing
- Weakness: Lightning x1.5, Water x1.5, Earth x1.5
- Resist: Poison nullifies
- Status weak: Darkness (50%), Slow (90%), The End
- Mission item, not enemy drop: Weapons Monthly June Issue

Strategy: Blind BGH251F2 so Chaingun misses, then attack with Lightning, Water, or Earth-elemental damage. Its warning message means Beam Cannon is coming next turn; use Defend to halve the damage. Shell does not reduce the Missile Base Beam Cannon. Draw **Shell**, **Protect**, and **Stop** if needed, but the timer rewards decisiveness. **Weapons Monthly June Issue** is obtained from the mission sequence after this fight; BGH251F2 itself has no mug or drop.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Pet Nametag #2 reminder}} If you encouraged the aspiring journalist outside the Timber Maniacs building on Disc 1, put **Timber** on the cleanup list now. The reward is collected by returning to that same Timber square and speaking to him after the Missile Base sequence; if the Garden route does not let you travel there immediately, do it at the first mobile-Garden free-roam window before advancing Disc 2.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

After BGH251F2 falls, run to the front gate and press the front gate action point, then run to the complex door and use that action point too. Return to the middle of the area for the escape scenes. Squall's team now has to get Balamb Garden moving before the missiles arrive.

checkpoints.label: Weapons Monthly June
checkpoints.description: Complete the Missile Base escape sequence and BGH251F2 fight; the mission sequence awards the June weapon magazine.

checkpoints.label: Invisible Full-Life draw point
checkpoints.description: In the launcher room, draw from the invisible point in front of the missile launchers before leaving.

checkpoints.label: Pet Nametag #2 reminder
checkpoints.description: If the Timber journalist was encouraged on Disc 1, return to Timber after the Missile Base sequence or at the first mobile-Garden free-roam window and collect the Pet Nametag reward.

enemies.notes: Mandatory security fight; silence works extremely well.

enemies.notes: Timer boss; AP 0, no direct mug/drop.

chapters.title: Return to Balamb Garden
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:story|Garden Crisis opening}} Control returns to **Squall's team** at Balamb Garden's front gate. Before entering, the game briefly allows you to leave Garden. This is the **Queen of Cards trade-rule window** if she is in **Balamb Town** and you need to adjust trade rules before the Garden crisis locks you in for a while.

Junction the active party, then move toward Garden. When stopped by Faculty, answer **I don't get it** and defeat the forced **Grat + Caterchipillar** fight. At the gate, speak to the sitting child twice for a **Mega-Potion**, then enter Garden.

{{CALLOUT:hint|High-level enemy check}} If Squall is already high level from early beach grinding, the Garden Crisis fixed encounters can expose stronger enemy tables. **High-level Bombs** can be drawn from for **Firaga and Meltdown**; Meltdown is excellent for Vit junctions and inflicts Vit 0 when cast. This only applies to high-level Bombs, not every Bomb card you see.

{{CALLOUT:point|Garden Crisis room rewards}} The elevator and directory are unavailable, so clear the Garden rooms manually. Always help out or support Cid when asked.
| Area | Monsters Fought | Speak To | Reward |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Infirmary | Caterchipillar or Granaldo | Dr. Kadowaki | Elixir |
| Quad | Bomb, Glacial Eye, or nothing | Back-most male SeeD | X-Potion |
| Cafeteria | Bomb | 2nd girl from left; speak twice | Gysahl Greens |
| Dormitory | Caterchipillar | N/A | Bed and Save Point access |
| Parking Lot | Grendel | Upper SeeD | Tent |
| Training Center | T-Rexaur | SeeD | Remedy |
| Library | Grat | Girl with pigtails | Remedy or Mega Phoenix |

{{CALLOUT:relationship|Zell Love Scene #4}} In the Library, put **Zell in the party** before defeating the Library monster and speaking to the girl with pigtails. With Zell present, this scene counts toward the Zell love quest and rewards **Mega Phoenix**. Without Zell, you only receive **Remedy**.

After every area is cleared, return to the main hall. Ride the elevator, go to the end of the hallway for a scene, ride the elevator again, and keep talking to Cid until he sends the party to the basement.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

{{CALLOUT:point|MD Level route}} When the elevator stops, examine the control panel, then the floor panel, and climb down. In the hallway, examine the hatch and climb down again. Junction **Fire** to Elem-Atk; nearly everything down here except Buel, and sometimes Blobra, is weak to Fire. At the valve, use the action command repeatedly. Squall cannot open it alone, so party members join in after failures and make it easier.

Continue through the catwalk route. In the open area with the large column, draw from the **Full-Life Draw Point** on the right side of the screen. On the ladder prompt, choose one of the top two options, watch the scene, examine the nearby control panel, then return down the ladder. Examine the flashing light at the bottom of the screen to open the gate, climb down, use the Save Point, flip the switch, and enable **Mug**. Sleep on ST-Atk and Blind on ST-Def are useful.

**Boss: Oilboyle** (Lv 1-100) - HP: 2,136-15,630 | AP: 10 | EXP: 40+10x
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Esuna, Blind, Cure, Confuse | Lv 20-29: Esuna, Blind, Cura, Confuse | Lv 30-100: Esuna, Curaga, Confuse, Dispel
- Mug: Lv 1-29: 4x Fuel | Lv 30-100: 4x Fuel (89.5%); 2x Orihalcon (10.5%) (25%)
- Drop: Lv 1-29: 8x Wizard Stone | Lv 30-100: 8x Wizard Stone (89.5%); Orihalcon (5.9%); 2x Orihalcon (4.7%) (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Fire
- Resist: Water nullifies
- Status weak: Drain, Eject, Poison (50%), Sleep (80%), Slow (90%), The End

Strategy: Two Oilboyles fight together. Fire-elemental attacks are the cleanest damage route, while Water is useless. Mug if you want **4x Fuel** or, at Lv 30+, a small chance at **2x Orihalcon**. Each Oilboyle also drops **8x Wizard Stone** unless the high-level table rolls Orihalcon. After the fight, climb down, follow the catwalk to the console, and examine it for the Garden movement scenes.

Talk to Cid twice, agree to check on the others, go down the lift, take the elevator, and run to the hallway balcony. After the scenes, return to the hallway. You can now begin the **CC Group quest** opening steps, though it cannot be finished yet, and this is also the next **Novice SeeD Candidates** dialogue window in the Cafeteria.

After more scenes, Squall wakes in the Dormitory. If Rinoa is in the party, Squall gives her an impromptu tour. Either way, head to the front gate for the announcement. Before going to B1, junction **Aero** or another Wind spell to Elem-Atk if possible, protect against **Silence** and **Slow**, and make sure at least one character has **Mug** and **Draw**.

**Boss: NORG Pod** (Lv 1-27) - HP: 2,000 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Cure | Lv 20-27: Cura
- Mug: Nothing
- Drop: Nothing
- Card drop: Nothing
- Weakness: None
- Resist: None
- Status weak: The End

**Boss: Left Orb** (Lv 1-27) - HP: 57-2,865 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Thunder, Life | Lv 20-27: Thundara, Life, Bio
- Mug: 1x Mag Up (69.5%); 2x Mag Up (19.9%); 3x Mag Up (5.9%); 4x Mag Up (4.7%) (25%)
- Drop: Nothing
- Card drop: Nothing
- Weakness: None
- Resist: All elements nullify
- Status weak: Drain

**Boss: Right Orb** (Lv 1-27) - HP: 57-2,865 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Dispel, Confuse, Slow
- Mug: 1x Spr Up (69.5%); 2x Spr Up (19.9%); 3x Spr Up (5.9%); 4x Spr Up (4.7%) (25%)
- Drop: Nothing
- Card drop: Nothing
- Weakness: None
- Resist: All elements nullify
- Status weak: Drain

**Boss: NORG** (Lv 1-27) - HP: 4,400-12,200 | AP: 20 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Shell, Protect, Esuna, Leviathan
- Mug: Circlet (25%)
- Drop: 8x Wizard Stone (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Wind
- Resist: Poison nullifies
- Status weak: Drain, Slow (90%), The End

Strategy: Break the pod first; it has fixed HP and no offense. The orbs cannot be permanently destroyed, but they can be attacked to keep their color from escalating. Mug the Left Orb for optional **Mag Up** and the Right Orb for optional **Spr Up** before opening the main target. When NORG is exposed, **Draw Leviathan** immediately, then mug **Circlet** if desired. NORG is weak to Wind, so Aero/Tornado on Elem-Atk helps. After Leviathan is drawn, he loses Water access; finish with Meltdown and strong attacks.

{{CALLOUT:hint|SeeD written test opportunity}} After the NORG fight, during the post-crisis free period, you can take **SeeD written tests** via Tutorial → Information. Passing tests jumps SeeD Exp to the next 100 milestone. This is an easy rank boost before the Galbadia Garden assault.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Draw Leviathan}} Leviathan is missable here. If you defeat NORG without drawing it, the backup draw is much later from Trauma in Ultimecia Castle. Draw it as soon as NORG is exposed.

{{CALLOUT:refinement|Leviathan GF ability priority}} If you have **4 or more Blobra Cards**, Card Mod them into a **Rune Armlet** and use it to teach **Spr+20%** to Leviathan. If you kept a **Force Armlet** from X-ATM092 and are not saving it for Rinoa's weapon route, it can teach **Spr+40%** and skip straight toward **Spr Bonus**. Set Leviathan to learn **Supt Mag-RF**, then the Spr+% abilities, **Spr Bonus**, **GFRecov Med-RF**, **Boost**, junctions, and remaining abilities.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|GF grouping after Leviathan}} With all GFs available so far, a clean grouping is: **Strength** - Squall plus Zell/Irvine with Quezacotl and Ifrit; **Defense** - Quistis plus Irvine/Zell with Brothers, Carbuncle, and Leviathan; **Magic** - Selphie plus Rinoa with Shiva, Siren, and Diablos. If Carbuncle has not learned **Mag-J**, temporarily move Diablos to the Defense group when a strong Draw user is needed, then prioritize Mag-J on Carbuncle or Leviathan after refinements and stat bonuses.

Before leaving the NORG area, draw from the hidden **Bio Draw Point** beside NORG's remains. Then go to the Infirmary, tell Dr. Kadowaki you need to see Cid, choose each option, head to the entrance area, then go to the 2F balcony and the Library.

{{CALLOUT:relationship|Zell Love Scene #5}} After the NORG crisis, Squall is alone when routed through the Library study area. This scene plays regardless of party and counts toward Zell's love quest finale.

After the Library study scenes, Squall returns to the Dormitory. Leave and head south for the next story scene, then take Balamb Garden toward **Fisherman's Horizon**.

chapters.title: Fisherman's Horizon
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:story|Fisherman's Horizon arrival}} After the Garden crisis, leave the bridge and head down to **2F**. If the early CC Group members have been defeated, another member can be challenged now. To continue the story, go to Balamb Garden's **2F balcony** and exit onto the metal walkway toward Fisherman's Horizon.

After the first FH scene, keep talking to the first local until he leaves. Follow the walkway to the large water tank. If you spoke with the Cafeteria Lady earlier, her son can be found here for extra dialogue.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Master Fisherman's Quest}} On the left side of the water tank, use the **semi-concealed ladder** to reach the Master Fisherman. This starts the **Master Fisherman's Quest**, which is only available for a short window. Complete the first half before advancing the Mayor Dobe story scenes, then finish the quest after the bridge battle.

{{CALLOUT:point|FH exploration checklist}} Use the elevator to reach the lower town.
- Near the Save Point: possible Queen of Cards location and reusable **Regen Draw Point**
- Docks area: **Junk Shop** near the fisherman's pupil; nearby building has an **Inn (100 Gil)** and Item Shop
- Item Shop upstairs: **Timber Maniacs** issue
- Small shack by the abandoned station: examine the **Moomba doll** for a scene and collect another **Timber Maniacs** issue
- Solar farm lower area: **Martine** can hold the **Ifrit Card** if it was lost to Caraway, plus rare cards lost in Galbadia Garden
- Mayor Dobe's house: **Quezacotl Card** from Mayor Dobe; **Irvine Card** from Flo if the Sacred Card has been lost to the Queen of Cards; **invisible Ultima Draw Point** upstairs

Try to spread or preserve the **Open** rule while playing in FH. When ready, head back toward Garden to trigger the next scene, then run up to the railroad tracks and use the Save Point.

{{CALLOUT:point|Battle on the Big Bridge}} Follow the tracks toward the abandoned station. Random encounters can appear along the path. Before the station scene, junction Lightning, Water, or Earth to Elem-Atk and equip Mug if it was removed. At the station, answer however you like, then defeat the **Elite Soldier and 2x G-Soldier** before BGH251F2 arrives.

**Boss: BGH251F2 (FH)** (Lv 1-28) - HP: 5,100-7,800 | AP: 20 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Shell, Protect, Stop
- Mug: Adamantine (12.5%)
- Drop: 8x Running Fire (69.5%); 8x Missile (30.5%) (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Weakness: Lightning x1.5, Water x1.5, Earth x1.5
- Resist: Poison nullifies
- Status weak: Darkness (50%), Slow (90%), The End

Strategy: This BGH251F2 rematch is a valuable item fight. Mug **Adamantine** if possible, then finish it with Lightning, Water, or Earth-elemental attacks. Its guaranteed drop is **Adamantine or 8x Running Fire/Missile** depending on whether Mug succeeds: if Adamantine is stolen, the drop is skipped; without Mug, it drops **8x Running Fire** most of the time or **8x Missile** less often. Blind and Slow still help control it.

After the battle, talk to Rinoa, then Mayor Dobe. Choose **I want him to understand us**, then **Try to continue**. Finish the Master Fisherman's Quest if you started it. On the way back, Irvine joins if he has not already; go to Grease Monkey's house near the station, speak to the officer, leave and re-enter, then speak to the Grease Monkey for **Mega Phoenix**. Return to Balamb Garden afterward.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}
