chapters.title: The Garden Festival
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:story|Garden Festival opening}} Irvine tells Squall to go to the Quad. Head there and **talk to Selphie** to trigger the festival setup scenes.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Concert instrument combinations}} There is no item reward for choosing the right song, but the music plays in several scenes later, so use one of the two complete combinations. Character assignment does not matter; only the instrument set matters.
| Song | Instruments |
| --- | --- |
| Irish Jig | Guitar, Violin, Flute, Tap |
| Slow Dance | Sax, Electric Guitar, Piano, Bass Guitar |

{{CALLOUT:missable|Before confirming Zell's instrument}} While selecting instruments, do not confirm Zell's instrument yet. Run to the **Grease Monkey's house**, speak to the Officer, go outside, re-enter, then talk to the Grease Monkey for **Phoenix Down**. Speak to the Officer for **15x Fast Ammo**, **10x AP Ammo**, and **5x Pulse Ammo**.

{{CALLOUT:relationship|Zell Love Scene #6}} During the short **Irvine solo** window, visit the Library and speak directly to the **Library Girl with Pigtails**. Talking only to the other Library Committee members does not count.

Return to the area by Mayor Dobe's house and finish selecting the band. Control switches back to Squall in the Dormitory. Leave the room for scenes; all dialogue replies are safe, with only tone and extra dialogue changing. Then talk to Irvine twice, run toward Mayor Dobe's house, run right from the stage, and **examine the magazine** to continue.

checkpoints.label: Festival ammo and Phoenix Down
checkpoints.description: Before confirming Zell's instrument, revisit Grease Monkey's house as Irvine for Phoenix Down, 15x Fast Ammo, 10x AP Ammo, and 5x Pulse Ammo.

checkpoints.label: Zell Love Scene #6
checkpoints.description: During Irvine solo control, speak directly to the Library Girl with Pigtails.

chapters.title: Exploring the World
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:story|Garden at sea}} When Squall wakes up, head to the **3F bridge** for scenes. After control returns, Balamb Garden can travel to most of the world except much of the eastern landmass. **Xu suggests visiting Balamb**, but this is a strong optional cleanup window before following the main story there.

{{CALLOUT:hint|UFO note}} From this point onward, a strange UFO encounter can appear. It belongs to a sidequest that cannot be completed yet. If it appears naturally, just note it and continue; active UFO hunting is easier later.

{{CALLOUT:sidequest|Optional route menu}} Useful things to do before continuing to Balamb:
- **CC Group Quest**: the chain can now be completed. The immediate rewards are modest, but finishing the quest before the end of Disc 3 is extremely valuable for rare-card recovery later.
- **Centra Ruins**: obtain **Tonberry King** now; **Odin is best saved** until later if you want to avoid random Zantetsuken interruptions during farming.
- **Cactuar AP farming**: Cactuars can be fought in the southeastern corner of the Kashkabald Desert on Centra. Each gives **20 AP** and little EXP, but they are hard to hit. Squall is reliable because his weapons have 255% Hit; other characters need strong Hit-J support such as Triple.
- **Shumi Village**: visit for rewards and the paid Ultima draw point.
- **Chocobo Forests**: start solving forests if you want Chocobo access and the Chicobo Card chain.
- **Winhill**: revisit for optional items.
- **Timber**: handle the Pet Nametag pickup in the missable reminder below, then view the Timber train-station scene with the girl on the tracks if desired. The hotel ticker changes afterward, but no item is awarded.
- **Dollet or Deling City cleanup**: revisit if any earlier optional cards, magazines, or town tasks were skipped.
- **Novice SeeD Candidates**: speak to the Cafeteria candidates again before the end of Disc 2.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Pet Nametag #2 pickup}} If the Timber journalist was encouraged on Disc 1, return to the square outside the Timber Maniacs building now and speak to him for the **Pet Nametag**. This is the practical cleanup window if you could not detour immediately after Missile Base.

{{CALLOUT:point|Ultima Drawing Session #1}} In **Shumi Village** on Winter Island, the Ultima Draw Point is available for 5,000 Gil per draw. Save first; if the draw count is poor, reload and retry. If you are doing the Shumi quest, draw whenever you pass the entrance and after completing other sidequests, since the walking involved can refill the point.

{{CALLOUT:refinement|Refinement Moment #8}} This is the first open window with **Supt Mag-RF**, access to **Level 7 Cards** through Martine or the CC Group, and several new rare-card/refinement routes. Use the top Cards and Refine tabs for exhaustive recipes, but the high-priority new options are:
- **Rinoa Card** -> 3x **Magic Armlet**
- **Carbuncle Card** -> 3x **Glow Curtain** -> 300x Reflect via Supt Mag-RF
- **Irvine Card** -> 3x **Rocket Engine** -> 150x Triple via Time Mag-RF
- **Gilgamesh Card** -> 10x **Holy War**
- **Tiamat Card** -> 10x Flare Stone -> 10x Flare
- **Red Giant Card** -> 5x Meteor Stone
- **Ultima Weapon Card** -> Ultima Stone
- **Ifrit Card** -> 3x Elem Atk
- **Quezacotl Card** -> 100x Dynamo Stone -> 2,000x Thundaga
- **Imp Card** -> Wizard Stone -> 20x Dispel
- 4x **Blobra Card** -> Rune Armlet -> 40x Shell
- 2x **X-ATM092 Card** -> Turtle Shell -> 30x Protect

When this optional cleanup window is done, continue the main story by taking Garden to **Balamb**.

checkpoints.label: Tonberry
checkpoints.description: Unlock Guardian Force Tonberry from Tonberry King in Centra Ruins during this optional window.

checkpoints.label: Pet Nametag
checkpoints.description: If the Timber journalist was encouraged on Disc 1, return to Timber now and collect the Pet Nametag reward.

checkpoints.label: Novice SeeD Candidates
checkpoints.description: Speak to the Cafeteria candidates before the end of Disc 2.

chapters.title: Return to Balamb
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:story|Balamb occupation}} Take mobile Garden to **Balamb**. Head toward the **car rental place** for the occupation scene, talk to both the hotel owner and his wife, then talk to the entrance guard. Walk away and re-approach the guard for the follow-up scene. **Zell is required** for this town sequence; if he is not already in the party, the game forces him in. **Selphie is available** as an optional party member. Continue into town, enter Zell's house, and go into the room to the right of the entrance.

{{CALLOUT:sidequest|Optional sneak-out and Pandemona Card early}} Balamb normally locks you in until the occupation is resolved, but the **Big Bad Rascal** can open a temporary exit route and also unlock the **Pandemona Card early**. Talk to both the girl and man in Zell's neighbor's house, speak to the Big Bad Rascal twice and Ma Dincht at least once in Zell's house, chat with the hotel guards, then return to Zell's house and talk to the Rascal again. Go back to the town entrance and talk to the guard, leaving the textbox open until the Rascal finishes speaking with the hotel owner and his wife. Return to Zell's house and talk to the Rascal again. Speaking to him once more lets you sneak out; Selphie is forced out of the party while outside Balamb. During this window, the **hotel owner's daughter** next door to Zell's house holds the Pandemona Card.

Go to the Hotel and speak to the guards. You cannot stay there during the occupation, but Zell lets the party rest and save in his room. If Selphie is in the party, **do not side against Selphie** during Zell's room scene or you lose SeeD Rank.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Captain search}} The occupation has three search outcomes. The best route is the **dog method**: return to Zell's house, go to the harbor, speak to the guard with the dog, return to the Dincht residence once smoke appears from the door, talk to Ma Dincht, then follow the dog to identify the Captain for **+100 SeeD Exp**. The alternate route, **boarding the train**, gives **+65 SeeD Exp**. Wandering until the Captain appears at the Hotel works, but gives no SeeD Exp bonus.

{{CALLOUT:point|Boss preparation}} Before meeting the Captain, junction **Bio to Elem-Atk**, **Darkness to ST-Atk**, equip **Mug**, and make sure at least one character has **Draw**. Bio boosts damage against both Raijin and Fujin; Darkness helps suppress physical pressure.

**Boss: Raijin (solo)** (Lv 1-29) - HP: 400-11,600 | AP: 10 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Thunder, Thundara, Shell, Protect
- Mug: 2x Str Up (25%)
- Drop: Str Up (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Poison x1.5
- Resist: Lightning absorbs
- Status weak: Darkness (60%), Drain (40%), Silence (20%), Sleep (60%), Slow (60%), The End

Strategy: Two G-Soldiers assist Raijin. Remove or disable the soldiers first, then focus Raijin. Do not put Lightning on Elemental Attack because he absorbs it. Mug **2x Str Up**, draw Shell/Protect if useful, and lean on Bio/Poison-boosted offense. Raijin can be controlled with Darkness, Sleep, Slow, and Drain support; if only female characters remain standing, his normal physical pressure drops sharply.

Raijin immediately returns with Fujin outside the Hotel.

**Boss: Raijin (with Fujin)** (Lv 1-29) - HP: 400-11,600 | AP: 10 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Thunder, Thundara, Shell, Protect
- Mug: 2x Str Up (25%)
- Drop: Str Up (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Poison x1.5
- Resist: Lightning absorbs
- Status weak: Darkness (60%), Drain (40%), Silence (20%), Sleep (60%), Slow (60%), The End

**Boss: Fujin** (Lv 1-29) - HP: 300-8,700 | AP: 10 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Aero, Cura, Life, Pandemona
- Mug: Megalixir (89.5%) or Hero (10.5%) (25%)
- Drop: Megalixir (89.5%) or Combat King 002 (10.5%) — one always drops
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Poison x1.5
- Resist: Wind absorbs
- Status weak: Darkness (60%), Drain (40%), Sleep (60%), Slow (60%), The End

Strategy: Draw **Pandemona** from Fujin immediately, then decide whether to Mug for Megalixir/Hero before ending the fight. Avoid Wind-elemental attacks because Fujin absorbs Wind. Poison works on both bosses; Darkness, Sleep, Slow, and Drain can soften their pressure. Watch for Sai dropping a character to 1 HP when Fujin is wounded.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Draw Pandemona}} **Draw Pandemona** from Fujin before ending the hotel battle. This is the missable GF for the chapter. Fujin absorbs Wind, so do not use Wind-elemental attacks. She opens with Tornado pressure and can use Sai to drop a character to 1 HP when wounded, so draw Pandemona early and then finish the fight. **Combat King 002** is tied to Fujin's battle reward, while both Raijin appearances drop Str Up.

After the scenes, name Pandemona if it was drawn. When control returns on Balamb Garden's bridge and any Zell-related scenes are done, return to Balamb Town. If the Pandemona Card was not won early, win it from the Hotel owner. If you have the Chicobo Card and are ready to advance that Queen of Cards branch, lose Chicobo to the Queen and make sure she moves to Dollet; the **Chubby Chocobo Card** then becomes available from the man sitting on the bench in Balamb Garden next to the Library.

{{CALLOUT:item|Spd Up from Zell's bed}} In Balamb Town, bring Zell if needed, go to the port, and find the Big Bad Rascal behind the barrels on the right-hand side. Speak to him, watch the scene, then return to Zell's house and check Zell's bed for **Spd Up**.

{{CALLOUT:gf|Pandemona setup}} Set Pandemona to learn **Spd-J**, then **Elem-Defx2**, the Spd+X% abilities, **Boost**, and finally the cleanup abilities. Its main strategic value is Speed junctioning, but the summon is still useful against Wind-weak enemies. If all prior sidequests are complete, a clean GF grouping is: Strength team - Quezacotl, Ifrit, Pandemona; Defense team - Brothers, Carbuncle, Leviathan, Tonberry; Magic team - Shiva, Siren, Diablos.

{{CALLOUT:point|Ultima Drawing Session #2}} Before moving to Trabia, return to Shumi Village and draw Ultima again if the point has recharged. This second paid draw is easy to forget because the story immediately points toward the next Garden.

{{CALLOUT:training|Cactuar AP side trip}} This is an excellent window for Cactuar AP farming. Go to **Kashkabald Desert** on the far west end of Centra, park Garden before the canyon it cannot pass, equip Enc-None, and walk into the desert. In the far southeastern corner, encounters are only Cactuars: **20 AP** and almost no EXP. Squall hits reliably because his weapons have perfect Hit; Selphie also does if she has Strange Vision. Other characters need Triple on Hit-J to connect reliably. Stay until Tonberry has the shop abilities you care about.

{{CALLOUT:hint|Stat-J Scrolls}} With Familiar and access to the Timber Pet Shop, either physically or through Call Shop, you can buy basic **Stat-J Scrolls** for HP, Str, Vit, Mag, and Spr. They cost 10,000 Gil each before Haggle, but can save AP. Only teach a scroll if that GF naturally supports the ability; otherwise unlearn a low-risk command such as Magic, GF, Draw, or Item first because those commands are common and easy to relearn.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

enemies.notes: Two support Raijin in the first hotel fight.

chapters.title: Trabia Garden
chapters.content:
Your next objective is **Trabia Garden**, in the **northeastern part of Trabia Continent**. Use the large world map if the snowy northern continent is hard to read.

{{CALLOUT:story|Trabia Garden ruins}} After the entry scene, **climb up the net-like thing** to get inside the ruined Garden. Talk to the girl near Selphie and choose **Play along**. At the fountain, draw from the **Thundaga Draw Point** first, then hold the walk action, **walk south 5 steps**, then examine the ground for **Weapons Mon Aug**.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Trabia Garden collectibles}} From the fountain, take the northern-right exit to the **graveyard**. The **Timber Maniacs** issue is camouflaged in the back of the graveyard screen. Return to the fountain afterward.

From the fountain, head left. Speak with the students if you want the extra flavor, but make sure to **examine the terminal** before leaving the area. Continue west, speak to the **little girl and boy**, then go two screens west to the **basketball court**. There is a **Save Point** at the north end of this area — save before the memory sequence begins. Try to leave to start the memory sequence.

{{CALLOUT:story|Orphanage memory}} When Squall appears in **phantom form**, go through the **northern door** and talk to the boy. Follow the **stone steps down to the beach**. When control returns, talk to all the kids, leave the room for the next scene, then on the beach speak to **Irvine, Quistis, and Selphie** in order. The others have optional comments. Finally, **approach Rinoa** and form a party when prompted.

The party returns to Balamb Garden. Before continuing, go back to Trabia Garden through the net entrance and challenge Selphie's friend, the girl Selphie spoke with earlier, to win the **Selphie Card**. The card refines into 3x Elem Guard, which teaches El.Def.Jx4.

{{CALLOUT:point|Before the next battle}} All previously available optional activities remain available here. When ready to continue the story, head to the **stone house on Centra continent's western edge**. You cannot enter it yet; approach the familiar nearby Garden to trigger the next major sequence.

checkpoints.label: Weapons Mon Aug
checkpoints.description: At Trabia Garden fountain, stand at the Thundaga Draw Point, walk south 5 steps, then examine the ground.

checkpoints.label: Timber Maniacs
checkpoints.description: Find the camouflaged magazine in the graveyard screen at Trabia Garden.

checkpoints.label: Selphie Card
checkpoints.description: After the orphanage memory scenes, return to Trabia Garden and win it from Selphie's friend.

chapters.title: Battle of the Gardens
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:story|Battle preparations}} Give the Garden orders: **Prepare for the attack**, **Prepare our defenses**, and **Take care of the junior classmen**. Other orders only create extra dialogue. Go down and talk to Quistis, choose a party, and remember that **Zell and Rinoa are unavailable** for this segment. Head to the 2F Hallway; leave junctions alone for now because this party is not fighting yet.

In the 2F Hallway, speak to everyone, then continue to the classroom entrance area. Talk to the pair standing there and the Junior Classman walking toward the classroom. If he enters the classroom, go in and back out to reset him. He gives you a **Cottage**. Afterward, go to the Quad, using the Save Point on the way if needed, then talk to Zell and Rinoa for the next scenes.

{{CALLOUT:point|Defending the Garden}} Control shifts to Zell's team. Set their junctions, run to the end of the Quad, leave the screen after the first scene for the next one, then run to the front gate. When Squall selects two party members, move junctions to this crew. As Squall's team, go to the classroom and defeat the large group of **Paratrooper enemies**. They are tougher than standard G-Soldiers and like status ailments; GF summons work well if Silence is not blocking the command. Speak to the SeeD after the fight, then return to the bridge.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Emergency exit duel}} As Squall, go to the 2F hallway and talk to the Junior Classman. During the scene choose **Look around for another option**, then **emergency exit**. In the rope mini-game, block until the Paratrooper winds up for a punch, quickly kick to stun, then punch or use **Deathblow** if it has appeared. Losing only restarts the mini-game and can increase Squall's starting HP; it is not a Game Over.

When Squall lands, run left for the scene, name the ring if prompted, and answer **Rinoa, let's go**. Junction someone with a high Magic stat, run toward the trees in the distance, draw from the **Aura Draw Point**, then enter Galbadia Garden.

{{CALLOUT:route|Card Key route}} Choose a party inside Galbadia Garden. Go right, right again, and up the stairs. Talk to Raijin and Fujin, take the left-leading hallway, then enter the right-hand door for **Card Key 1**. Return to the entrance Save Point, take the left fork, and unlock the left-hand door. Cross the **hockey rink**; Slappers can appear here but are not especially valuable. The locker room is a dead end, so take the right door, cross the next hallway, collect **Card Key 2**, then go south twice to return to the start.

From the entrance route, go right, right again, climb the stairs to the top, and go through the upper door. Follow the path until the party jumps down. Unlock the nearby shortcut back toward the entrance if you want save access, then continue the other way to the **main hall**.

{{CALLOUT:boss|Cerberus preparation}} Cerberus is optional but strongly worth doing now. Give everyone Draw. Defend against Wind, Earth, and Lightning, or use Float during battle to avoid Quake. Add Darkness and Silence protection, equip Mug, and make sure someone has Magic with Dispel available. Talk to Cerberus to start the fight.

**Boss: Cerberus** (Lv 1-30) - HP: 7,100-10,000 | AP: 30 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Quake, Double | Lv 20-30: Quake, Double, Triple
- Mug: Spd-J Scroll (25%)
- Drop: 8x G-Returner (100%)
- Card drop: Cerberus (100%)
- Weakness: None
- Resist: Lightning absorbs; Wind nullifies
- Status weak: Drain (80%), The End

Strategy: Remove Lightning and Wind from Elemental Attack. Cerberus uses Triple on itself; Dispel it quickly. Quake, Tornado, Silence, Blind, and Berserk are the main problems, so elemental/status defense matters more than raw offense. At Lv 20+, draw Triple; below Lv 20, only Quake and Double are available. Mug the **Spd-J Scroll** before finishing. After the fight, name Cerberus, then set its ability priority to **Spd-J**, **Spr-J**, the ST-Def-J line, **Mag-J**, **ST-Atk-J**, the Spd+X% abilities, **Auto-Haste**, then cleanup.

{{CALLOUT:training|Tri-Face farming}} If you want strong junction magic before Disc 3, fight **Tri-Face** in the center of the main hall where Cerberus stood. The encounter rate is high. High-level Tri-Faces can provide Flare and Pain; Pain is especially strong for status attack/defense. Curse Spikes support Quistis's LV? Death route and, with Siren at Lv 100, the Dark Matter route for Shockwave Pulsar.

Save at the Save Point, leave the main hall through the left hallway, and enter the classroom on the left for **Card Key 3**. You may unlock the bottom-screen door as a shortcut, then return to the main hall. Take the exit opposite the Save Point, climb the stairs near the Reception Room, take the left hallway at the top, and ride the elevator.

{{CALLOUT:boss|Seifer setup}} Before stepping forward, equip **Bio to Elem-Atk**, **Drain to ST-Atk**, and make sure at least one character has Mug. Approach Seifer.

**Boss: Seifer (auditorium)** (Lv 1-31) - HP: 1,300-10,300 | AP: 20 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Fire, Thunder, Dispel, Haste | Lv 20-29: Fira, Thundara, Dispel, Haste | Lv 30-31: Firaga, Thundaga, Dispel, Haste
- Mug: 8x Mega Phoenix (12.5%)
- Drop: 8x Mega-Potion (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Poison x1.5
- Resist: None
- Status weak: Darkness (1%), Drain, Silence (1%), Sleep (1%), The End

Strategy: Fire defense helps against Seifer's magic, and Bio/Drain support work well. Mug **8x Mega Phoenix**, draw Haste/Dispel if useful, then end the fight. Save afterward if you want a clean point before the balcony route.

Return to the stairs near the Reception Room and take the right hallway this time to reach the balcony. Follow it to the far doorway. For the next fight, use the same Seifer setup, add **Sleep in ST-Atk**, protect against Ice, Lightning, Silence, KO, and Curse if possible, and make sure someone has Draw equipped.

**Boss: Seifer (with Edea)** (Lv 1-32) - HP: 1,200-7,400 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Fire, Thunder, Dispel, Haste | Lv 20-29: Fira, Thundara, Dispel, Haste | Lv 30-32: Firaga, Thundaga, Dispel, Haste
- Mug: Hero (89.5%) or Holy War (10.5%) (12.5%)
- Drop: Hero (89.5%) or Holy War (10.5%) (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Poison x1.5
- Resist: None
- Status weak: Drain, Sleep (20%), Slow (1%), The End

Strategy: This Seifer has lower HP than the auditorium version. Mug for Hero or rare Holy War if you want the item roll, then finish him. Edea takes over immediately afterward.

**Boss: Edea** (Lv 1-32) - HP: 500-16,000 | AP: 50 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Blizzard, Demi, Esuna, Alexander | Lv 20-29: Blizzara, Demi, Esuna, Alexander | Lv 30-32: Blizzaga, Demi, Esuna, Alexander
- Mug: Royal Crown (12.5%)
- Drop: Force Armlet (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: None
- Resist: None
- Status weak: Darkness (80%), Drain, Sleep (80%), Slow (90%), The End

Strategy: Draw **Alexander** from Edea before ending the battle. Mug Royal Crown if possible; otherwise Edea drops Force Armlet. Her opening Maelstrom can inflict Curse, which blocks Limit Breaks, so Aura or Pain on status defense is excellent. Death protection prevents her Death spell from ruining the fight, and Sleep creates safe draw windows.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Draw Alexander}} **Draw Alexander** from Edea before ending the battle. Mug **Royal Crown** if possible; otherwise Edea drops **Force Armlet**. Her opening Maelstrom can inflict Curse, which blocks Limit Breaks; Aura or Pain on status defense is excellent, and Death protection prevents her Death spell from ruining the fight. Sleep works well for safe drawing windows. After Edea is defeated, the possession reveal plays and **Disc 2 ends**.

chapters.title: The Aftermath
chapters.content:
After the Disc 2 ending scene, **head to the bridge**. The next story destination is **Edea's House**, near where the enemy Garden was fought, but this is a strong cleanup window before going there.

{{CALLOUT:gf|Alexander setup}} Set the new **Alexander** GF to learn **Med Data**, **Med LV Up**, **High Mag-RF**, **Elem-Defx4**, **Elem-Atk-J**, and **Boost** in that order, followed by remaining abilities. A practical GF grouping after the Disc 2 sidequests is: **Strength team** - Quezacotl, Ifrit, Cerberus, Alexander; **Defense team** - Brothers, Carbuncle, Leviathan, Tonberry; **Magic team** - Shiva, Siren, Diablos, Pandemona.

{{CALLOUT:refinement|Refinement Moment #10}} If you won the **Leviathan Card** from Card Magician Joker, Card Mod it into 3x **Doc's Code** and use one to teach Alexander **Med Data** immediately. If you completed the Chicobo/Queen branch and have the **Chubby Chocobo Card**, Card Mod it into **100x LuvLuv G** for broad GF compatibility boosting.

{{CALLOUT:relationship|Zell Love Scene #9}} Pay one last visit to the Library with Zell in the party. This is the final Library-side setup before completing Zell's sidequest in Balamb.

{{CALLOUT:point|Ultima Drawing Session #4}} Return to Shumi Village for another paid Ultima draw. If your main drawer is near capacity, move some Ultima stock to another character so the drawer has space for up to 15 new spells.

{{CALLOUT:sidequest|Disc 3 cleanup unlocks}} Still in Balamb Garden, go to the Training Center and take the left path to find **Weapons Mon Jul**. This is also the final advancement point for the **Novice SeeD Candidates** quest. With Disc 3 open, the **Dollet Bone Quest** is now worth completing because its rewards improve. Previously available optional quests remain available if unfinished.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

{{CALLOUT:story|Edea's House}} Edea's House is on the **western tip of Centra Continent**. Go inside for a scene, pick up the **Timber Maniacs** issue lying on the ground near the doorway, then follow Cid. Afterward, Edea can be challenged for the **Edea Card**.

{{CALLOUT:card|Centra rules warning}} Edea is an excellent Triple Triad player and uses Centra rules: **Random, Plus, and Same**. Try to spread Open or abolish Random/Plus before seriously chasing the Edea Card. Cid can also hold the **Seifer Card** here if it was not won earlier, but he now plays with Centra rules instead of Balamb rules.

To continue, keep talking to Edea until she stops giving new lines, then attempt to leave for a scene.

{{CALLOUT:perfect|Perfect Game Alert #11}} Talk to Edea until Adel's name appears in yellow text; this unlocks **Esthar (1)** and **Esthar (2)** in Tutorial. Also talk to Cid until he stops giving new lines to receive **Time Compression** and **The Great Hyne**. These entries belong to this Edea's House window.

After the Edea's House scene, Squall returns to Balamb Garden's cockpit. There is nothing new to collect in Garden at this point. Head to the Infirmary, talk to **Kadowaki**, then approach Rinoa to trigger the next sequence.

chapters.title: Trabia Canyon
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:story|Laguna film scene}} In the dream sequence, choose who receives each junction set. **Laguna will always have Squall's** set. **Kiros** and **Ward** can receive whichever other sets you choose; if the **Shumi Village Timber Maniacs** issue was read, Ward is absent. Make sure every available character receives a junction set before continuing.

The sword-fighting mini-game is about timing, not stats. **Block when the dragon attacks** and when it shows a **fast recovery** after your previous strike, because it attacks almost immediately afterward. Attack only during clean openings. If you lose, you can retry.

After a successful film take, choose **H-Hold on a sec** to regain control of Laguna's party before the real fight. Recheck junctions, return to the Save Point, and **Save and Tent** if needed. You can run around the mountainside for **Bite Bug** and **Mesmerize** encounters, but they are not especially useful here.

{{CALLOUT:point|Ruby Dragon draw setup}} The fixed **Ruby Dragon** is the real reason to pause. It is **weak to Ice and Holy**, **absorbs Fire and Wind**, and is vulnerable to **Sleep and Slow**. Ruby Dragon supports Tonberry's **LV Down and LV Up** commands, which lets you control its draw table. At **Lv 45+**, Ruby Dragon carries **Flare and Meteor**; below that range, do not expect Meteor.

{{CALLOUT:warning|High-level Ruby Dragon danger}} Raising Ruby Dragon to **Lv 45+** unlocks powerful attacks, including **Breath** and Meteor. Breath is a huge non-elemental physical attack and can be used against a living **three-person party** with nobody KO'd. If Ward is absent, Breath is not used in this Laguna dream fight, even when the dragon is high level.

For a non-perfect file, the easy drawing method is to **KO a character** while the other two draw, then rotate revives and KOs so everyone gets time to stock magic. This prevents Breath from being available, but it is not valid for a **Perfect Game** file that tracks character KOs.

For a perfect-file route, keep everyone alive. Put Meltdown on Vit, use a strong HP junction such as Regen or better, and bring Protect plus reliable healing. Give someone the GF command and **Summon Cerberus** before raising Ruby Dragon with LV Up, then cast **Protect** on the party and draw while refreshing protection and healing after Breath or Meteor. This method is slower but avoids KO records.

When drawing is complete, use LV Down to **level the dragon back down** before finishing it. This reduces EXP and prevents more high-level Breath pressure. If Meteor draws are painfully slow, it is reasonable to skip heavy stocking here and draw later with better Magic stats.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

checkpoints.label: Ruby Dragon draw stock
checkpoints.description: Optional: use LV Up to raise the fixed Ruby Dragon to Lv 45+ for Flare and Meteor, then level it back down before defeating it.

chapters.title: Picking Up the Trail
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:sidequest|Novice SeeD Candidates reward}} If you triggered every **Novice SeeD Candidates** dialogue window, return to Balamb Garden's **Cafeteria** now to view the final reward scene.

As Squall, go to the cockpit and fly to Edea's House. Speak with Edea to receive the **Sorceress' Letter**. There is nothing else required here unless you want to play Edea or Cid in Triple Triad or manipulate Centra rules. Return to Balamb Garden afterward.

{{CALLOUT:point|White SeeD Ship location}} Use the map reference to find the **White SeeD Ship** near the Centra inlets around Edea's House. To board, simply **drive Balamb Garden into the ship**.

After boarding, watch the scene and speak to people if you want. Head toward the stairs for another scene, then ascend the stairs. The **Timber Maniacs** issue is on the left-hand side of the upper room.

{{CALLOUT:warning|Zone and Girl Next Door}} If **Zone** is present and you still have **Girl Next Door**, you can give it to him for the **Shiva Card** and a **Rename Card**, sell it for **25,000 Gil**, or keep it. Keeping it is recommended for a completion-focused file because Girl Next Door is **one-of-a-kind**; the rewards can be obtained or worked around through other routes.

{{CALLOUT:card|Watts and Angelo Card}} Back downstairs, **Watts** still uses **Galbadia rules**. If the **Angelo Card** was not won earlier, he can still have it here. The Angelo Card is much stronger as a refinement target than as a playing card: Card Mod turns it into **100 Elixirs**.

Go downstairs into the cabin for a scene. Afterward, draw from the **Holy Draw Point** with the character who has the highest Magic stat, then speak to the **leader** for the next story scene.

checkpoints.label: Angelo Card

chapters.title: Journey to the Silent Country
chapters.content:
After the scene, Squall is back aboard Balamb Garden. If anything remains on the **White SeeD Ship**, it can still be revisited; speak to the boy on the ship's starting screen to return to Garden.

{{CALLOUT:sidequest|Preparations for the journey}} This prep is optional but strongly recommended. Return to **Kashkabald Desert** and fight Cactuars until **Alexander learns Med LV Up**. Staying longer is fine, but Med LV Up is the key target.

Collect at least **6 each of Steel Pipe, Remedy+, and Malboro Tentacle** — these are the materials for the **Doomtrain GF** obtained in Esthar (next chapter). **Steel Pipe** can be mugged from **Wendigo** near the first Laguna dream area away from the cliffs, or refined from **Elastoid Cards**. **Remedy+** is simplest: buy **60 Remedies** from a shop and refine them with Med LV Up. **Malboro Tentacle** can come from Malboros in **Grandidi Forest** near **Chocobo Sanctuary**; bring a Chocobo from Roaming Forest and use **Degenerator** to end Malboro fights quickly. For a perfect-game route, **Malboro Cards** are often cleaner because 4 cards refine into 1 Malboro Tentacle.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

{{CALLOUT:story|Setting out for Esthar}} Go to **FH**. After the scene, follow the linear path. When control returns, talk to Rinoa, then speak to Edea. Form a party with **Edea available as a temporary member**, then run into the **dried-up lake** to the east. Seaside Station can be re-entered, but its only real use here is card play with the old man.

{{CALLOUT:route|Great Salt Lake route}} Reform the party on entry and go north. Climb to the upper ledge using the **skull and spine**. On the next screen, check right of the entry point for a **hidden Meteor Draw Point**. Cross the spine to the next screen, use the **Thundaga Draw Point** if desired, continue north, then use the jump prompt at the cliff. On the following screen, the right path leads to a **Save Point**.

{{CALLOUT:boss|Abadon preparation}} Equip **Recover or Revive** if available, or use **Fire- or Holy-elemental magic** on Elemental Attack. Equip **Mug**, protect against **Silence and Confuse**, and bring Draw if the party is high enough to access Flare. Save, then follow the path to fight Abadon.

**Boss: Abadon** (Lv 1-34) - HP: 510-17,010 | AP: 40 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Cure, Esuna, Dispel | Lv 20-29: Cura, Esuna, Dispel | Lv 30-34: Curaga, Esuna, Dispel, Flare
- Mug: Power Wrist (12.5%)
- Drop: 10x Flare Stone (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Undead: Yes
- Gravity: Vulnerable
- Weakness: Fire, Holy
- Resist: None
- Status weak: Darkness (40%)

Strategy: Abadon is undead, so Recover, Revive, healing items, Fire, and Holy are all strong. Mug **Power Wrist** if possible; the guaranteed drop is **10x Flare Stone**. At Lv 30+, draw Flare before ending the fight. Silence and Confuse protection prevent the most annoying control loss.

After the battle, follow the cliff until the background flickers. Examine the edge to open the new route, then follow it into the **mystery building**.

{{CALLOUT:point|Mystery building}} There is very little to collect inside. On the second screen, examine the **console** for story context, then follow the linear path through the scenes.

{{ENCOUNTERS:1}}

chapters.title: The Resistance
chapters.content:
The Esthar flashback prompts you to assign junction sets to **Laguna's gang**. **Squall is always Laguna**, while **Kiros and Ward** can receive whichever remaining sets you prefer.

Start by talking to the **Esthar Soldier near the elevator**, then the soldier near the other door. Address the **Moomba twice**, speak to the man, then speak to the Moomba again. When the Esthar Soldier arrives after the scene, choose **Better check** to review junctions. Kiros and Ward have had their GFs removed, so re-equip GFs and rebuild junctions before choosing **Perfect...!**

The first forced battle is against **Gesper**, an Esthar Soldier, and a **Cyborg Esthar Soldier**. Defeat them normally, then ride the elevator up.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Weapons Mon 1st}} Keep approaching Dr. Odine until the group exits the building. After the scene, save at the Save Point, then go back inside and pick up **Weapons Mon 1st** from the ground before taking the elevator down.

{{CALLOUT:warning|Elastoid Laser Cannon window}} Equip **Mug** before approaching Odine. The next fight includes **Elastoid**, Esthar Soldier, and Cyborg Esthar Soldier. If Elastoid is **Lv 30+**, draw **Meltdown** and mug **Laser Cannon**. Laser Cannon teaches Quistis **Homing Laser**, her second-strongest single-target limit, and this is the **last time you will see one** in this Laguna sequence. If the party can meet Lv 30+ enemies and Mug misses or the level is wrong, reload from the upstairs save.

Follow Odine into the elevator and outside for more scenes. Afterward, defeat two more Esthar Soldiers and try to open the door. Examine the **bench thing** in the middle of the room to continue. When it stops, move to the next room for one final battle against two Cyborg Esthar Soldiers. Examine the **right-hand side of the console**, return to the entrance, and go through the **previously-locked door** for the scene.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

chapters.title: Esthar
chapters.content:
Back as Squall, watch the Esthar arrival scenes. Once control returns, challenge **Odine** to Triple Triad and win the **Ward Card**. Save first if you are mixing Centra rules.

{{CALLOUT:map|Esthar City Map}} Esthar is one of the largest areas in the game. On the city map, **yellow numbered points** are for a later quest, though yellow-circle screens can still be visited now. **White marks** show key locations and **green circles** show other screens. At Yellow Spot #2, paths cross visually but do not intersect; keep moving along the same path you entered from. The Combat King 004 man screen intersects the Odine's Lab path and Yellow Spot #2 path, while the outer spur does not connect to either.

{{CALLOUT:point|City points of interest}} You begin at the Presidential Palace. Odine can still be challenged here for Ward if needed, but only before advancing the plot. The President's office is inaccessible. Outside the **Airstation**, talk to the **Presidential Aide**, then return to the Palace waiting room and speak to the Presidential Secretary; he drops **Occult Fan IV**. At the point marked **Combat King 004**, speak to the soldier so he can hand over **Combat King 004** later. **Odine's Lab** cannot be entered right now. The **Save/Heal** point is a Save Point under a full-party healing device.

{{CALLOUT:shop|Shopping Mall gifts}} The **Shopping Mall** contains **Rinrin's Store**, **Cloud's Shop**, **Johnny's Shop**, **Don Juan's Shop**, **Karen's Store**, and **Cheryl's Store**. Gift table: Rinrin - none; Cloud - Hi-Potion, X-Potion; Johnny - Hi-Potion, Mega-Potion; Don Juan - none; Karen - Hi-Potion, Mega Phoenix; Cheryl - **Rosetta Stone**. Cheryl is never open; keep trying for the Rosetta Stone gift.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Missable — Occult Fan IV}} Collect **Occult Fan IV** from the Presidential Secretary now. This window closes when you advance to Lunar Gate. After that, it is permanently missed.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Missable Item Alert #6}} Visit every Esthar shop except Don Juan's Junk Shop and Cheryl's closed store at least once so they appear in **Call Shop**. If you skip this, several items become unattainable in Disc 4. Also collect **Occult Fan IV** now; its window is brief.

{{CALLOUT:refinement|Refinement Moment #11}} With **Esthar Shop!!!** and Tonberry shop abilities, the Infinite Money Trick reaches its best form: buy Cottages at Esthar shops (with Haggle) and refine them via Recovery Med-RF into Mega-Potions, then sell with Sell-High for a significant profit per loop. Exact per-loop numbers vary based on current Haggle/Sell-High discounts. New high-value refinement routes include Power Wrist/Hypno Crown -> Aura Stone -> Aura, Edea Card -> Royal Crown, Ward Card -> Gaea's Ring, Giant's Ring -> Protect, Force Armlet -> Shell, and 10 Elixirs -> Megalixir.

Esthar shops are useful beyond refinements. The Pet Shop sells **Pet Pals Vol.5** and **Pet Pals Vol.6**; Vol.5 teaches Angelo Search and Vol.6 teaches Rinoa's strongest Combine skill. The Book Store can cover missed Combat King and Weapons Monthly issues, with high-end tomes requiring Familiar. Esthar Shop!!! has the strongest healing-item selection available right now.

{{CALLOUT:sidequest|Acquiring Doomtrain}} Doomtrain can be acquired after reaching Esthar on Disc 3; Occult Fan magazines hint at it but are not required. At **Tears' Point**, collect the **Solomon Ring** from the foot of the large statue. You also need **6 Remedy+**, **6 Steel Pipe**, and **6 Malboro Tentacle**. Steel Pipes come from Wendigo mugs or Elastoid Card Mod; Remedy+ comes from refining 60 Remedies with Med LV Up; Malboro Tentacles come from Malboro drops/mugs, Malboro Card Mod, or later Esthar-region Malboros.

When all ingredients are ready, use the Solomon Ring from the item menu. The ingredients are consumed and Doomtrain joins. Teach Doomtrain **Forbid Med-RF**, **ST-Def-Jx4**, **Elem-Defx4**, **Auto-Shell**, **Boost**, then the remaining abilities.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

{{CALLOUT:point|Great Plains of Esthar}} Esthar world-map encounters vary by area. Around the city, expect **Bite Bug, Gayla, Glacial Eye**. Near the **northern cliffs**, expect Bite Bug, Caterchipillar, Gayla, Glacial Eye, and Hexadragon. On the broad plains, Mesmerize and rare Malboro can appear. Use a car or Enc-None if you are not farming.

Of the available Esthar-area destinations, the important ones right now are **Tears' Point** for Doomtrain and **Lunar Gate** for the story. Go to Lunar Gate when you are done exploring.

{{CALLOUT:story|Lunar Gate}} Enter the building, watch the scene, and follow the woman. Say you are ready, choose any reply, then form a party. **Rinoa will occupy a slot**, so choose one third member; that character does not fight during the upcoming space sequence. Answer **Yeah...** and enter the pod. As Zell, run outside, then choose the party member who will accompany Zell and **Edea**. Junction as needed and return to Esthar.

chapters.title: Siege of Esthar
chapters.content:
Return to Esthar and make sure the active party is junctioned before starting the operation. From the city entrance, go left until you reach the **Save Point**, save, then return to **Odine's Lab**. Ride the platform, enter the lab, and ask any background questions you want before the timer starts.

{{CALLOUT:map|Timed Contact Points}} The city route uses the three yellow spots on the Esthar map. **Yellow Spot #1** works from **15:00-12:00**, **Yellow Spot #2** from **10:00-5:00**, and **Yellow Spot #3** from **3:00-0:00**. Random battles begin throughout the city once the mission starts; **Enc-None** prevents them and makes the first contact point trivial to reach.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Combat King 004}} If you spoke to the soldier before visiting Lunar Gate, stop at the screen marked for the Combat King 004 man and talk to him during the route. He hands over **Combat King 004**, which teaches Zell a powerful finisher.

Reach Yellow Spot #1 if at all possible. Missing it does not end the mission, but boarding at Spot #2 or Spot #3 evicts you before you can collect most Lunatic Pandora treasures. If the timer reaches zero before you board, the interior exploration is skipped entirely.

{{CALLOUT:story|Lunatic Pandora Arrival}} When the Lunatic Pandora appears on the screen, approach it and defeat the **G-Soldier** and **Elite Soldier** within that spot's time window. If the timer slips past 12:00 at Spot #1, 5:00 at Spot #2, or 0:00 at Spot #3 before the battle is cleared, you miss that contact point and must catch the next one.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

{{CALLOUT:point|Lunatic Pandora enemy levels}} Inside Lunatic Pandora, non-Galbadia war-asset encounters are fixed at **Level 1**. That makes it worthwhile to remove Enc-None if you want AP, drops, or mugs with almost no EXP pressure. The Galbadian war assets use their normal level behavior.

After boarding at the first contact point, immediately check the **Meteor Draw Point** to the right, then go up the stairs to the three-elevator room. **Elevator #1** advances toward the end, **Elevator #2** is a dead end, and **Elevator #3** leads to the treasure route; take Elevator #3 first.

At the bottom, go right, descend the stairs, then climb down the ladder. Draw from the **Confuse Draw Point**. If Laguna picked up the Old Key in this area on Disc 1, the nearby door is open and contains a **LuvLuv G**. If Laguna wedged the boulder into the wall, cross the embedded rock to continue left.

{{CALLOUT:treasure|Disc 1 prerequisite treasures}} The three panels only pay off if Laguna handled the excavation site correctly on Disc 1. The right-hand panel contains a **Power Generator**, the central panel hides a **Silence Draw Point**, and the left panel opens access to a **Phoenix Pinion**. The Power Generator teaches Quistis **Ray-Bomb** and is one of the rarest single pickups in the game.

On the next screen, collect **Combat King 005** from the ground. If Laguna found the Old Key near this hall, the far door is open and hides a one-use **Ultima Draw Point**. Its yield is usually low here, so leave it untouched if you want to draw later with stronger Magic stats.

Return to the elevator room, ride up, and take **Elevator #1**. Follow the linear path. Near the exit on the elevator screen, check the cavity opened by Laguna pushing the tunnel-wall rock; it contains the **Spd-J Scroll**. Continue upward until the scene stops the exploration.

{{ENCOUNTERS:1}}

chapters.title: Lunar Base
chapters.content:
After the Lunar Base scenes, speak to **Piet**, the man in white, then move toward Rinoa. Follow Piet when he leaves.

{{CALLOUT:hint|Draw points}} Check the **examination room** for a hidden draw point (Apocalypse). Draw from it before following the story route.

{{CALLOUT:card|Alexander Card setup}} Use the **Save Point** in the examination room, then follow the straightforward route to the **control room**. Before checking the monitor, repeatedly start and quit Triple Triad games with Piet until one of the Lunar rules abolishes. All seven rules start active here; if anything other than **Random or Plus** abolishes, reload your save. Once Random or Plus is gone, save again, return to Piet, and win the **Alexander Card**.

Do **not** Card Mod Alexander yet; it is required for the **Queen of Cards** quest. If this game is too painful right now, Alexander can still be won from Piet later after more rule cleanup, but this is the first opportunity.

After the card game, inspect the **monitor** beside your partner. When Piet directs you to Ellone, save again, go up the stairs outside the room, and follow the simple route toward her. You can speak to the **woman looking out the window** on the way for optional scenes.

{{CALLOUT:missable|Laguna Card}} Do not speak to **Ellone** until you play cards with her. Win the **Laguna Card** first; once you talk to her and advance the story, you **can't play her again** on the Lunar Base. Save before the attempt and reload if needed.

When ready, speak to Ellone, then head for the **med bay**. After the scenes, talk to Rinoa in the med bay, then return to the control room. Wait for Rinoa to arrive; if she takes a long time, follow her route to force the scene. After she leaves, inspect the monitor.

Go upstairs and enter the room near the unconscious man. Examine the **locker** to put on a **space suit**, then follow Rinoa. When the door closes, return to the hallway outside the locker room.

Head back to the control room and speak to Piet, then speak to Ellone. Leave on the right side of the screen, save your progress, talk to Ellone again, and enter the **escape pod**.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Space rescue}} During the space mini-game, you must **catch Rinoa** before the timer ends or it is Game Over. Use Squall's **boosters**, keep Rinoa **centered on your screen**, and continue boosting until you reach her.

checkpoints.label: Alexander Card
checkpoints.description: Win from Piet after abolishing Random or Plus if possible. Do not Card Mod before the Queen of Cards step.

checkpoints.label: Laguna Card
checkpoints.description: Win from Ellone before speaking to her. This is her only Lunar Base card window.

chapters.title: Ragnarok
chapters.content:
When control returns aboard the Ragnarok, use the **Save Point** so you do not have to rewatch the long Lunar Base scenes. Move to the next room for the scene, then continue through the door for another scene.

{{CALLOUT:preparation|Propagator junction setup}} Junction the party before going downstairs. **Take any magic off Edea** and move it to active characters. Set a strong **ST-Atk Junction**; anything except **Confuse** works, including instant KO. Add **Lightning protection** to elemental defense if possible, and cover **Darkness and Silence** on status defense.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

**Boss: Propagator** (Lv 1-42) - HP: 1,100-5,200 | AP: 5 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Thunder, Cure, Esuna, Life | Lv 20-29: Thundara, Cura, Esuna, Life | Lv 30-42: Thundaga, Curaga, Esuna, Life
- Mug: 6x Wizard Stone (69.9%); 8x Wizard Stone (19.5%); 10x Wizard Stone (5.9%); 12x Wizard Stone (4.7%) - overall 50%
- Drop: color-based Spell Stones - overall 75%
- Card drop: Nothing
- Vulnerable: Gravity; LV Up/LV Down
- Status weak: Death, Poison, Petrify, Blind, Silence, Berserk, Zombie, Sleep, Slow, Stop, Doom, Degenerator
- Immune: Confuse

Strategy: Propagators revive unless you defeat the other Propagator of the **same color** immediately before or after it. The fallback condition is killing **25 total Propagators**, but that is slow and not worth the reward, so solve the color-pair route instead.

{{CALLOUT:route|Propagator pair route}} You just defeated a **purple Propagator**. Go through the large door, quickly run to the left door, and defeat the second purple Propagator in the entrance area. From there, go right once and defeat the **red Propagator**. Return to the previous room, go left again, ignore the **green alien**, and go south to defeat the second red one.

For the yellow pair, go north two screens while ignoring the green enemy. Defeat the **yellow Propagator**, optionally read the controls notice, then continue south along the upper level to the second yellow Propagator in front of the **air lock**. Afterward, you can go south and save.

For the final pair, return to the hangar where the first purple battle happened. Use the small door on the right, defeat the green Propagator there, save if desired, then defeat the matching green Propagator in the **elevator room**. Ride the elevator once all pairs are gone to start the long scene.

{{CALLOUT:loot|Propagator drops by color}} **Purple pair**: Shell Stone 69.5%, Protect Stone 19.9%, Aura Stone 5.9%, Death Stone 4.7%. **Green pair**: Death Stone 69.5%, Holy Stone 19.9%, Ultima Stone 10.6%. **Yellow pair**: Flare Stone 69.5%, Meteor Stone 19.9%, Ultima Stone 10.6%. **Red pair**: Aura Stone 69.9%, Death Stone 19.9%, Ultima Stone 10.6%. Every Propagator can also be mugged for Wizard Stones.

{{CALLOUT:route|Late optional route order}} The Ragnarok opens the Disc 3 cleanup route. Do the optional quests in this order if you want the cleanest power curve: **Cactuar Island** for Cactuar and stat Bonus abilities, **Deep Sea Research Center** for Bahamut and Eden, then **Obel Lake Quest** and **PuPu Sidequest** while Enc-None keeps travel painless. The Omega fight itself belongs to the Final Preparations / Ultimecia Castle endgame route, but the setup reminders are here so you can plan supplies early.

{{CALLOUT:gf|Cactuar Island}} Requirements: after Lunar Base. Fly to Cactuar Island near the Kashkabald Desert AP-farming spot. Junction **Water** to Elem-Atk, push Strength as high as possible, enter with low HP if you want Limit Break access, and remove **Mug** because stealing is not useful here.

{{ENCOUNTERS:1}}

**Boss: Jumbo Cactuar** (Lv 1-100) - HP: 33,000-330,000 | AP: 20 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Meltdown, Demi, Tornado
- Mug: 3x/4x Cactus Thorn (not advised)
- Drop: Gaea's Ring
- Card drop: Nothing
- Weakness: Water

Strategy: Treat this as a high-HP Limit Break race. Water-boosted physicals, Aura-backed Renzokuken, Shot, and Duel all work well. After winning, Cactuar joins as a GF. Teach **Luck-J**, **Eva+30%**, **Defend**, **Luck+50%**, **Kamikaze**, then the rest. Put the stat Bonus abilities on the character you intend to level; prioritize **Str Bonus**, **Mag Bonus**, **Vit Bonus**, and **Spr Bonus**, leaving HP Bonus out if only four slots are available.

{{CALLOUT:enemy|Deep Sea Research Center}} The Deep Sea Research Center is in the southwestern ocean and requires the Ragnarok to enter. Enemies here calculate normal levels, then add **+15 levels** after the final level calculation, so equip stat Bonus abilities before exploring. Save outside. In the Laboratory, move only while the core is not glowing; moving during the glow triggers encounters.

Before touching the core, prepare for Ruby Dragons: give someone **Magic** with Blind and Shell, equip Auto-Shell where possible, cover Lightning and Wind in Elem-Def, and protect against Stop or equip Auto-Haste. Equip **Draw** on everyone and remove Mug.

Core answers: choose **"It is not our will to fight"** for the first Ruby Dragon, **"Never"** for the second Ruby Dragon back attack, then choose the **hidden bottom option** for Bahamut.

{{ENCOUNTERS:2}}

**Boss: Bahamut** (Lv 1-100) - HP: 10,800-90,000 | AP: 40 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Flare, Curaga, Full-life, Dispel
- Mug/Drop: Hyper Wrist
- Card drop: Bahamut

Strategy: Keep Shell active, answer the core prompts correctly, and use Blind/Shell support from the setup above. After the fight, Bahamut joins as a GF. Teach **Boost**, **Mug**, **Auto-Protect**, and **Rare Item**, then everything else. Rare Item is not a general upgrade; it shifts drops toward the second slot and can make fourth-slot items impossible.

{{CALLOUT:route|Deep Sea Deposit}} After Bahamut, the core room gains a save point and random encounters. Climb down the vine into the steam-unit puzzle. The area has two practical paths: the **fixed encounter path** with Zell, which is better for Perfect Game farming and repeatable fixed enemies, and the more direct final-boss path, which spends more steam and opens the Steam Room but skips the fixed Deep Sea Deposit farming route. You need **10 steam units** after the final area to engage the boss at the bottom.

The fixed encounter path is valuable because Deep Sea Deposit screens can be re-entered to farm fixed enemies. Tri-Face is the standout target for Curse Spikes and Dark Matter routing; other useful fixed encounters include Ruby Dragon, Behemoth, Iron Giant, and Imp/Grendel depending on what your inventory still needs. Use the Bestiary and Items tabs for level-banded draw, mug, and drop tables instead of duplicating every farming table here.

{{ENCOUNTERS:3}}

**Boss: Ultima Weapon** (Lv 1-100) - HP: 51,100-160,000 | AP: 100 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Regen, Dispel, Ultima, **Eden**
- Mug: Three Stars
- Drop: 100x Ultima Stone
- Card drop: Eden

Strategy: Raise Speed, use Auto-Haste / Auto-Shell / Auto-Protect if available, and cover Earth damage. Keep Draw on everyone and **draw Eden before killing Ultima Weapon**; Tiamat in Ultimecia Castle is the only later fallback. Light Pillar is the danger spike, so keep the party healthy and use Meltdown plus Limit Breaks for damage. If you want the fixed Tri-Face farming route later, leave Ultima Weapon alive until that farming is complete.

{{CALLOUT:sidequest|Obel Lake Quest}} Equip Enc-None. At Obel Lake near Timber, hum at the small peninsula until the shadow asks you to find **Mr. Monkey**. Mr. Monkey is in the forest near Dollet; find him, throw a rock, sing, then return to Obel Lake. Collect the required rock clues, skip rocks until one skips "many many times," then throw rocks at Mr. Monkey until he gives the final rock.

Assemble the rocks to point to **Mordred Plains** north of Esthar. Red rocks lie: do the opposite of what they say. When a red rock says the treasure is not here, examine the same spot again for **Three Stars**. Then fly to **Minde Island** southwest of Esthar and examine the pictured location for a **Luck-J Scroll**.

{{CALLOUT:sidequest|PuPu Sidequest}} Enc-None works for UFO sightings. Visit the four sighting regions: Winhill Bluffs, Mandy Beach, Heath Peninsula, and Kashkabald Desert. Then land on the canyon top near Chocobo Sanctuary in Grandidi Forest and fight the UFO? enemy until it falls for an **Aegis Amulet**.

Bring at least **5 Elixirs** and the Item command, then run around the Balamb crater edge until PuPu appears. Give PuPu five Elixirs for the one-of-a-kind **PuPu Card**. Killing PuPu gives an Accelerator, and Devour gives Speed +1 to the Devourer, but neither reward is unique; the card is the completion route.

{{CALLOUT:warning|Omega handoff}} Omega Weapon is in Ultimecia Castle, not the open world. Before going there, stock Megalixirs, Phoenix Downs or Life/Full-Life, Death for ST-Def, Meltdown access, Aura or Aura Stones, and Defend users. The exact bell route and battle plan are handled in Final Preparations so this page stays focused on Ragnarok-unlocked cleanup.

checkpoints.label: PuPu Card

enemies.name: Propagator
enemies.notes: Eight total, two per color. Defeat same-color pairs consecutively to prevent respawns.

enemies.name: Jumbo Cactuar
enemies.notes: Remove Mug; Water on Elem-Atk and Limit Breaks are the clean plan.

enemies.name: Ruby Dragon
enemies.notes: Two forced prompt battles before Bahamut; the second is a back attack.

enemies.name: Bahamut
enemies.notes: Answer the hidden bottom option on the third prompt.

enemies.name: Ultima Weapon

chapters.title: Back on Earth
chapters.content:
After the scenes, **Rinoa will be unavailable**. Go back inside the Ragnarok, enter the **cabin** to the **left of the elevator**, then try to leave.

{{CALLOUT:map|Sorceress Memorial Location}} Fly to the **Sorceress Memorial** on the **peninsula east of Esthar**. Go inside as far as the game allows for the scenes. Afterward, **Rinoa returns** to the crew and you can set any party you like as long as Squall remains in it.

{{CALLOUT:sidequest|Aerial Exploration}} This optional window is **strongly recommended** before continuing the story. Use the **Sidequest & Completion Index** for the newly opened quests instead of duplicating their full routes here. Do not refine the **Alexander or Doomtrain Cards** until their **Queen of Cards** steps are finished.

{{CALLOUT:perfect-game|Perfect Game pacing}} If you are playing a Perfect Game route, do the fighting and collection you need in this window, then keep the story moving. **Disc 4** unlocks several important refinements if you are holding off on **Eden**. The main exception is the **fixed Elnoyle encounter** in Esthar, which is the easiest source of **Energy Crystals**; Elnoyle is extremely rare later and card farming takes much longer.

{{CALLOUT:story|The Promise}} Go to **Edea's House**, follow Angelo, then **speak to Rinoa** for the promise scene. Return to the Ragnarok afterward.

checkpoints.label: Rinoa returns
checkpoints.description: Visit the Sorceress Memorial on the peninsula east of Esthar to recover Rinoa.

checkpoints.label: Aerial exploration window
checkpoints.description: Use the sidequest index for optional quests opened by the Ragnarok; avoid refining Alexander or Doomtrain before Queen of Cards steps.

checkpoints.label: The Promise
checkpoints.description: Visit Edea's House, follow Angelo, and speak to Rinoa.

chapters.title: The Final Mission
chapters.content:
For the first time in a long while, it is **mission time**.

{{CALLOUT:story|The Briefing}} Head to **Esthar's Presidential Palace**. There are **random encounters in Esthar City**, but landing directly at the **Airstation** saves time. Enter the Palace, follow the hallway past the waiting room, and enter the section that was previously ignored.

Speak to the **President**. Ask any background questions you want, then ask about the **mission to defeat Ultimecia**. Speak to the President again and answer yes to advance the dialogue. After the scenes, return to the cabin and challenge the President to Triple Triad for the **Squall Card**.

You can still complete earlier aerial exploration before committing. When ready, **fly the Ragnarok into the Lunatic Pandora** for the final Disc 3 mission.

{{CALLOUT:missable|End of Disc 3 point of no return}} Before flying the Ragnarok into the Lunatic Pandora, finish any remaining Disc 3 world cleanup: Obel Lake, PuPu, CC Group, Shumi Village, Chocobo Forests, Doomtrain/Solomon Ring, Esthar shop registration, rare-card setup, and any town errands. After Time Compression, towns and most world locations become inaccessible.

{{CALLOUT:preparation|Storming setup}} Exit the Ragnarok by taking the cockpit elevator and using the right-hand door. Before running forward, set **Poison-elemental Magic** on Elemental Attack, use **Sleep or Darkness** on Status Attack, defend **Wind and Lightning**, and cover statuses in this order: **Confuse, Darkness, Silence, Slow, and Poison**. Remove Mug before the first boss pair.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

**Boss: Fujin** (Lv 1-44) - HP: 5,300-18,200 | AP: 8 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Aero, Cure, Life | Lv 20-29: Aero, Cura, Life | Lv 30-44: Aero, Curaga, Full-Life, Tornado
- Mug: 2x Megalixir (25%)
- Drop: Megalixir (100%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Weakness: Poison x1.5
- Resist: Wind absorbs
- Vulnerable: Gravity; Darkness (40%), Drain (70%), Sleep (80%), Slow (90%), The End

**Boss: Raijin** (Lv 1-44) - HP: 5,400-22,600 | AP: 12 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Thunder, Protect, Shell | Lv 20-29: Thunder, Thundara, Shell, Protect | Lv 30-44: Thundara, Thundaga, Shell, Protect
- Mug: Power Wrist (25%)
- Drop: 3x Str Up (69.5%); 4x Str Up (19.9%); 5x Str Up (5.9%); 6x Str Up (4.7%)
- Card drop: Nothing
- Weakness: Poison x1.5
- Resist: Lightning absorbs
- Vulnerable: Gravity; Darkness (40%), Drain (70%), Sleep (80%), Slow (90%), The End

Strategy: Fujin and Raijin are both vulnerable to Sleep and Slow. Bio or a Poison-element junction helps both. Decide whether you value Raijin's **Power Wrist** mug more than his guaranteed Str Up drop table; remove Mug if you want the Str Up result. Fujin is a clean Megalixir source either way. After the fight, **Biggs and Wedge** appear but do not fight; talk to them if you want the scene, use the **Save Point**, then enter the doorway.

{{CALLOUT:point|Level 1 interior}} **All enemies here except bosses will be Level 1**. The next room forks; stay on the same path and ride the elevator to the elevator room. The stairway still has a **Meteor Draw Point**. If you missed the conditional Lunatic Pandora treasures from the earlier visit, use the Siege of Esthar route before continuing onward.

Ride **Elevator #1**, follow the path north, and save. Equip Lightning-elemental magic to Elemental Attack and re-equip Mug if you want Laser Cannons or probe stat-ups. Continue to fight the guardian robot.

**Boss: Mobile Type 8** (Lv 1-41) - HP: 30,300-42,300 | AP: 40 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Fire, Blizzard, Thunder, Flare | Lv 20-29: Fira, Blizzara, Thundara, Flare | Lv 30-41: Firaga, Blizzaga, Thundaga, Flare
- Mug: Laser Cannon (69.5%); 2x Laser Cannon (19.9%); 3x Laser Cannon (5.9%); 4x Laser Cannon (4.7%) - overall 12.5%
- Drop: Laser Cannon (69.5%); 2x Laser Cannon (19.9%); 3x Laser Cannon (5.9%); 4x Laser Cannon (4.7%) - 100%
- Card drop: Nothing
- Weakness: Lightning
- Vulnerable: Gravity; The End

**Boss: Left Probe** (Lv 1-41) - HP: 5,100-9,100 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Cure, Esuna | Lv 20-29: Cura, Esuna | Lv 30-41: Curaga, Esuna
- Mug: 2x Vit Up (89.5%); 4x Vit Up (10.5%) - overall 12.5%
- Drop: Nothing

**Boss: Right Probe** (Lv 1-41) - HP: 5,100-9,100 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Fire, Blizzard, Thunder | Lv 20-29: Fira, Blizzara, Thundara | Lv 30-41: Firaga, Blizzaga, Thundaga
- Mug: 2x Str Up (89.5%); 4x Str Up (10.5%) - overall 12.5%
- Drop: Nothing

Strategy: Lightning is the key offensive element. Mug Mobile Type 8 for **Laser Cannon** if you want more Pulse Ammo routes, and mug the probes only if you are willing to spend turns fishing for the 12.5% chance at **Vit Up** or **Str Up**. After the fight, go back and save; use **another slot** in case you want to return to this point.

Switch the Lightning spell in Elemental Attack to **Bio** and remove Mug unless you specifically want Seifer's Hero/Holy War table. Continue past Mobile Type 8's room for the final Disc 3 duel.

**Boss: Seifer** (Lv 1-45) - HP: 3,700-34,500 | AP: 40 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Fire, Blizzard, Thunder | Lv 20-29: Fira, Blizzara, Thundara | Lv 30-45: Firaga, Blizzaga, Thundaga, Aura
- Mug: Hero (89.5%); Holy War (10.5%) - overall 12.5%
- Drop: Hero (89.5%); Holy War (10.5%) - 100%
- Card drop: Nothing
- Weakness: Poison x1.5
- Vulnerable: Drain, The End

Strategy: At Lv 30+, Seifer draws **Aura**, making this a valuable stock-up point if your party level allows it. Poison-elemental damage is effective. His reward is **Hero or Holy War** from both Mug and drop. Seifer can end the Odin storyline if Odin is present, replacing him with Gilgamesh after the scene; keep Odin out if you want to avoid that. After Seifer falls, watch the scenes and reform the party if Rinoa was active. This is the transition into the next disc sequence.

checkpoints.label: Squall Card

checkpoints.label: Conditional Lunatic Pandora treasures

checkpoints.label: Aura from Seifer
checkpoints.description: At Lv 30+, the final Disc 3 Seifer fight is a rare chance to draw Aura directly from an enemy.

checkpoints.label: End of Disc 3
checkpoints.description: Before flying into Lunatic Pandora, finish Disc 3 world cleanup. Time Compression makes towns and most world locations inaccessible.
