chapters.title: The Awakening
chapters.content:
When control returns, make sure everyone is **Junctioned properly**, especially if the game forced you to use **changed party members** after Seifer.

Return to **Type 8's room**. Climb the **crane-like structure** and follow it around for a scene. You can still visit other areas in **Lunatic Pandora**, but you **won't be able to leave**.

{{CALLOUT:preparation|Adel setup}} Give someone **Mug**, push your stats as high as possible, and prioritize **defending Earth and Holy** in Elemental Defense. Avoid party-wide attacks during the fight because Rinoa is attached to Adel.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

**Boss: Adel** (Lv 1-45) - HP: 3,700-34,500 | AP: 40 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Fire, Blizzard, Thunder | Lv 20-29: Fira, Blizzara, Thundara | Lv 30-45: Firaga, Blizzaga, Thundaga
- Mug: Samantha Soul (6.25%)
- Drop: Nothing
- Card drop: Nothing
- Vulnerable: The End

**Boss: Rinoa** (Lv 1-45) - HP: 6,021-9,036 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Esuna, Dispel, Regen
- Mug: 8x Megalixir (25%)
- Drop: Nothing
- Devour: All systems go!
- Card drop: Nothing
- Vulnerable: Darkness (80%), Doom (80%), Drain, Poison (80%), Silence (80%), The End

Strategy: **Adel periodically drains HP from Rinoa** to recover his own HP — keep Regen active on Rinoa and use single-target attacks only to avoid hitting her. Mug Adel for **Samantha Soul** if you want it, but do not let the fight drag so long that Rinoa is critically low. Mugging Rinoa gives **8x Megalixir** but is not recommended unless your healing is stable. Draw **Regen** from Rinoa and keep it active on her. Use single-target attacks and Limit Break choices that will not hit Rinoa. After Adel falls, enjoy the scenes.

checkpoints.label: Samantha Soul
checkpoints.description: Optional 6.25% mug from Adel.

checkpoints.label: 8x Megalixir
checkpoints.description: Optional 25% mug from Rinoa during the Adel battle; only attempt if healing is stable.

enemies.name: Adel
enemies.notes: Avoid party-wide attacks because Rinoa is attached.

enemies.name: Rinoa
enemies.notes: Draw Regen and keep her alive; optional 8x Megalixir mug.

chapters.title: Commencement Room
chapters.content:
When the dust, water, matter, or time settles, you begin in a room with a **Save Point**. Try to use it once to spawn several more save points; every new one is **fake**, while the original remains real. **Save and Tent** as needed.

{{CALLOUT:preparation|Element defense check}} The most important preparation here is **Elem-Def**. Cover as many elements as possible; full absorption is not realistic without sacrificing too much elsewhere.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

Leave the room to begin a sequence of **"Sorceress" enemies**. They are fought one at a time. They drop **Flare Stones**, **Holy Stones**, **Meteor Stones**, and **Ultima Stones** from most common to rarest. Mugging can yield a Meteor Stone or Ultima Stone.

{{CALLOUT:enemy|Sorceress sequence}} There are three types. **type A** covers the first six and uses one spell each in order: **Fire, Thunder, Fira, Blizzara, Thundara, and Aero**. Type B covers #7-#10: #7 casts **Double** then **Thundaga**, #8 uses **Quake and Meltdown** so remove Vit 0 if it lands, #9 casts **Meteor every turn**, and #10 uses Double then repeated **Flare**.

The **final "Sorceress"** is the hardest. She counts down to **Ultima**, and when not counting she counters damage with **Creepy Touch**, an automatic critical attack. Keep the party healed and end the fight quickly.

After the scene, you appear at **Edea's House(?)**. Run through the right-hand door, then out the back door for another scene. You cannot exit to the world map from here.

Along the linear path is a **Triple Draw Point**. Use it if needed. The **portals to the left** of the **large chain** lead to the world map, but unless Eden is already secured, it is better to continue. Use the Save Point, then run up the steps toward the castle.

checkpoints.label: Real Save Point
checkpoints.description: The original Save Point is real; the extra spawned ones are fake.

checkpoints.label: Triple Draw Point
checkpoints.description: Draw Triple on the path after Edea's House(?), before the castle approach.

enemies.name: "Sorceress" A
enemies.notes: First six enemies. Cast one spell each in order: Fire, Thunder, Fira, Blizzara, Thundara, Aero. Drops Flare/Holy/Meteor/Ultima Stones.

enemies.name: "Sorceress" B
enemies.notes: #7-#10. Uses Double + Thundaga, Quake + Meltdown, Meteor every turn, and Double + Flare patterns.

enemies.name: "Sorceress" C
enemies.notes: Final enemy. Counts down to Ultima and counters damage with Creepy Touch.

chapters.title: Ultimecia's Castle
chapters.content:
Inside **Ultimecia's Castle**, abilities will be sealed on entry. The **Attack Command, Junctions, Character Abilities, and Party Abilities** still work, and GFs can still learn abilities while inside. Green Party Switch Points allow party switching; this is a **two-party dungeon** even though the critical path can be done mostly with one team.

{{ENCOUNTERS:0}}

{{CALLOUT:enemy|Castle random encounters}} Most **regular enemies** can appear somewhere in the castle, and their **levels will be completely random** instead of following the party average. Use the encounter list as a local checklist, but farm specific enemies elsewhere when possible.

{{CALLOUT:enemy|First guardian — Sphinxaur/Sphinxara}}

**Boss: Sphinxaur** (Lv 1-50) — HP: 10,000 | AP: 0
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Fire, Thunder, Cure | Lv 20-29: Fira, Thundara, Cura | Lv 30-50: Firaga, Thundaga, Curaga
- Mug: Elixir (25%)
- Drop: Nothing
- Weakness: Holy

**Boss: Sphinxara** (Lv 1-100) — HP: 10,000 | AP: 30
- Draw: Lv 1-19: Fire, Thunder, Blizzard, Cure | Lv 20-29: Fira, Thundara, Blizzara, Cura | Lv 30-50: Firaga, Thundaga, Blizzaga, Curaga
- Mug: Nothing (0%)
- Drop: Megalixir (100%)
- Weakness: Holy

Strategy: With most abilities sealed, attack directly. **Mug Elixir** from Sphinxaur. Sphinxara uses Doom (countdown kill), Slow, Sleep, Zombie, and summons Jelleye → Forbidden → Tri-Face in sequence. If everyone is under Zombie status, Sphinxara only uses Doom (which misses against Zombie targets). When Doom is on a character, Sphinxara may cast Zombie on that character — this removes the Doom countdown. Finish quickly with physical attacks. Holy junctioned to Elemental Attack deals double damage (Sphinxaur/Sphinxara have Holy ×2 weakness). After this fight, **unlock Draw** — this is the priority since it allows drawing Eden from Tiamat and recovering any missed GFs. Most castle bosses hold missed GFs on their Draw lists; this is the **last shot at drawing** Siren, Leviathan, Pandemona, Carbuncle, Cerberus, Alexander, and Eden if they were missed earlier. **Sphinxara Card refine chain**: Sphinxara Card (win by Card commanding Sphinxara) → Card Mod → **1× G-Mega-Potion** per card.

Proceed through the door behind Sphinxaur. The chandelier drops — inspect the floor hatch and descend. Junction **Thundaga to Elem-Def-J** for all three party members before going down.

**Boss: Tri-Point** (Lv 1-51) — HP: 2,400-22,400 | AP: 30
- Draw: Haste, Tornado, Blind, Siren
- Weakness: Fire ×4, Ice ×4

Strategy: Tri-Point counterattacks with **Mega Spark** (ignores Spr) whenever it is hit with the wrong element. With Thunder class magic on El.Def-J, Mega Spark heals the party. Tri-Point alternates between Fire and Ice weaknesses — attacking with the current weakness does NOT trigger a counter, and shifts the weakness to the other element. Exploit this: alternate members with Fire and Ice on El.Att-J. When Tri-Point uses **Scan** on a party member, immediately cast **Protect** on that character — the next attack is Onrush, which ignores Vit and will KO. Draw **Siren** here if it was missed. Tri-Point drops **Rocket Engine** — these refine into Spd Up (Forbid Med-RF) and eventually Spd-J Scrolls via GFAbl Med-RF. **Unlock Magic** after this fight.

{{CALLOUT:refinement|Tri-Point Card refine chain}} The Tri-Point Card (Lv 7 boss card, won from card players or the CC Group) mods into **40× Jet Engine** via Card Mod (requires 40 cards per batch — the most costly ratio in the game, but Jet Engines are unique). Each **Jet Engine** teaches a GF **Spd+40%** when used directly (via GFAbl Med-RF) — this is one of only two ways to grant Spd+40% to a GF, alongside the Irvine Card chain. Alternatively, Jet Engines refine via **Forbid Med-RF** (Doomtrain, Lv 100 required) into **Spd Up** stat items, the rarest method for permanently increasing base Speed. Since Jet Engines appear on the Angelo Search uncommon table (~1.39%), patient farming is a viable alternative to card modding. The Tri-Point Card itself is a strong Lv 7 playing card (8/8/5/2 + Thunder element) useful in high-rule regions.

**Unlock Draw** (if not already done) or **Magic** (if still sealed). Return to the main hall, go left down the hallway, then right to enter the Art Gallery. Examine all paintings. The clock reads VIII, VI, and IIII — find those numerals in the paintings and enter **VIVIDARIUM** · **INTERVIGILIUM** · **VIATOR** into the large left painting. Trauma appears from behind.

**Boss: Trauma** (Lv 1-56) — HP: 5,555-34,114 | AP: 30
- Draw: Meltdown, Leviathan
- Weakness: Wind ×2

**Boss: Droma** (Lv 1-56) — HP: 1,010-3,128 | AP: 30
- Draw: Esuna, Dispel
- Mug: 2x Meteor Stone (25%)
- Drop: Nothing

Strategy: Open with **Meltdown** on Trauma — draw it directly from Trauma for a free cast. Trauma creates two Dromas at the start. Keep the Dromas alive — if both Dromas are killed, Trauma uses the devastating **Mega Pulse Cannon** (ignores Spr and Shell), then creates two new ones. Trauma will eventually Drain its own Dromas to recover HP. Simply keep attacking Trauma; let it consume the Dromas itself. Draw **Leviathan** if missed. **Mug 2× Meteor Stone** from each Droma. Trauma drops **Elem Atk** items (teach El.Att-J to a GF). **Unlock Item**, or **Limit Break** if Item is already open.

**Unlock Limit Break** next if it is still sealed. From the Art Gallery, go north twice and down the stairs. Take the upper-left exit, grab the key in the prison cell, then fight Red Giant.

**Boss: Red Giant** (Lv 1-52) — HP: 30,000 | AP: 30
- Draw: Demi, Pandemona

Strategy: Red Giant has **no elemental weaknesses** (all elements ×1) — its defenses come from extremely high Vitality (255) and Spirit (255), not elemental resistance. Cast **Meltdown** immediately — Red Giant has fixed HP at 30,000 regardless of level. Cast Blind to reduce its physical attacks (80% Darkness success rate). **Gravity and Demi work against Red Giant** despite its imposing appearance — draw and cast Demi from Red Giant itself to deal 25% current HP per cast, making it a useful self-damaging strategy early in the fight when raw damage is limited. When its HP falls below 9,000 it loses its sword and switches to unarmed punches. Draw **Pandemona** if missed. Red Giant drops **Diamond Armor** — useful crafting material. **Unlock Limit Break** after this fight.

**Unlock Item.** Return to the Main Hall. Switch to the secondary party and move them left through the hall to the chandelier room — lower the lever. Switch back to the primary party and go upstairs through the doors, over the chandelier, to the balcony.

**Boss: Krysta** (Lv 1-55) — HP: 5,200-16,000 | AP: 30
- Draw: Holy, Carbuncle

Strategy: Krysta has extremely high Vitality — **Meltdown is mandatory**. Additionally, Krysta has **zero Spirit** — magic damage hits for full maximum value without any Spirit reduction, making magic attacks superior to physical hits if Meltdown has already been applied. **Elemental notes**: Krysta is **completely immune to Ice** and **Earth magic misses entirely** (not just reduced — Earth-element spells simply fail to connect). Krysta counters any attack with a (Counter rocket). Stay at high HP before the final blow — when Krysta is defeated, it uses **Ultima** as a desperate last strike. Draw **Carbuncle** if missed. Krysta drops **Elem Guard** items (teach El.Def-Jx4 to a GF — very valuable). **Unlock Item** after this fight.

**Unlock Command Ability.** Navigate through the Chapel (restoring Mug, Defend, and Draw is now the priority). Head upstairs, across the bridge — **walk, do not run** across this bridge or the Armory Key will be knocked off and fall into the water below. Hold Triangle to keep balance. If the key does fall, it flows downstream — find it on the right side. Use the Armory Key to unlock the door before the Flood Gate room. Fight Gargantua.

**Boss: Gargantua** (Lv 1-54) — HP: 10,100-15,400 | AP: 30
- Draw: Bio, Quake, Reflect, Cerberus
- Drop: Magic Armlet (100%)

Strategy: Starts as Vysage, Lefty, and Righty — defeat them to trigger the true Gargantua. **Do not use physical attacks** against Gargantua — every physical hit triggers **Counter Twist**, which always deals critical damage. Use magic exclusively. Gargantua's **Evil-Eye** inflicts Slow and Curse on a single target — keep Esuna or Remedy+ on hand; junctioning Pain and Slow to St.Def-J blocks both. Quake targets all party members (Earth on El.Def-J to absorb). Cast Meltdown, then Aura on Squall and unleash Renzokuken — Gargantua falls in one Limit Break chain. **Zombie trick**: Gargantua is also weak to Zombie status — if Zombie can be inflicted, a single Elixir then kills it instantly (Elixir heals a Zombie-status target to 0 HP, bypassing its physical counter entirely). Draw **Cerberus** if missed (Cerberus can also be drawn from Gargantua). Also draw **Reflect** from Gargantua — this is one of the few available sources of Reflect magic at this point in the game. Gargantua always drops **Magic Armlet** items (teaches St.Def-Jx4 to a GF). **Unlock Command Ability** after this fight.

**Unlock Resurrection.** Head to the fountain area below and check the **left side of the fountain** for the **Treasure Vault Key**. Go back to the Main Hall, upstairs, west, down the stairs, and find the difficult-to-see door on the left. Open the puzzle — the goal is to have all four coffins open simultaneously. The simplest sequence: **touch the first, second, third, and fourth in order** (each touch toggles nearby coffins; this sequence leaves all four open). If that fails (order can differ based on puzzle state), try touching them in any order that leaves all four open simultaneously.

**Boss: Catoblepas** (Lv 1-57) — HP: 10,500-38,500 | AP: 30
- Draw: Meteor, Alexander
- Weakness: Earth ×2, Water ×2

Strategy: Catoblepas is weak to **Earth (×2) and Water (×2)** — junction Quake or Water to El.Att-J. **Catoblepas absorbs Lightning** — it is healed by any Thunder-class magic, so remove Lightning from El.Att-J entirely for this fight. Catoblepas has low Vitality and low Spirit — both physical and magical damage land cleanly. Apply Blind to greatly reduce its claw and Deadly Horn physical attacks (60% Darkness success rate). Keep Thunder class magic on El.Def-J to handle Thundaga. Catoblepas uses **Thunder Summon** (area Thunder attack that ignores Spr) after it has used Thundaga three times — the damage is still mitigated by junctioning Thundaga/Blizzaga to El.Def-J. Before landing the killing blow, ensure Shell is active on all party members — Catoblepas uses **Meteor** as a final desperate strike. Draw **Alexander** if missed. Catoblepas always drops **Status Atk** items (teach St.Att-J to a GF). **Catoblepas Card refine chain**: Catoblepas Card → Card Mod → **1× Rename Card** — Rename Cards allow renaming any GF (the only in-game method to change a GF's name). **Unlock Resurrection** after this fight.

{{CALLOUT:point|Rosetta Stone}} Go to the left elevator area and use the correct party combination to raise the chandelier platform (Quistis/Selphie/Irvine left side, Squall/Zell/Rinoa right side — or other valid combos). Obtain the **Floodgate Key**. Return to the Flood Gate area (near Red Giant/Gargantua), unlock the lever, and drain the water. In the Chapel, **press ALL organ buttons simultaneously** and hold for a few seconds. The key is that the note must **cut off cleanly** — release all buttons at exactly the same time so the chord ends sharply rather than trailing. If the spikes do not lower, the timing was off; retry until you hear the brief sound effect confirming success. Then go east in the waterways to find the **Rosetta Stone** chest.

Now continue to the clock tower. Go upstairs from the Chapel, cross the bridge, through the door, climb the clock tower stairs, and use the bell to jump from the left side to the right side. On the balcony, fight Tiamat.

**Boss: Tiamat** (Lv 1-58) — HP: 21,200-89,600 | AP: 30
- Draw: Flare, Eden

Strategy: Tiamat spends several turns counting down before unleashing **Dark Flare** — an area attack that ignores Spr and carries Fire, Ice, and Thunder elements simultaneously. To absorb all three elements, junction **Firaga** (Fire), **Blizzaga** (Ice), and **Thundaga** (Thunder) to El.Def-J slots — this covers all components of Dark Flare simultaneously. Do not junction Fire, Thunder, or Wind class magic to El.Att-J — Tiamat resists both Fire and Thunder (×0.5 each) and is fully immune to Wind. **Draw GF Eden from Tiamat if it was not obtained from Ultima Weapon** — this is the absolute last opportunity in the game. Drawing Eden here triggers **Achievement: Eden** upon obtaining the GF. Cast Meltdown and use Aura + Renzokuken to end the fight quickly. Tiamat always drops **Status Guard** items (teach St.Def-Jx4 to a GF). **Unlock Save** after this fight — the internal save point is now accessible, allowing a full save before Omega Weapon and the final sequence.

{{CALLOUT:enemy|Omega Weapon}} Have the primary party stand at the fountain area (Party Switch Point). Switch to the secondary party, go to the chandelier room, then to the bell room before the Art Gallery — pull the rope inside. **Immediately switch back to the primary party** at the Party Switch Point. With the primary party, go north through the Chapel to encounter Omega Weapon. Total time: about 60 seconds.

**Boss: Omega Weapon** (Lv 1-100) — HP: 111,105-1,161,000 | AP: 250
- Draw: Flare, Holy, Meteor, Ultima

Strategy (Omega Weapon): Pre-battle requirements — (1) All three characters must have exactly **9,999 max HP** — use HP+??% abilities if needed. (2) Junction **Death** to St.Def-J on all characters — Lv5 Death kills instantly at multiples of Lv5. (3) Remove all elemental magic from El.Att-J — Omega absorbs ALL elements. (4) Stock at least 10 **Megalixirs** (Bahamut card → Card Mod → 100× Megalixir). (5) Have **100× Fast Ammo** + **50× AP Ammo** + **50× Pulse Ammo** for Irvine — Fast Ammo's Quick Shot cycles rapidly to fill Limit Break time windows, AP Ammo's Armor Shot ignores Vitality for consistent max damage, and Pulse Ammo's Hyper Shot delivers peak single-hit damage on turns with full time available. (6) Have Squall and Irvine with Str-J above 220. (7) Set battle speed to slowest. (8) Equip **Defend** command on at least two characters.

Omega absorbs all elements and cannot be Slowed, Mugged, or Blinded. It can be drawn from: **Flare, Holy, Meteor, Ultima** (all levels). No drops besides Three Stars and the Proof of Omega item.

Omega's attack pattern is strictly fixed (random physical swipes occur between any step):
1. **Lv5 Death** → Death on St.Def-J ignores this; cast Meltdown on Omega
2. **Meteor** → use Megalixir to restore full HP on all members
3. **Megido Flame** (fixed 9,998 to all — requires exactly 9,999 HP or someone dies) → all members at 1 HP; unleash Renzokuken + Irvine Armor Shot + Quistis Shockwave Pulsar
4. **Gravija** (75% current HP — at 1 HP deals zero damage) → Squall Renzokuken; two others use Defend
5. **Terra Break** (16 physical hits distributed randomly, each dealing 4,000+ damage — Defend absorbs all) → revive any KO'd, use Mega-Potion
6. **Ultima** → Renzokuken + Shockwave Pulsar + Armor Shot (Ultima deals at most ~1,200 with high Spr)
7. **Light Pillar** (9,999 to one character — unavoidable unless Invincible) → Revive the KO'd member; use Megalixir
8. **Repeat from Meteor**

Omega has over a million HP at high levels — this is a long fight. **Version note**: In the original **PlayStation** version, Omega is fixed at Level 100. In **PC/Steam/Remastered**, Omega scales to the party's level. In either case, its Lv5 Death cannot be avoided by choosing a level not divisible by 5 on PlayStation — and on PC/Remastered, if the party is at a level divisible by 5, Lv5 Death will target them. Death on St.Def-J is mandatory regardless of version.

{{CALLOUT:hint|Alternate Invincible strategy}} Use **Holy War** items (everyone gains Invincible status — grants full immunity to all damage and most status effects for several turns) or **Hero** items (single character Invincible). With Invincible active, the party can weather Megido Flame and Light Pillar without any HP management, transforming the fight from a timed survival exercise into a straightforward damage race. Collect Holy Wars from Card Modding the Gilgamesh Card (1 card → 10× Holy War), or buy Hero from shops. With three characters each under Holy War, the entire Omega pattern can be weathered through Gravija and Terra Break freely. **Aura Stone farming for Limit Break access**: If Aura magic is in short supply, **Power Wrists** bought from the Esthar Pet Shop can be refined into **Aura Stones** via Siren's **Tool-RF** (1 Power Wrist → 1 Aura Stone; 1 Aura Stone → 10× Aura via F Mag-RF). This converts a purchasable item directly into the Limit Break-enabling spell, bypassing the need to draw or refine Aura from enemies.

Drop: **Three Stars** — teaches any GF the **Expend×3-1** ability (while under Triple status, each spell cast draws from only 1 stock instead of 3; from a 100-stock supply that means 100 casts instead of 33, tripling magic endurance). Extra Three Stars beyond the three needed can also be refined into **100× Triple each** via Time Mag-RF. Defeating Omega also adds the **Proof of Omega** entry to the Tutorial. **Achievement: Omega Destroyed** — awarded upon defeating Omega Weapon.

From the Chapel, ascend the clock tower, cross the clock arrows, and proceed to the final save point. The save is available here — this is the absolute last save before the ending. Make all final preparation, junction every character optimally, and enter the final chamber. The Left Diamond girl plays rare cards that were modded (following the Queen of Cards chain to Shumi Village). Challenge her repeatedly to recover missed GF cards. The PuPu Card is never played by any CC member.

checkpoints.label: Omega Destroyed
checkpoints.description: Defeat Omega Weapon in Ultimecia's Castle. Station the primary party near the chapel, switch to the reserve party, ring the bell in the room before the art gallery to spawn Omega, then engage with the primary party. Card Mod the Gilgamesh Card → 10× Holy War for full party invincibility.

checkpoints.label: Missed GFs
checkpoints.description: If you missed any missable GFs (any of the ones listed above), Draw them from the bosses in Ultimecia Castle. (Not possible in the original JP version.)

checkpoints.label: Eden
checkpoints.description: Draw from Tiamat in Ultimecia Castle (preferable), or from Ultima Weapon.

checkpoints.label: Ultima Weapon, Omega Weapon
checkpoints.description: Do not defeat them (or at least don't save after doing so).

enemies.name: Forbidden
enemies.notes: Draws Death, Zombie, Stop, Blind; mug Dead Spirit

enemies.name: Armadodo
enemies.notes: Draws Protect, Shell, Quake; mug Turtle Shell; **Roll Attack** 100% Critical; can fall over after physical hit (Vit/Spr × 50% for 4 turns) then rise again - exploit the fallen phase with magic

enemies.name: Imp
enemies.notes: Draws Blind, Silence, Break, Confuse; mug Poison Powder

enemies.name: Behemoth
enemies.notes: Draws Flare, Tornado, Regen; mug Barrier

enemies.name: Tri-Face
enemies.notes: Draws Pain, Flare, Slow; mug Curse Spike - continue farming

enemies.name: Jelleye
enemies.notes: Draws all elemental tiers (Fire/Ice/Thunder/Wind up to -ga); mug Healing Water

enemies.name: Iron Giant
enemies.notes: Draws Haste, Quake, Shell, Reflect; mug Star Fragment

enemies.name: Red Bat
enemies.notes: Draws Drain; mug Vampire Fang

enemies.name: Hexadragon
enemies.notes: Grand Hall & Storage Room areas; draws Firaga/Dispel/Shell/Protect (Lv30+); absorbs Fire & Poison; weak to Water; mug Red Fang (8x at Lv30+)

enemies.name: Malboro
enemies.notes: Wine Cellar area (7.5% chance); draws Bio/Demi/Quake (Lv30+); Bad Breath inflicts all statuses at once - have status protection ready; mug Malboro Tentacle

enemies.name: Tonberry
enemies.notes: Passageway area (Screen 17); draws Death; mug & drop Chef's Knife (Tonberry compatibility item for Tonberry GF)

enemies.name: Elnoyle
enemies.notes: Prison Cell & Chapel areas; draws Pain/Double (all levels), Holy (Lv40+); mug Moon Stone; **drops Energy Crystal at Lv30+** - at Lv40+ all drop slots are Energy Crystal, making this the best Pulse Ammo source in Disc 4 (Leviathan's Ammo-RF: 2 Energy Crystal → 1 Pulse Ammo)

enemies.name: Adamantoise
enemies.notes: Fountain Square area; draws Blizzaga/Shell/Protect/Reflect (Lv30+); mug Orihalcon (Lv30+); weak to Thunder & Earth; **drops Adamantine at Lv30+** (all 4 drop slots) - needed for Lion Heart and Ehrgeiz

enemies.name: Grand Mantis
enemies.notes: Multiple areas (Screens 16, 17, 25); draws Water/Esuna/Life (Life at Lv30+); weak to Ice & Thunder; mug Sharp Spike (8x at Lv30+) - efficient source for Save the Queen and Cardinal crafting

enemies.name: Blobra
enemies.notes: Main Hall & Passageway areas; draws Shell (all levels), Reflect/Blind/Confuse (Lv30+); has random elemental weakness each encounter (up to × 7) - Scan or rotate elements to exploit it; mug Rune Armlet (5.8%+4.7% chance at Lv30+); 4 × Blobra Cards → Card Mod → 1 × Rune Armlet (teaches GF Spr+20%); **Sticky Icky** attack inflicts Slow - junction Slow resistance to St.Def-J

chapters.title: Final Preparations
chapters.content:
{{CALLOUT:card|CC Group on the Ragnarok}}

{{CALLOUT:point|Final overworld access}} Leave Ultimecia Castle through the portal area **one screen south of the Save Point and castle entrance**. From here, the Ragnarok and the compressed-time world map let you finish any remaining Disc 4 cleanup before entering the final chamber.

{{CALLOUT:perfect|Perfect Game tiers}} A Perfect Game is best treated as six nested completion tiers. **Every tier inherits the tiers above it**, so do not start a later grind until the required missables, cards, GFs, treasures, tutorials, shops, and one-time flags from the earlier tiers are secured.

{{CALLOUT:perfect|Level 1}} Core perfect-file baseline:
- Able to beat the main game, Ultima Weapon, and Omega Weapon without saving those superboss kills on the final perfect file
- Best weapons for all six main playable characters and all Limit Breaks unlocked
- All 16 junctionable GFs, at least one of every card, and all 32 magic slots filled for every character
- All sidequests completed except permanently defeating Ultima Weapon and Omega Weapon
- Battle Meter fully upgraded, all shops registered in Call Shop, SeeD Rank A, and either zero or all 30 written SeeD tests answered
- X-ATM092 destroyed, **0 kills for Seifer and 0 kills for Edea**, **0 KOs for every character and GF**, and exactly **1 escape** from the mandatory X-ATM092 beach escape
- Both superbosses still alive if you want the repeatable perfect-file state

{{CALLOUT:perfect|Level 2}} Adds at least one of every item, all main characters and GFs at Level 100, max GF compatibility for every character, all treasures, max junctioned stats, all Tutorial entries, only the Open play rule and All trade rule active globally, and all GFs retaining their unique non-restorable abilities.

{{CALLOUT:perfect|Level 3}} Adds 100 copies of every common card, exactly one copy of each rare Lv8/Lv9/Lv10 card and PuPu, base Str/Vit/Mag/Spr maxed without junctions for all characters, 100 of nearly every item, and all GFs at 9,999 HP.

{{CALLOUT:perfect|Levels 4-6}} Level 4 adds max Gil, max kill count for the six main characters, 100 of every item including stat-boosting items, and max Speed. Level 5 adds max Luck. Level 6 requires PocketStation hardware for the exclusive items and a fully powered Boko.

{{CALLOUT:mechanics|Disc 4 stat-max prerequisites}} Before committing to the ending, confirm that the CC Group was defeated by the end of Disc 3, all Esthar shops were physically visited before time compression, Ultima Weapon is still alive if you want the fixed Deep Sea Research Center Tri-Face route for Luck Up farming, Siren is Level 100 if Dark Matter refinement is needed, and the relevant RF/menu abilities are learned in the Cards, GFs, Abilities, Refine, and Items tabs.

With the castle guardians defeated and the sealed commands restored, the party should have access to Items, Magic, GF, Draw, Command Abilities, Limit Breaks, and Resurrection. Before the final save, refresh **Auto-Haste**, **Auto-Protect**, **Auto-Shell**, **Death on ST-Def**, full HP, Meltdown access, Megalixirs, Phoenix Downs, and any Life/Full-Life or Revive fallback you plan to use.

{{CALLOUT:enemy|Omega Weapon setup}} Omega Weapon is optional, but this is the clean final-prep checklist. Give **Defend to everyone who has learned it** (normally two characters). Everyone should have **Item** and 9,999 HP; if someone falls short, use HP+80%. Elem-Def and ST-Atk do not matter here, Elem-Atk should be empty, and **ST-Def calls for Death**. Fill passive slots with Auto-Haste, Auto-Protect, Auto-Shell, then Str+60%. Keep Magic and GF in spare command slots, make sure someone can cast Meltdown, and bring plenty of Megalixirs plus Phoenix Downs or Life/Full-Life/Revive.

{{CALLOUT:route|Omega Weapon route}} Save first. Put the **main party in the Courtyard Party Switch Area** on the green light, then switch to the reserve party. With the reserve party, go to the **Entrance Hall balcony and exit right**. Examine the rope to **ring the bell**, then quickly switch back to the main party and run into the Chapel before the timer expires.

For the full Omega Weapon strategy (junction setup, attack sequence, and item requirements), see the **Ultimecia's Castle** chapter.

checkpoints.label: World Map Access
checkpoints.description: Use the portals one screen south of the castle entrance Save Point to return to the compressed-time world map.

checkpoints.label: Omega Weapon Bell Route
checkpoints.description: Main party waits at the Courtyard switch point while the reserve party rings the Entrance Hall balcony rope.

checkpoints.label: Proof of Omega
checkpoints.description: Defeating Omega Weapon adds Proof of Omega to Tutorial; avoid saving afterward if you want a repeatable Perfect Game fight.

enemies.name: Omega Weapon
enemies.notes: Optional superboss. Ring the bell with the reserve party, then engage from the Chapel with the main party.

chapters.title: The Final Battle
chapters.content:
From the pendulum section of the clock tower, ascend the rest of the tower. At the clock exterior, cross the hands, climb down the ladder on the far side, continue to the bridge, and save. This is the last calm moment before the full final sequence.

{{CALLOUT:spoiler|Final battle party rules}} **three party members are selected at random** to begin. If a KO'd character is left down too long, they are **absorbed into time** and removed from the battle; another reserve character enters if one is available. If no reserves remain, that slot stays empty. Revive wanted party members quickly, or deliberately let unwanted members be replaced. If all current fighters are KO'd at once, it is Game Over. Also, **any abilities still sealed remain sealed** for the entire final battle.

{{CALLOUT:preparation|Final junction setup}} **Give your main junctions to your strongest three party members**. Give **Tonberry to a fourth member** so at least one backup has useful abilities. Junction **Water or Wind elemental magic to Elem-Atk**; ST-Atk is not useful here. Defend Stop, Pain statuses, Curse, and Slow in that priority order, remembering that Auto-Haste already blocks Stop and Slow. Pain on ST-Def is excellent because it covers Curse plus Darkness, Poison, and Silence. For passive abilities, prioritize Auto-Haste, Auto-Shell, Auto-Protect, then stat boosts. Focus especially on Spr, and **do not use Mug or Rare Item** because nothing in the sequence has useful mugs or drops.

When ready, enter the door and choose **"Hell, let's go in."** The battle begins after the scene.

**Boss: Ultimecia** (Lv 1-65) - HP: 4,600-43,000 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Haste, Slow, Reflect, Demi
- Mug: Nothing (0%)
- Drop: Nothing (0%)
- Card Drop: Nothing
- Weakness: Poison [1.5x]
- Element Resist: Holy nullifies
- Status Weak: The End

Strategy (Ultimecia): Apply Meltdown and use Aura-backed Limit Breaks. Keep HP high, cure Curse quickly if Maelstrom lands, and do not allow a KO'd character you want to keep to stay down long enough to be absorbed into time.

**Boss: Griever** (Lv 1-65) - HP: 16,750-115,950 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Bio, Quake, Tornado
- Mug: Nothing (0%)
- Drop: Nothing (0%)
- Card Drop: Nothing
- Weakness: Wind, Holy [2.5x]
- Element Resist: Flying, Poison absorbs
- Status Weak: The End

Strategy (Griever): Remove Poison from Elem-Atk before attacking because Griever absorbs Poison. Wind and Holy hit hardest. If Griever uses Triple, Dispel it. Stay ready for Shockwave Pulsar and revive immediately after any KO.

**Boss: Ultimecia/Griever** (Lv 1-65) - HP: 13,050-176,250 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Firaga, Thundaga, Blizzaga
- Mug: Nothing (0%)
- Drop: Nothing (0%)
- Card Drop: Nothing
- Weakness: Lightning, Water
- Element Resist: Flying, Poison nullifies, Holy absorbs
- Status Weak: The End

**Boss: Helix** (Lv 1-65) - HP: 4,400-30,000 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Esuna, Dispel
- Mug: Nothing (0%)
- Drop: Nothing (0%)
- Card Drop: Nothing
- Weakness: None
- Element Resist: Flying
- Status Weak: The End

Strategy (Ultimecia/Griever): The **Helix support units** can be drawn from for Esuna and Dispel. Destroy them quickly when they appear so Great Attractor pressure does not build. Use Lightning or Water damage on the main body, keep Protect/Shell up, and avoid summoning GFs during this phase.

**Boss: Ultimecia (Final Form)** (Lv 1-65) - HP: 16,500-278,900 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Flare, Holy
- Mug: Nothing (0%)
- Drop: Nothing (0%)
- Card Drop: Nothing
- Weakness: None
- Element Resist: Flying
- Status Weak: The End

**Boss: Ultimecia (Lower Portion)** (Lv 1-65) - HP: 2,300-21,500 | AP: 0 | EXP: 0
- Draw: Apocalypse
- Mug: Nothing (0%)
- Drop: Nothing (0%)
- Card Drop: Nothing
- Weakness: None
- Element Resist: Flying
- Status Weak: The End

Strategy (Final Form): Draw Flare and Holy if needed. **Warning:** her Magic attack removes 100 stocks of your highest-stocked spell from a character, stripping those junction bonuses mid-fight. Prioritize killing her quickly before she can repeat this. Finish with Aura-backed Limit Breaks. Hell's Judgment drops everyone to 1 HP; respond immediately with Limit Breaks and a Megalixir rhythm. When the lower portion appears, destroy it before **Apocalypse** is cast, or draw Apocalypse first if you want the spell in stock.

{{CALLOUT:spoiler|Ending}} After Ultimecia falls, the conclusion plays. The ending is long and fully cinematic, so enjoy it once the battle is done.

**Congratulations - Final Fantasy VIII Remastered is complete.**

checkpoints.label: Final Bridge Save
checkpoints.description: Save after crossing the clock hands and reaching the bridge before entering the final chamber.

checkpoints.label: End of Game
checkpoints.description: Defeat Ultimecia and clear the ending sequence.

achievements.title: Magic Miner
achievements.description: Draw magic 100 times from draw points throughout the game. Activate the Move-Find ability (Siren) to reveal hidden field draw points. Begin drawing every draw point encountered from the start.

achievements.title: Magician
achievements.description: Draw (Stock) magic from enemies 100 times in battle. Junction the Draw command to a character before each encounter to ensure actions are counted. Draw from every enemy throughout the game.

achievements.title: 100 Kills
achievements.description: Defeat 100 enemies. Achieved through standard story progression. If using the No Encounters booster or running from battles, disable it during normal play to accrue kills naturally.

achievements.title: 1000 Kills
achievements.description: Defeat 1,000 enemies. Achieved through normal progression by disc 3. If short, grind at the Island Closest to Hell (northeast of Esthar) after obtaining Ragnarok — enemies are weak to Quistis's Degenerator.

achievements.title: Quezacotl
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Quezacotl. Interact with the desk in Squall's classroom, select "Turn on the power," then "Tutorials." Quistis forces acquisition at the front gate if missed.

achievements.title: Shiva
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Shiva. Obtained simultaneously with Quezacotl via the classroom desk tutorial sequence at Balamb Garden.

achievements.title: Ifrit
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Ifrit. Defeat Ifrit in the Fire Cavern east of Balamb Garden within the chosen time limit. A 10-minute limit is sufficient.

achievements.title: Siren
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Siren. Draw Siren from Elvoret atop the Dollet Radio Tower. Missable — only opportunity in the game.

achievements.title: Diablos
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Diablos. Use the Magical Lamp (received from Cid after graduation) to trigger the Diablos battle. Save beforehand; cast Demi twice to reduce its HP, then defeat it.

achievements.title: Brothers
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Brothers (Sacred + Minotaur). Complete the Tomb of the Unknown King northeast of Deling City. At every 4-way crossroad, take the right-hand route to reach each directional chamber.

achievements.title: Carbuncle
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Carbuncle. Draw from the Iguion bosses during the Deling City assassination attempt. Both Iguions must still be present when the draw occurs.

achievements.title: Leviathan
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Leviathan. Missable — draw from NORG aboard Balamb Garden. Damage the pod to expose NORG's body, then draw Leviathan immediately.

achievements.title: Tonberry
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Tonberry. Defeat at least 20 Tonberries in the Centra Ruins to trigger the Tonberry King encounter; GF awarded upon victory. The Tonberry King will appear the next time a Tonberry battle ends in the correct scene, so keep hunting until it appears.

achievements.title: Pandemona
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Pandemona. Missable — draw from Fujin during the boss fight in Balamb Town.

achievements.title: Cerberus
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Cerberus. Located in the main hall of Galbadia Garden — interact to initiate battle. Missable — must be obtained before proceeding past this point.

achievements.title: Alexander
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Alexander. Missable — defeat Seifer in the auditorium, then draw Alexander from Edea before she is defeated.

achievements.title: Cactuar
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Cactuar. After obtaining Ragnarok, locate Cactuar Island (small island off the eastern coast of the Esthar continent). Interact with Jumbo Cactuar on the world map to begin the encounter.

achievements.title: Doomtrain
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Doomtrain. Acquire the Solomon Ring from Tears' Point (near Esthar). Collect 6× Remedy+, 6× Steel Pipe, and 6× Malboro Tentacle, then use the Solomon Ring from the Items menu.

achievements.title: Bahamut
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Bahamut. Navigate to the core of the Deep Sea Research Center (southwest of Esthar). At the steam room panel choose: "It's not our will to fight" → "Never" → the bottom option. Awarded upon victory.

achievements.title: Eden
achievements.description: Unlock Guardian Force Eden. Draw from Ultima Weapon deep in the Deep Sea Research Center after defeating Bahamut. Last opportunity: draw from Tiamat in Ultimecia's Castle on Disc 4.

achievements.title: First Salary
achievements.description: Receive the first SeeD salary. Awarded automatically after the Training Center incident and the return to the dormitory.

achievements.title: Timber Maniacs
achievements.description: Collect all 12 Timber Maniacs magazines. Locations span the full game: Balamb Hotel, Timber Maniacs Office, Timber Hotel, Deling City Hotel, Dollet Hotel, Dollet Pub, FH Inn, FH rail dead-end house, Shumi Village, Trabia Garden Graveyard, Edea's Orphanage, White SeeD Ship.

achievements.title: Card Player
achievements.description: Play a game of Triple Triad. Press Square near any NPC who accepts card challenges to initiate a match.

achievements.title: Loser
achievements.description: Lose a rare card (Level 5+) in a Triple Triad match via the Trade Rule. Intentionally losing to the Queen of Cards in Balamb is the standard method.

achievements.title: Cards Club Master
achievements.description: Defeat every CC Group member at Triple Triad. Order: Jack (Main Hall) → Joker (Training Center docks) → Club (Dormitory corridor) → Diamond (below Directory) → Spade (2F, post-Norg) → Heart (Bridge, challenge Xu) → King (dormitory bed rest event, post-Kadowaki hint).

achievements.title: Chocobo
achievements.description: Capture a Chocobo. Visit the Beginner Forest (northernmost continent near Shumi Village). Purchase ChocoWhis for 1,000 Gil, then use the whistle in the upper-left corner where only one Chicobo appears.

achievements.title: Ragnarok
achievements.description: Acquire the Ragnarok airship. Obtained automatically during the Lunar Base sequence on Disc 3. After Lunatic Pandora launches, board the Ragnarok and clear the color-coded monster pairs in sequence to prevent revival.

achievements.title: Handyman
achievements.description: Upgrade any weapon at a Junk Shop using the Remodel command. The first full Junk Shop access is at Fisherman's Horizon. The Lionheart (Squall's ultimate weapon) requires 1× Adamantine, 4× Dragon Fang, and 12× Pulse Ammo.

achievements.title: Obel Lake Secret
achievements.description: Complete the Obel Lake side quest. Hum at the lake peninsula (Trabia continent) → find Mr. Monkey near Dollet → collect 4 stone inscriptions across the world map → decode "TREASUREATMINDEISLE" → retrieve Luck-J Scroll at Minde Isle. Must be completed before Disc 4.

achievements.title: UFO
achievements.description: Complete PuPu's side quest. Witness UFO sightings at: Mandy Beach, Kashkabald Desert, Winhill Bluffs, Heath Peninsula, and Grandidi Forest. Return to the original Balamb Garden crash site with 5× Elixir and the Item command junctioned; give PuPu all 5 Elixirs.

achievements.title: Omega Destroyed
achievements.description: Defeat Omega Weapon in Ultimecia's Castle. Station the primary party near the chapel, switch to the reserve party, ring the bell in the room before the art gallery to spawn Omega, then engage with the primary party. Card Mod the Gilgamesh Card → 10× Holy War for full party invincibility.

achievements.title: End of Game
achievements.description: Complete Final Fantasy VIII Remastered. Defeat Ultimecia at the culmination of the Time Compression sequence.

achievements.title: Top Rank
achievements.description: Reach SeeD Rank A (maximum). Pass SeeD Tests 1–30 via Menu → Tutorial → TEST. All 30 test answers are available in the SeeD Rank & Salary reference page. Best done starting from Disc 2 when tests unlock.

achievements.title: Maximum HP
achievements.description: Reach 9,999 HP on any character. Junction HP+80% (from Diablos/Brothers) and HP+40%/HP+20% abilities. Grind levels at the Island Closest to Hell (northeast of Esthar) after obtaining Ragnarok for simultaneous AP and EXP.

missables.title: Siren
missables.description: Draw Siren from Elvoret atop the Dollet communications tower before ending the battle.

missables.title: X-ATM092
missables.description: Destroy X-ATM092 before it reaches the town.

missables.title: Battle Meter, Magical Lamp
missables.description: Obtain both items from Cid before boarding the train to Timber. (Diablos can be obtained at any point in the game so long as you have the Magical Lamp.)

missables.title: Angelo Recover, Angelo Reverse
missables.description: Do not teach Rinoa these skills until you're completely finished with Angelo Searching.

missables.title: Girl Next Door
missables.description: Grab from Timber Maniacs building before Disc 4.

missables.title: Pet Nametag #2
missables.description: After leaving Timber, encourage the man outside the Timber Maniacs building to become a journalist (must be done before finishing Disc 1), then return here after completing Missile Base and speak to him.

missables.title: Location Displayer
missables.description: Buy it from the soldier outside Caraway's Mansion before giving him the student's ID number.

missables.title: Carbuncle
missables.description: Draw Carbuncle from the Iguion bosses during the Deling City assassination sequence before defeating them.

missables.title: Eyes on Me
missables.description: When speaking to Raine in Winhill, ask her about Julia before leaving the conversation. This unlocks the Eyes on Me entry and the opportunity does not return.

missables.title: Pet Nametag #1
missables.description: Obtain from D-District Prison (Level 2, left cell).

missables.title: Character Report
missables.description: Beat the Floor 10 right-cell card player once before leaving D-District Prison to obtain Character Report, the Battle Meter upgrade.

missables.title: Leviathan
missables.description: Draw Leviathan from NORG after the pod opens during the Balamb Garden basement fight.

missables.title: Master Fisherman's Quest, Occult Fan III
missables.description: Do this before going upstairs in Mayor Dobe's house (this is the house at the middle of the solar panel array).

missables.title: Pandemona
missables.description: Draw from Fujin in Balamb.

missables.title: Cerberus
missables.description: Defeat Cerberus before the end of Disc 2.

missables.title: Alexander
missables.description: Draw Alexander from Edea during the final Disc 2 battle before she is defeated.

missables.title: Esthar / Time Compression tutorial entries
missables.description: On Disc 3, talk to Edea until Adel is mentioned in yellow to unlock Esthar (1) and Esthar (2), then talk to Cid until he has no new lines for Time Compression and The Great Hyne.

missables.title: Timber Maniacs
missables.description: Pick up the Timber Maniacs issue on the White SeeD Ship while the ship is accessible; after this story window, the ship disappears.

missables.title: Zone
missables.description: Do not give Zone the Girl Next Door. It's unique, but nothing he gives in return can't be gotten through other methods.

missables.title: Occult Fan IV
missables.description: Before going to Lunar Gate, speak to the Presidential Aide near the Airstation in Esthar, then again in the Presidential Palace, then pick up the magazine off the ground.

missables.title: End of Disc 3
missables.description: All towns and most world locations become inaccessible on Disc 4 once Time Compression begins. Confirm all side quests are complete — Obel Lake, PuPu, CC Group cards, Shumi Village, Chocobo Forests, Solomon's Ring — before proceeding through the Lunatic Pandora. The disc transition is the absolute point of no return for Disc 3 optional content.

missables.title: Missed GFs
missables.description: If you missed any missable GFs (any of the ones listed above), Draw them from the bosses in Ultimecia Castle. (Not possible in the original JP version.)

missables.title: Eden
missables.description: Draw from Tiamat in Ultimecia Castle (preferable), or from Ultima Weapon.

missables.title: Ultima Weapon, Omega Weapon
missables.description: Do not defeat them (or at least don't save after doing so).

missables.title: PuPu Card
missables.description: Finish PuPu's Quest by feeding PuPu 5 Elixirs to receive this.

gfs.name: Quezacotl
gfs.attack: Thunder Storm: Deals Lightning-elemental damage to all enemies
gfs.location: Examine computer in 2F Classroom (Balamb Garden) or given by Quistis before going to Fire Cavern

gfs.name: Shiva
gfs.attack: Diamond Dust: Ice-elemental damage to all enemies
gfs.location: Examine computer in 2F Classroom (Balamb Garden) or given by Quistis before going to Fire Cavern

gfs.name: Ifrit
gfs.attack: Hell Fire: Fire-elemental damage to all enemies

gfs.name: Siren
gfs.attack: Silent Voice: Non-elemental damage and Silence status to all enemies
gfs.location: Draw from Elvoret or Draw from Tri-Point
learningOrder: the Mag+% abilities (to unlock Mag Bonus)

gfs.name: Brothers
gfs.attack: Brotherly Love: Earth-elemental ground-based damage to all enemies
gfs.location: Defeat the Brothers in the Tomb of the Unknown King

gfs.name: Diablos
gfs.attack: Dark Messenger: For every level Diablos gains, deals 1% of enemy's current HP as Gravity damage (capped at 9,999)
gfs.location: Use Magical Lamp in menu, then win battle against Diablos

gfs.name: Carbuncle
gfs.attack: Ruby Light: Sets Reflect on all party members
gfs.location: Draw from Iguion or Draw from Krysta

gfs.name: Leviathan
gfs.attack: Tsunami: Water-elemental damage to all enemies
gfs.location: Draw from NORG or Draw from Trauma

gfs.name: Pandemona
gfs.attack: Tornado Zone: Wind-elemental damage to all enemies
gfs.location: Draw from Fujin (Balamb, Disc 2) or Draw from Red Giant

gfs.name: Cerberus
gfs.attack: Counter Rockets: Double and Triple status to all characters
gfs.location: Defeat Cerberus or Draw from Gargantua

gfs.name: Alexander
gfs.attack: Holy Judgment: Holy-elemental damage to all enemies
gfs.location: Draw from Edea (Disc 2) or Draw from Catoblepas

gfs.name: Doomtrain
gfs.attack: Runaway Train: Poison-elemental damage and Berserk, Confuse, Curse, Darkness, Doom, Silence, Sleep, Slow, Stop, and Vit0 to all enemies
gfs.location: Use Solomon Ring with 6 each of Remedy+, Steel Pipe, and Malboro Tentacle in inventory

gfs.name: Bahamut
gfs.attack: Mega Flare: Non-elemental, defense-ignoring damage to all enemies

gfs.name: Cactuar
gfs.attack: 1,000 Needles: Deals 1,000 damage per 10 Levels Cactuar has (maxes out at 10,000 damage at Level 100)

gfs.name: Tonberry
gfs.attack: Chef's Knife: Random non-elemental, defense-ignoring damage to one

gfs.name: Eden
gfs.attack: Eternal Breath: Deals EXTREME non-elemental damage to all (maxes out at 60,000 damage)
gfs.location: Draw from Ultima Weapon or Draw from Tiamat

gfs.name: Odin
gfs.attack: Zantetsuken / Gungnir: Instantly defeats all enemies (Zantetsuken) or deals heavy non-elemental damage when Zantetsuken fails
gfs.location: 1. Defeat Odin at Centra Ruins (time limit: 20 minutes). 2. After Seifer defeats Odin in Disc 3, Odin is replaced by Gilgamesh (who uses Zantetsuken, Excalibur, Excalipoor, and Masamune).

gfs.name: Phoenix
gfs.attack: Rebirth Flame: Revives all KO'd party members and deals Fire-elemental damage to all enemies
gfs.location: 1. Automatically obtained when Phoenix Pinion is in inventory (Phoenix Pinion found in Winhill after Disc 2 events, or from Ragnarok Item Machine). 2. Phoenix appears automatically mid-battle when all characters are KO'd, consuming one Phoenix Pinion.

cards.name: Chubby Chocobo
cards.location: Played by guy on bench near Library in Balamb Garden [QoC]

cards.name: Angelo
cards.location: Played by Watts (Forest Owls Base, Disc 1/White SeeD Ship, Disc 3)

cards.name: MiniMog
cards.location: Played by young boy circling Balamb Garden's main hallway

cards.name: Chicobo
cards.location: Reward for visiting Chocobo Sanctuary after completing all Chocobo Forest puzzles

cards.name: Shiva
cards.location: Reward for giving Zone Girl Next Door for free (White SeeD Ship, Disc 3)

cards.name: Siren
cards.location: Played by Dollet Pub Owner (private room only)

cards.name: Minotaur
cards.location: Reward for completing the Tomb of the Unknown King

cards.name: Kiros
cards.location: Played by man in black in Deling City Shopping Arcade, opposite Junk Shop [QoC]

cards.name: Leviathan
cards.location: Played by Joker [CC Group, any disc]

cards.name: Rinoa
cards.location: Played by General Caraway (Deling City, Disc 2-3), after losing the Ifrit Card to him

cards.name: Seifer
cards.location: Played by Cid (Balamb Garden Headmaster's Office, Discs 1-2/Edea's House, Disc 3)

items.name: Potion
items.useDesc: Restores 200 HP to one
items.obtain: Start with 6 in inventory Buy at Item Shops, Esthar Shop!!! Mug/Drop from many soldier-type enemies

items.name: Potion+
items.useDesc: Restores 400 HP to one

items.name: Hi-Potion
items.useDesc: Restores 1,000 HP to one
items.obtain: Buy at Item Shops, Esthar Shop, Esthar Shop!!! Mug/Drop from many high-level soldier-type enemies
refineFrom: Potion+ [3:1] (Med LV Up) Healing Mail [1:6] (Recov Med-RF) Wedge, Biggs Card [1:1] (Card Mod) Fujin, Raijin Card [1:1] (Card Mod)

junctions.name: ST-Atk
footnotes: * Drain: Only the healing ratio to the attacker is affected by junction strength or enemy resistance — damage dealt to the target stays the same regardless. If the target is Undead or Zombie, the Drain effect reverses: the target is healed and the attacker takes damage instead.

junctions.name: ST-Def
rows: Berserk, Confuse, Curse, Drain**, KO, Poison, Sleep, Zombie
Berserk, Confuse, Darkness, Petrify***, Poison, Silence, Sleep, Slow, Stop
Berserk, Confuse, Curse, Darkness, Petrify***, Poison, Silence, Sleep, Slow, Stop
footnotes: * Aura provides 200% Curse protection — junctioning just 50 Aura gives full (100%) immunity to Curse.
** Drain defense only blocks HP recovery for the attacker; the defended character still takes damage as normal, making Drain entirely pointless to defend against in ST-Def.
*** Petrify protection in ST-Def also blocks Petrifying (slow-petrification) by the same percentage.

characters.name: Squall Leonheart
notes: Renzokuken uses 22-power opening hits, and each hit can receive the gunblade trigger bonus.
Finisher chance rises sharply with Crisis Level; weapon upgrades determine which finishers can appear.
Single-target finishers keep the original target. Finishers are physical and can crit, which is the only time Squall uses Luck for criticals.

tables.title: Renzokuken Finishers
rows: Shear Trigger, Cutting Trigger, Flame Saber, Twin Lance, Punishment, Lion Heart
Flame Saber, Twin Lance, Punishment, Lion Heart

characters.name: Quistis Trepe
notes: Blue Magic is learned by using the listed item on Quistis.
For status-setting Blue Magic, higher Crisis Levels include the status effects from lower Crisis Levels too.
Most Blue Magic is magical and enemy-targeting unless the table notes otherwise.

tables.title: Blue Magic
rows: KO to enemies with divisible level

characters.name: Zell Dincht
notes: Duel skills can be executed whenever the input sequence is valid, even before the matching Combat King magazine has been read.
Normal Duel attacks continue the chain; finishers end the Limit, so they are strongest at the end of the timer.
Fast repeated short loops can outdamage rushing immediately to a finisher.

profile.Strong Stats: Magic, Spirit, Speed, Hit (with ultimate weapon), Luck
profile.Weak Stats: HP, Strength, Vitality, Hit (without ultimate weapon)

notes: Slots generates magic without requiring stocked spells and without consuming stocked magic.
Higher Crisis Levels improve the spell pool and can increase the number of casts, up to three for normal spells.
Full-Cure, Rapture, Wall, and The End are unique single-cast Slot results. Apocalypse is not available in a normal, unmodified game.

rows: Heals all characters to maximum HP
Ejects all opponents (fails on targets that are immune)
Sets Protect and Shell status on all characters
Inflicts The End status (essentially instant KO that very few enemies, mostly Undead, block)

characters.name: Rinoa Heartilly
notes: Passive Angelo skills require reading the listed Pet Pals issue, selecting the trick to learn, then walking the listed steps with Rinoa in the party.
Combine checks Crisis Level from high to low and uses the highest learned result available.
Angel Wing is available on Discs 3 and 4. A focused Meteor/Ultima stock setup turns it into one of the fastest high-damage strategies in the game.
Angelo Search can only trigger while Rinoa is in the party and battle time is moving with no other action resolving; a Confused Turtapod in its shell is the cleanest passive farming setup.

tables.title: Passive Angelo Skills
rows: Heals for 62.5% of one character's maximum HP
Revives KO'd party member and restores them to 12.5% max HP

tables.title: Angelo Search Item Pool
rows: Dark Matter and Pain route material
Pulse Ammo and high-end crafting route
Ultimate weapon and Vit Up route material

profile.How Joins: During first visit to Galbadia Garden
profile.Weak Stats: HP, Strength, Vitality, Magic, Spirit, Luck

notes: Shot consumes the selected ammo type; if the ammo runs out, that shot cannot continue.
All Shot types are physical and can crit. Irvine gets an extra Luck bonus for Shot critical checks.
Fast Ammo has low power per hit but extremely high firing rate, making Quick Shot excellent with high Strength.

abilitySections.title: Abilities
paragraphs: Use this tab to check which abilities can be assigned to characters, which GFs learn them, and which items can teach them. Magic spell data, GF data, and item data live in their own top-level tabs so this view stays focused on ability assignment.
Available To lists the GFs that come with the ability or can learn it through AP. AP is the learn cost; if every native GF already has the ability learned, the cost is shown as N/A.

Description mirrors the in-game function. Taught By lists the teaching item when one exists. If Taught By is N/A, do not remove that ability from a GF with Amnesia Greens on a perfect-file route because there is no item-based way to relearn it.

To assign an ability, junction a GF that knows it, open the Junction menu, then equip the command, character, or party ability in an available slot. Attack remains available even without a GF, though some abilities modify or replace that command.

abilitySections.title: Junction Abilities
paragraphs: These abilities open up Junction slots (remember that no Magic and no more than 3 Command Abilities and 2 total Character/Party Abilities can be Junctioned without a proper Junction Ability learned), allowing you to increase stats, modify elemental or status properties, or assign more than two Character/Party Abilities.

In this section, the stat Junctions will each contain ranked lists of spells in order of best to worst (excluding Apocalypse, as it can't be Junctioned in a normal game, but you can still look at its stats if you feel like using a cheating device to hack it in), while the Elem and Status Junction abilities have spell effects covered in their respective Junction Reference sections, with the idea of helping you make efficient use of whatever Magic you have at the moment.
Allows Magic to be Junctioned to a character's HP, increasing their total. The spells usable to boost HP, in order:
Allows you to junction Magic to a character's Strength stat, increasing your physical attack power. The spells, in order of strength:
Allows you to Junction Magic to a character's Vitality stat, increasing physical defense. See the list below for a list of Junctions.
Allows you to Junction Magic to a character's Magic stat, increasing magical potency. Good spells to junction:
Allows you to Junction Magic to a character's Spirit stat, reducing damage taken from magical attacks. The best Junctions here are:
Allows you to Junction Magic to a character's Speed stat, reducing the amount of time between turns. The best options are:
Allows you to Junction Magic to a character's Evasion stat, causing enemy physicals to miss more often. The most useful choices are:

(Note: Due to the mechanics of the Evasion stat (it's derived from Speed), the exact value of your Evasion% may vary by a point from the spell values listed below. This is because Eva Junctions don't actually Junction directly to Eva; instead, they increase the character's Speed for the sole purpose of calculating Evasion. Thus, the spells Junctioned to Spd-J (as well as any increases through Items) will increase the character's Eva proportional to the amount they raise Speed.)
Allows you to Junction Magic to a character's Hit stat, causing the character's physicals to hit more often. The strongest Junctions to Hit are:
Allows you to Junction Magic to a character's Hit stat, causing their physicals to do critical hits more often. The most useful spells here are:
Allows you to Junction Magic to Elem-Atk, giving your basic attack the associated elemental property.
Allows you to Junction Magic to ST-Atk, giving your basic attack the associated status effects.
Allows you to Junction one spell to Elem-Def, reducing damage taken from elemental attacks.
Allows you to Junction up to two spells to Elem-Def, reducing damage taken from elemental attacks.
Allows you to Junction up to four spells to Elem-Def, reducing damage taken from elemental attacks.
Allows you to Junction one spell to ST-Def, reducing the probability of associated status ailments being inflicted.
Allows you to Junction up to two spells to ST-Def, reducing the probability of associated status ailments being inflicted.
Allows you to Junction up to four spells to ST-Def, reducing the probability of associated status ailments being inflicted.

Allows you to assign up to a total of three Character and Party Abilities to a character. Note that this does not automatically allow you to equip a given ability; you still must learn the ability in question on a GF equipped to the character.

Allows you to assign up to a total of 4 Character and Party Abilities to a character. Note that this does not automatically allow you to equip a given ability; you still must learn the desired ability on a GF equipped to the character.

abilitySections.title: Command Abilities
paragraphs: Command Abilities allow your characters to use commands (in other words, take turns) in battle. They must be Junctioned using the Ability Junction menu.

Causes character to perform an attack with his/her equipped weapon. ST-Atk and Elem-Atk properties are considered when this command is used (and in fact this is the only situation in which they are checked). This command is always available in some form and cannot be removed, even if the character has no GF Junctioned.
The Mug Party Command will change the Attack command to Mug, which is identical to Attack except it adds a chance to steal.

Allows you to cast the character's stocked Magic. For information about effects of specific spells when cast, please refer to the data for the spell in question. Remember that casting a Junctioned spell will reduce your stock, in turn reducing the potency of the associated stat or elemental/status effect.
Allows you to Summon any Guardian Force equipped to the character. For information about effects of specific GF Summon effects, please refer to the data for the GF in question.
When a GF is selected for summoning, the GF temporarily takes damage in place of the character. The character's defensive stats and evasion still determine incoming damage and hit checks.
Summon delay shortens as compatibility with that GF rises. If the GF's HP reaches zero during the delay, the summon is canceled and that GF becomes KO'd. KO'd GFs cannot be summoned until revived.

Allows character to Draw Magic from enemies, either Stocking it for later use or Casting it directly (Draw-casted spells have a random spell power up to the normal power of the spell). For information about which spell(s) can be Drawn, refer to the data for the enemy you wish to draw from. Draw is also required to use Draw Points in the field or on the world map.
Allows character to use Items in inventory during battle.
Card attempts to convert a weakened monster into a Triple Triad card. Success chance equals 100% minus the enemy's current HP percentage, so an enemy at 25% HP has a 75% Card success chance.
On success, the common card slot is awarded 93.75% of the time and the rare card slot 6.25% of the time. If an enemy has no card slots, it is immune and Card always fails.
Enemies defeated by Card do not give any Exp.
Inflicts Doom status on one enemy.
Inflicts Haste, Berserk, and Protect on your entire party. This ignores immunities (i.e. you can't block Berserk by Junctioning Berserk to ST-Def).
Removes Berserk, Confuse, Curse, Darkness, Doom, KO, Petrifying, Petrify, Poison, Sleep, Silence, Slow, Stop, Vit0, and Zombie from one target.
Defend nullifies physical attacks, including physical attacks that carry statuses, and halves magical damage until the character selects another command.
It is especially useful with Selphie while fishing for Slots results, or with Cover on the middle party member while the other two stay in low HP for Limit Break access.
Causes character to do a physical attack for triple his/her normal Attack damage, at the expense of losing 10% of their maximum HP each time the command is used.
Restores target to maximum HP. Instantly KO's Undeads and those under Zombie status.
Drains HP from one target, giving it to the caster. Weaker than the Drain spell, so it ends up being quite useless.
Removes KO from one target and restores them to 12.5% max HP. Instantly KO's Undeads and those under Zombie status. This is, by and large, identical to a Life spell.

Halves enemy's level (rounded down, to a minimum of 1). It can be used multiple times to "stack" the effect. Some enemies (usually bosses) are immune to the effect. This is highly useful if you want to reduce the Exp gained from battles, or you need Mugs/Drops from a lower-leveled version of the enemy.

Doubles enemy's level (to a maximum of 100). It can be used multiple times to "stack" the effect. Some enemies (usually bosses) are immune to the effect. This is highly useful if you need a higher-leveled version of an enemy for its Draws/Mugs/Drops, or just want to level up faster.
Inflicts KO on self and removes the character from battle, while damaging one target for six times the user's maximum HP (can exceed 9,999 damage).

Attempts to Devour one enemy, which, if successful, has an effect dependent on the enemy and its Level. The overall chance of Devour succeeding is the same as the enemy's current HP as a percentage of the user's current HP (e.g. if the enemy has 1,000 HP and the character using Devour has 4,000 HP, Devour will succeed 75% of the time). Some enemies (usually bosses, but some normal enemies as well) are immune to Devour, in which case it will fail. Enemies defeated by Devour do not give Exp.

A successful Devour attempt will result in one of the following happening in addition to the enemy being defeated, again determined by the enemy type and its Level. Beneficial effects are in green, while negative ones are in red.

The stat boosts come from Ruby Dragon (HP), T-Rexaur (Str), Adamantoise (Vit), Behemoth (Mag), Malboro (Spr), and PuPu (Spd). All must be in their high-level forms to receive a stat boost. No enemies can boost Luck by being Devoured, by the way.
Summons MiniMog to restore HP to your GFs, which is the only way to heal GFs while in battle. Each use will cost you Gil equal to your party's average level *100.

abilitySections.title: Character Abilities
paragraphs: These abilities are passive abilities that provide a variety of effects. They must be Junctioned using the Ability Junction menu, and only apply to characters with the ability equipped. Note that the Stat+% abilities stack additively, and directly increase the stat in question, capping at the stat's natural maximum (9,999 for HP, 255 for all other stats).
Raises max HP by 20%.
Raises max HP by 40%.
Raises max HP by 80%.
Raises Str by 20%.
Raises Str by 40%.
Raises Str by 60%.
Raises Vit by 20%.
Raises Vit by 40%.
Raises Vit by 60%.
Raises Mag by 20%.
Raises Mag by 60%.
Raises Spr by 20%.
Raises Spr by 40%.
Raises Spr by 60%.
Raises Spd by 20%.
Raises Spd by 40%.
Raises Eva by 30% of its current value.
Raises Luck by 50%.

Changes the Attack command into Mug, which adds a chance to steal items in addition to dealing damage. The chance is based upon the success rate of the enemy to steal from, and is further increased by half the character's Spd stat as a percentage. Be advised that Mugging successfully will prevent the Mugged target from dropping items, so check in advance if you want the Mug or the Drop.
Causes healing items (e.g. Potions) to heal double their normal amounts. This also doubles damage dealt to Undeads/Zombies.
Causes the character to counterattack (with the Attack command) any time they are targeted by a single-target physical attack. The counterattack will occur even if the attack misses.
If the character is damaged, 25% of the damage dealt to the character will be returned to the attacker.

Causes the character to intercept any single-target attack against an adjacent ally that would KO that ally, and only take half damage. Ideally, a character with Cover should be in the middle slot (characters on the ends can only cover the middle character).
Causes character to begin battle with a full ATB guage.
Causes character to regenerate HP by walking around on the field/world map.
Permanently increases character's max HP by 30 any time a level is gained while the ability is equipped. This obviously has no effect if the character is already at Level 100.
Permanently increases character's Strength by 1 any time a level is gained while the ability is equipped. This obviously has no effect if the character is already at Level 100.
Permanently increases character's Vitality by 1 any time a level is gained while the ability is equipped. This obviously has no effect if the character is already at Level 100.
Permanently increases character's Magic by 1 any time a level is gained while the ability is equipped. This obviously has no effect if the character is already at Level 100.
Permanently increases character's Spirit by 1 any time a level is gained while the ability is equipped. This obviously has no effect if the character is already at Level 100.
Gives the character permanent, unremovable Protect status while equipped.
Gives the character permanent, unremovable Shell status while equipped.
Gives the character permanent, unremovable Reflect status while equipped.
Gives the character permanent, unremovable Haste status while equipped. Equipping Auto-Haste also has the effect of granting immunity to Slow and Stop.
Automatically uses the first Restorative Item in the main Item list (not the battle menu) when the character is damaged.
When casting spells under Double status, only one spell in inventory will be used. Does not affect spells cast under Triple.
When casting spells under Triple status, only one spell in inventory will be used. Does not affect spells cast under Double.
Prevents all status ailments except KO.

abilitySections.title: Party Abilities
paragraphs: These abilities affect the party as a whole, even if only equipped on one character. They must be Junctioned to a character using the Ability Junction menu.
Prevents Back Attacks and First Strikes on the part of the enemies, while increasing the party's chances of receiving one of these advantages starting conditions.

Shows hidden Save Points and Draw Points in the field, and allows party to use hidden Save Points. Hidden Draw Points can still be used without this ability, however they will be invisible (although solid).
Reduces random encounter rate by 1/2.
Prevents random encounters. Does not prevent fixed encounters (for example, boss battles or battles triggered by crossing a certain area).

Changes Mug/Drop odds to the following: Common, 128/256 (50%); Uncommon, 114/256 (44.53%); Semi-Rare, 14/256 (5.47%); Rare, never. Interestingly, Rare Item only improves the odds of the Uncommon slot and reduces all the others (including the Rare slot to 0), making this ability nearly always worthless.

abilitySections.title: GF Abilities
paragraphs: These abilities affect the GF itself (but not other GFs), particularly when it is Summoned. Simply learning them applies their effects.
Raises GF's Summon attack damage by 10%. Does not affect Diablos, Cactuar, or GFs that do not use damaging attacks.
Raises GF's Summon attack damage by 20%. Does not affect Diablos, Cactuar, or GFs that do not use damaging attacks.
Raises GF's Summon attack damage by 30%. Does not affect Diablos, Cactuar, or GFs that do not use damaging attacks.
Raises GF's Summon attack damage by 40%. Does not affect Diablos, Cactuar, or GFs that do not use damaging attacks.
Raises GF's maximum HP by 10%.
Raises GF's maximum HP by 20%.
Raises GF's maximum HP by 30%.
Raises GF's maximum HP by 40%.
Boost raises GF summon damage during the animation. Hold the Boost-display input, then tap the prompted action whenever the red X is not covering the screen image.

The multiplier starts at 75%, deals normal 100% damage if you never boost, and can climb to 250%. Tapping during the red X resets the multiplier to 75%. Some early GFs have short animations and cannot realistically reach the cap.

abilitySections.title: Menu Abilities
paragraphs: Menu abilities are used from the in-game Ability menu, while a few work passively without equipping the ability or its GF. Refinement abilities convert ingredients into magic, items, ammunition, medicine, tools, or card rewards.
Ingredient means the input; Yield means the resulting item, spell, or quantity. Refinements marked with * require the associated GF to be Level 100.

Except for Card Mod, refinement lists are ordered by the resulting menu category, then by yield ratio from worst to best, then by ingredient order. These abilities are not item-taught, so do not erase them with Amnesia Greens.
Reduces shop prices by 25%. If you also have Sell-High, most items can be bought and sold for the same price.
Increases the value of sold items by 50%. If you also have Haggle, most items can be bought and sold for the same price.
Allows you to buy shops' rare items (without Familiar they will not appear in inventory).
Allows you to access shops from the menu screen at any time. Only shops you have visited at least once will appear on the list. Here is the shop list as it appears in the menu:
Access the Junk Shop from the menu. (Note that all Junk Shops are identical.)
Refine Lightning- and Wind-elemental Magic from Items.
Refine Ice- and Water-elemental Magic from Items.
Refine Flare and Fire-elemental Magic from Items.
Refine Death, Zombie, Holy, and Restorative Magic (except Esuna, which is refined using Supt Mag-RF) from Items.
Refine Demi, Quake, Double, Triple, Haste, Slow, and Stop Magic from Items.
Refine Bio and status attack Magic (except Slow and Stop, which are both refined using Time Mag-RF) from Items.
Refine Esuna, Dispel, Drain, and positive status Magic (except Double, Triple, and Haste, which are refined using Time Mag-RF) from Items.
Refine Meteor and Ultima Magic from Items. Since both spells (and Ultima especially) are awesome in Junctions, you'll probably be making good use of this ability once you have Bahamut.

Refine Character Recovery Items (except Forbidden Medicine, which is refined using Forbid Med-RF, and Status Recovery Items, which are refined using ST Med-RF) from other Items. In most cases it's better just to buy the items, but making Mega-Potions in particular can be useful for profiteering.
Refine Status Recovery Items from other Items. In most cases it's better just to buy the Items rather than refining other, rarer Items, but you may find occasional uses for this ability.
Refine Ammo from other Items.

Refine Shelters, Tools (except Hero-trial, which is made using Forbid Med-RF), Dark Matter (requires Siren to be Level 100), Wizard Stones, Shaman Stones, and LuvLuv G's from other Items. In other words, Tool-RF is used to make items not covered by other refinement categories in addition to the obvious Tool items; in most case Tool-RF is just an intermediate step in a refinement chain.
Refine Forbidden Medicine, Hero-trials, and Stat Boosting Items from other Items.

Refine Pet Houses and GF Recovery Medicine from other Items. I don't find this ability overly useful (it only gives healing items for GFs and none of the items it can produce are able to converted into anything except other GF Recovery Items), but it doesn't take that much AP to learn, so you might as well learn it early. You'll definitely want it if you use GF Summons a lot though.

Refine GF Ability Items from other Items. This is likely the most useful item-to-item refinement skill (and second only to the almighty Card Mod out of all refinement abilities), unless you're big into stat boosting (Forbid Med-RF). Not only are the Items produced by GFAbl Med-RF useful in and of themselves, but many of them also refine into other amazing things. The only drawback is that how late in the game you get it...
Refines mid-level Magic from low-level Magic. This ability tends to be rather inefficient, but it does provide an alternate means to item hunting for upgrading your Magic inventory.
Refines high-level Magic from mid-level Magic. This ability tends to be rather inefficient, but it does provide an alternate means to item hunting for upgrading your Magic inventory.

Refines Items into improved versions. Med LV Up is mostly a novelty, but its most famous (and important) use is to make the Remedy+ Items needed to recruit Doomtrain, so be sure to learn this ability.

Turns Cards into Items. This is by far the best refinement ability in the game, and guess what? It's available right from the get-go, by teaching Quezacotl Card first to unlock Card Mod. There are a total of 110 possible refinements, one per card type. For considerations of space, the Card Mod table is integrated into the card list tables, however, a listing of all cards with the links to their respective tables is given below.
